Select the reference type you wish to add a new version for. 3. Click the New button 4. You will be taken to the Reference Type Version Maintenance screen. 5. Enter a label for the version unique to the reference type. 6. Add items as required, delete items if needed. 7. Move items to different locations in the tree as required. 8. Each selected items details can be entered a. A list number is optional but would typically match the numbering used in the organisations source document (is any used at all). Maximum 30 characters. Be aware, if you already have a version with the Summary being prefixed with list numbers, separating the list numbers out into this field will have no benefit, unless the scripting to produce the report is also changed to utilise the List Number field. b. The Summary is mandatory. It can be up to 500 characters and it is the text that is shown in the tree for this screen and also for the Report Data screen. c. Optionally enter the Full Text if the summary is a summarised version of the full text. Maximum 4000 characters. Be aware, if you already have a version with the Summary captured but not the Full Text, unless the scripting to produce the report is also changed to utilise Full Text, adding Full Text will not have any benefit. d. Optionally enter a Report Level and/or Agenda Section. i. If an Agenda Section is provided 1. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to true, then selection of this item for a report will see the agenda section defaulted to this items agenda section. If the agenda section is not provided for the item, then it will look to see if a Report Level is defined. See point ii. a. If there is no agenda section provided and a report level is not provided then an exception will result. 2. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to false, then any agenda section will be ignored 3. If the Agenda section selection defaults reference type item to true, then if a matching agenda section is selected for the report, then this item will automatically be selected. e. If a Report Level is provided, then it must be pointing to another item in the reference version that has an Agenda Section provided. i. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to true, then selection of this item for a report will see the agenda section defaulted to the Report Levels items agenda section. 1. If no agenda section is provided, an exception will result. ii. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to false, then the Report Level will be ignored. 9. Click Save 10. You w

title: Adding Attachments
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Adding Attachments 1. Before adding any attachment to a report, each attachment must be carefully prepared. It must be in an acceptable format , of appropriate size and stored in an appropriate location. 2. To add attachments to a report, first open the report then click on the Attachments button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Attachments window opens. 3. Click on the New button. This clears details from the right half of the window ready for you to add a new attachment. It is especially important to remember to use the New button when adding second and subsequent attachments. Otherwise, you will be replacing an attachment rather than adding another one! 4. Enter the File Location of the attachment. You may type or paste the file path or the EDRMS number, or click on the folder button to browse to the attachment, or click on the EDRMS button to locate the attachment in your organisation's EDRMS. Note that if Infocouncil is configured to automatically upload documents to the EDRMS on save of the report; the next time this item is viewed it will show the EDRMS Record ID (not the document path and name). 5. Type a Title . This becomes the attachment title that appears in the list of attachments in your report. It may be identical to the file name of the attachment, but it can be something different. 6. Type the number of Pages in your attachment. Applicable to only some organisations. 7. Check the Exclude Options as appropriate. For details of options that may appear, see the Exclude Options section in the Attachments topic. 8. If adding a PDF attachment, select how you want your attachment to appear. Coloured files are many times larger than black and white or greyscale files, so use sparingly. 9. If attaching a PDF document that has blank margins on each page then choose Crop Image . This crops 1cm from each margin of each page of the PDF as it is being converted, and results in a larger image in the agenda. You should be able to choose this option because most documents have at least 1cm margins that can safely be cropped. 10. If you have written an open report but you wish a particular attachment to be confidential then click on the padlock button to apply confidentiality . Remember that if you wrote a confidential report then all attachments will automatically be made confidential . 11. Click View to open the attachment to check that it is the one you intended to attach. 12. Click Save to save the details of your attachment. Its title will appear in the list on the left. 13. Click New to clear the details of the attachment you have just added so you can begin adding a second (or subsequent) attachment. 14. Click Close to close the Attachments window. Depending on how the system is configured for your organisation you will probably see a list of attachments somewhere in your report. Not every organisation's reports are configured to show references to attachments . 15. Click on the Save bu

title: Adding Members to a Committee
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Members > Adding Members to a Committee 1. To add members to a committee, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing members. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the first member to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Click on the Membership tab 5. Click on the Add button to add a new row to the meeting grid. 6. Select the Meeting to which you wish to add them. 7. Select their Position within the committee. 8. Check Ex-Officio , if appropriate 9. Type their Title . 10. Click on the Save button to save your changes. 11. Repeat steps 3-9 for each of the members you wish to add to the committee. 12. Click on the Cancel button to close the Members window. See Also Maintaining Members - How To Maintaining Meeting Dates - How To Synchronising Members and Memberships - How To

title: Adding Notes to an Action Sheet
text: Navigation: How-to > Monitoring Action Sheets > Adding Notes to an Action Sheet 1. To add a Note to an Action Sheet, go to the Infocouncil Ribbon and click on the Actions button. The Actions window opens. 2. Enter search criteria as desired to locate the action. 3. Select the action to which you wish to add a note then click on the Add/Edit Notes button. The Minute Item form opens to the Action Sheets tab. 4. Click on the Add button. The current date and time appear and the cursor is positioned for you to type. 5. Type the note you wish to add. 6. Click on the OK button to save your note. The details appear in the list on the left of the window. 7. Click Close to exit from the Action Sheet Notes form. 8. If you have completed the action that was allocated to you then you should also Complete the Action . 9. Click Close to exit from the Outstanding Actions window. See Also Actions Revising the Target Date for an Action - How to Allocating an Action to Another Officer - How to Completing/Acknowledging an Action - How to

title: Administering Infocouncil
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil Administering Infocouncil is not a process, but is rather a collection of individual tasks that can each be performed separately. Some are likely to be performed on a regular basis, but others will very rarely be performed. · Altering System Parameters · Exporting Documents From Infocouncil · Maintaining Agenda Items · Maintaining Agenda Sections · Maintaining Authors · Maintaining Confidentiality Clauses · Maintaining Custom Help · Maintaining Deadline Types · Maintaining Divisions · Maintaining Meeting Dates · Creating a Joint Meeting · Maintaining Meeting Types · Maintaining Members · Synchronising Member and Membership · Maintaining Meetings · Viewing a Meeting · Editing a Meeting · Cloning a Meeting · Creating a Joint Committee · Maintaining Procedural Motion Types · Maintaining Reference Types · Maintaining Subscribers · Resetting Item Numbers · Setting up Security · Maintaining Security Functions · Maintaining Security Groups

title: Allocating an Action to Another Officer
text: Navigation: How-to > Monitoring Action Sheets > Allocating an Action to Another Officer 1. To allocate an action to another officer, click on the Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Actions window opens. 2. Enter search criteria as desired to locate the action. 3. Select the action you wish to reallocate then click on the Allocate Action to Another Officer button. The Select the new officer form opens. Button only appears if you have rights to reallocate an action . 4. Select the new officer . 5. Click on the Continue button. The change of officers will be recorded the Actions window. And both officers receive emails - one to say the action has been allocated to them and the other to say it has been allocated away from them. 6. Click on the Close button to close the Actions window. See Also Actions Adding Notes to an Action Sheet - How to Revising the Target Date for an Action - How to Completing/Acknowledging an Action - How to

title: Altering System Parameters
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Altering System Parameters The system parameters were set up to customise the system for your organisation when it was implemented. Please do not alter any System Parameters without guidance from the Infocouncil team. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose System Parameters . The System Parameters window opens. 2. Navigate to the required parameter. Only click on the grey and black arrows on the left of the window. 3. Click on the name of a parameter to select it. Do NOT click to the right of the name of a parameter unless the Infocouncil team guides you. 4. An explanation of the selected parameter will be displayed across the bottom of the window. You can re-size the explanation area by dragging the grey bar above it up or down. 5. Click Cancel to exit the window without making any changes. 6. Click OK to save any changes that the Infocouncil team guided you to make.

title: Attaching Tabled Items to the Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes > Attaching Tabled Items to the Minutes 1. To attach tabled items to the Minutes, first get them into an electronic form and save the files. (scan documents or photograph objects) 2. Using the Document Menu button on the Infocouncil ribbon, open the Minutes for the meeting. 3. Within the relevant item of business, create an additional row (using either the row above or row below minute tools ) 4. Record who tabled the item and what it relates to. 5. With the cursor still within the item of business, click on the Other ribbon menu button and select Attachments . The Attachments window will open. 6. Fill in the relevant fields, including adding confidentiality to the tabled item if that is appropriate. 7. Save and Close the attachments form to return to your Minutes document. 8. The attachment will now be linked to the item in your minutes. Depending on how the system is configured in your organisation, you may see a reference to the attachments in the minutes. For example: 9. Save and Close the Minutes. 10. Once your minutes are finished, you must upload the Minutes for the meeting. The system needs you to do this so it can complete its process of linking the attachments to the minutes. (You do not need to create and distribute the action sheets, you just need to upload the items). 11. The final part of the process is to create a Minutes Attachment document, so, click on the Create Paper button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Create Paper window opens. 12. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . 13. Choose a Paper Type of Attachments - Minutes . 14. Check the confidential option if required. 15. Click on the Create Paper button to create the Attachments to Minutes document. 16. Wait while the document is created then check, save, and close it. Distribute it with the minutes. See Also Document Menu Attachment Formats - Concept Attachment Sizing - Concept

title: Authoring and Authorising Reports
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports Authoring and Authorising Reports is a process that is made up of many individual tasks: · Writing a Report · Making a Report Confidential · Removing Confidentiality from a Report · Adding Attachments to a Report · Editing an Attachment · Removing an Attachment · Sending a Report for Authorisation · Authorising a Report · Rejecting a Report · Cloning a Report · Moving a Report

title: Authorising a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Authorising a Report 1. When you receive an email requesting that you authorise a report, click on the Open Report link in the email. The report opens. 2. Review the report and its attachments to determine whether to authorise or reject/return the report. At this stage you have edit rights to the report so you could fix a small typing error. But if you make any significant changes then you should add your name as an author (using the Report Data button) or at least inform the original author of the changes you have made to their report. 3. Click on the black arrow under the Authorise ribbon button and choose Authorise (or Return). The Create Mail form opens, already addressed to the author and pre-populated with most of the information about your report. 4. Enter an Optional Note , if you wish. 5. Click on the OK button to send the email. 6. Click on the Close button to close the Report Authorisers form. Your report will be automatically saved and closed, and you will lose your rights to edit it. 7. If there is another authoriser after you, then you will be asked if you wish to notify the next authoriser. Click Yes . The Create Mail form opens, already addressed to the next authoriser and pre-populated with most of the information about the report. 8. Enter an Optional Note , if you wish. 9. Click on the OK button to send the email. The report will be automatically closed, and you will lose your rights to edit it. However, if the next authoriser rejects it then you may be notified by email and your edit rights will be reinstated. Alternatively, the author may be notified and their edit rights reinstated. Who is informed depends on how the system has been configured for your organisation . 10. Whether the next authoriser authorises or rejects the report, you should receive an email informing you of their decision. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Returning or Rejecting a Report - How To

title: Cloning A Meeting
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meetings > Cloning A Meeting 1. To clone a meeting, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meetings . The Meeting window opens. 2. Locate the meeting you are interested in by searching all meetings or doing a partial name search. 3. Click on the Clone button to clone the existing committee. 4. When asked for a new meeting name, type the name of the new committee then click OK . A new committee is created. It has 4 tabs of data that you now need to check/edit. 5. On the General and Defaults and Authorisations tabs , edit details as required. 6. On the File Save Paths tab , enter the save paths for Reports, Agendas, Minutes, Attachments and Action sheets for the new committee. These directories should already exist for each path. Only used by server-based organisations (non-EDRMS) . 7. On the Meeting Text tab , check that each line of text is correct or edit, if necessary, but it should be correct because you used the Clone function. 8. On the Insert Pages tab , check that it populated when you cloned the committee. You should not need to edit this tab because you cloned a committee, but if edits are required, please contact Infocouncil for advice. 9. Click on the Close button to exit the window. You have now set up the "shell" of your new meeting. Next, you need to: · tell the system what report types and agenda items are valid for the new meeting. · add members to the new meeting. See Also Viewing a Meeting - How To Editing a Meeting - How To Creating a Joint Committee - How To

title: Cloning a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Cloning a Report 1. To clone a report, first open the report. 2. Check the Title bar . The report will have a title comprising a unique collection of letters and numbers representing the meeting it was put to (e.g. CO20161001_... went to the council meeting held on 1 Oct 2016) 3. Click on the black arrow beside the Move/Copy button on the Infocouncil ribbon and select Clone . The Clone a Report window opens. 4. Select the Name and Date of the meeting you wish to put your cloned report to. 5. Also select the Report Type , Agenda Section and whether or not to Include Attachments . The choices available will depend on how the system has been configured for your organisation . 6. Click OK . The cloned report will open on top of the original report. 7. Check the Title bar . The cloned report will have a title of Document1 (or similar). It is a normal Infocouncil report that you can edit in the normal way. The only difference is that it is an exact replica of the original report. 8. Edit your cloned report as much as is required. 9. Click on the Save button to save your cloned report. 10. When ready, send your cloned report for authorisation then close the Report Authoriser window (which will save and close the cloned report). If you still have a report open on your desktop it will be the original report (check its title) so you can close it. You should not need to save it because you have not made any changes to it. See Also Shifting Reports Between Meetings - Concept Authoring and Authorising Reports - links to tasks for editing a report and its attachments Sending a Report for Authorisation - How To Save

title: Completing/Acknowledging an Action
text: Navigation: How-to > Monitoring Action Sheets > Completing/Acknowledging an Action 1. To complete a For Action action or acknowledge an Information Only action, click on the Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Actions window opens. 2. Enter search criteria as desired to locate the action. 3. Select the action you wish to complete/acknowledge. It may be appropriate to add a note to say what you did or why you are closing/acknowledging the action. 4. Select the action you wish to complete/acknowledge then click on the Complete/Acknowledge Action button. 5. You will be presented with either: a. I f actions authorisations are being used and there are authorisers for the action, the Approvals screen will be displayed. Notify the first authoriser. b. If not, you will be asked if you wish to complete/acknowledge the Action. A note may be required. If you are completing the action on behalf of someone else can can choose to notify that person. 6. The status of the item will be updated. Your completed/acknowledged action may disappear depending on your search criteria, but you can always find it by choosing to include Completed/Acknowledged items in the search criteria. See Also Actions Adding Notes to an Action Sheet - How to Revising the Target Date for an Action - How to Allocating an Action to Another Officer - How to

title: Conducting eVoting
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes > Conducting eVoting This topic describse how to conduct an eVote. eVoting must first have been configured for your site. Please see Configuring eVoting if eVoting is yet to be configured for your site or you wish to change the way it is configured. Electronic voting functionality (or eVoting) has been developed to allow for votes to be conducted within Infocouncil using either purpose built or generic devices. In terms of purpose built devices, Infocouncil has integrated with the Dicentis system developed by Bosch. Infocouncil has also developed a solution that is technology agnostic and can run on any device capable of reasonably rendering a web page in a browser. These solutions will be known as Dicentis eVoting and Infocouncil eVoting respectively. The following illustrates how to conduct eVoting. 1. Open a minutes document. 2. Start an eVoting session for the meeting by clicking the eVoting tool on the Infocouncil ribbon. 3. Click OK to the confirmation dialog that appears. 4. Position the cursor at an item for which you wish to hold a division. 5. Click the Division tool on the Infocouncil ribbon, or press Ctrl 3. 6. Either click the Start button to start the eVote, or Infocouncil may have been configured to automatically start the eVote. 7. Members will now have the opportunity to cast their votes either via a browser in their device or with the Dicentis hardware. 8. Either wait for all votes to be cast, wait for the timer to time out or manually stop the eVote when ready. 9. Complete any votes manually that were not able to be cast electronically (and amend any electronic votes if required). 10. Click OK on the Division tool to have Infocouncil automatically insert the results into the minutes. 11. Repeat steps 4 to 10 for as many divisions as are desired. 12. End the eVoting session by clicking the eVoting tool on the Infocouncil ribbon for a second time. Alternatively just close the minutes document and the eVoting session will automatically be closed. See Also eVoting Tool - Screen Help. Division Tool - Screen Help. eVoting Members Screens - Screen Help Configuring eVoting for how to configure electronic voting. eVoting - Concept.

title: Configuring SharePoint Integration
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Configuring SharePoint Integration Introduction Infocouncil has been enhanced to allow it to recognise and utilise SharePoint locations and files for much of its core functionality. This document will detail changes to the Infocouncil product required for SharePoint integration, as well as structural requirements in your SharePoint tenancy required to support Infocouncil integration. This documents intended audience is primarily those who administer IT systems and/or SharePoint administrators. The integration will work with a single tenancy and any of its sub sites. It will not work with multiple tenancies. Site Setup The integration between Infocouncil and SharePoint should work with any site template however if you are configuring a specific site for use by Infocouncil then its preferable to use the Document Center template because it will give you the basic structure required. To create a site based on this template it is necessary to use the SharePoint admin center, typically accessible at https://<your company>-admin.sharepoint.com. Once there you can select Sites -> Active Sites followed by Create, as shown below. This will then show the new site panel on the left of the browser window. The two featured sites in our tenancy are Team Site and Communication Site. We do not want to select either of these, so we choose Other Options from the bottom of the panel, as shown below. The following fields will then be displayed. Choose Document Center from the template drop down and enter other details as required. Once created, the new SharePoint site should be listed under Active sites. To access it, click on the site name and then select the URL on the General tab as shown below. This will open the site in a new browser tab at the default view, which for the document center template in our environment is a list of libraries and folders on the left and documents that are either new or recently modified by you. The basic structure of this site is as follows: Site -> Libraries -> Folders and Files. You can also create Sub-Sites if required. Subsequent content on the site will depend on which aspects of Infocouncil functionality you wish to integrate with SharePoint. Broadly speaking, Infocouncil can integrate with SharePoint in the following ways: - 1. Allow Infocouncil reports and papers to be stored, saved and opened from a SharePoint site. 2. Allow report and paper template files and inserts to be stored, saved and opened from a SharePoint site. 3. Allow publishing of HTML or PDF documents to a SharePoint site. 4. Allow Infocouncil reports and papers to be archived to a SharePoint site. 5. Allow report attachments to be sourced from or located in a SharePoint site. Adding libraries To add libraries to SharePoint go into the site itself and select Libraries from the left-hand menu. A screen like the following will be shown. Select the New button and then select Document Library. A side panel will be sh

title: Configuring Dicentis eVoting
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Configuring eVoting > Configuring Dicentis eVoting This topic relates to Dicentis eVoting not Infocouncil eVoting . Read the Configuring eVoting topic if you are unsure which is relevant to you. Several parameters control whether eVoting is available and how it behaves. This set of parameters can be defined at the system level via the System Parameters form, and they can be overridden at the meeting level via clicking the System Parameters button on the Meeting Maintenance form. Where undefined at the meeting level then the system level applies. Where defined at the meeting level then the system level is overridden. The following illustrates how to go about setting up eVoting with Dicentis. 1. Go to the System Parameters form and expand the eVoting group to display the relevant parameters. The process is the same for overriding at the meeting level. 2. Change the eVoting Type parameter to "2" for Dicentis eVoting. 3. Set the Dicentis Connection String parameter appropriately. This string must include the credentials of a user defined in Dicentis with sufficient privileges to start and stop votes. See the information on this parameter in the System Parameters form. It will also require someone with understanding of the Dicentis hardware to know what to type in. The optional InterimResultsMode* controls what users see on their Dicentis devices during a vote. It can be one of: 0 = Totals and individuals (default), 1 = Only totals, 2 = No interim results. 4. Decide how an eVote should be started by setting the Auto Init eVote parameter to "False" to give the minute taker control over when and if an eVote starts, or to "True" to make Infocouncil automatically start an eVote when the minute taker opens the Insert Division form. 5. Decide what the Insert Division form will display during an eVote as the votes are being received. Set the In Vote Display parameter to one of the following. Note that the way the InterimResultsMode* is set controls what information Infocouncil receives from Dicentis during a vote. In brackets is how the InterimResultsMode* needs to be set for Infocouncil to be able to correctly manage the display in the Division tool. a. 1 - Who has voted, how they voted and a tally of for, against and abstain (InterimResultsMode needs to be 0 to work) b. 2 - Who has voted and a tally of votes received, but not how members have voted (InterimResultsMode needs to be 0 to work, if 1 only the tally will work) c. 3 - A tally of votes received only (InterimResultsMode needs to be 0 or 1 to work) d. 4 - A tally of for, against and abstain (InterimResultsMode needs to be 0 or 1 to work) e. 5 - Nothing (InterimResultsMode can be anything) 6. An eVote can always be manually stopped by the minute taker but it can be configured to automatically stop by setting the Auto Stop Type parameter to one of the following. Note that the way the InterimResultsMode* is set controls what information Infocouncil receiv

title: Configuring eVoting
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Configuring eVoting Electronic voting functionality (or eVoting) has been developed to allow for votes to be conducted within Infocouncil using either purpose built or generic devices. In terms of purpose built devices, Infocouncil has integrated with the Dicentis system developed by Bosch. Infocouncil has also developed a solution that is technology agnostic and can run on any device capable of reasonably rendering a web page in a browser. These solutions will be known as Dicentis eVoting and Infocouncil eVoting respectively. Please see the relevant topic depending on which eVoting solution is being adopted. Configuring Infocouncil eVoting Configuring Dicentis eVoting See Also eVoting Tool - Screen Help. Meeting System Parameters - Screen Help. System Parameters - Screen Help. eVoting Members Screens - Screen Help Members Maintenance - Screen Help. Conducting eVoting - How To. eVoting - Concept.

title: Configuring Infocouncil eVoting
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Configuring eVoting > Configuring Infocouncil eVoting This topic relates to Infocouncil eVoting not Dicentis eVoting . Read the Configuring eVoting topic if you are unsure which is relevant to you. Several parameters control whether eVoting is available and how it behaves. This set of parameters can be defined at the system level via the System Parameters form, and they can be overridden at the meeting level via clicking the System Parameters button on the Meeting Maintenance form. Where undefined at the meeting level then the system level applies. Where defined at the meeting level then the system level is overridden. The following illustrates how to go about setting up eVoting with Infocouncil. 1. Go to the System Parameters form and expand the eVoting group to display the relevant parameters. The process is the same for overriding at the meeting level. 2. Change the eVoting Type parameter to "1" for Infocouncil eVoting. 3. Decide how an eVote should be started by setting the Auto Init eVote parameter to "False" to give the minute taker control over when and if an eVote starts, or to "True" to make Infocouncil automatically start an eVote when the minute taker opens the Insert Division form. 4. Decide what the Insert Division form will display during an eVote as the votes are being received. Set the In Vote Display parameter to one of a. "1" - Who has voted, how they voted and a tally of for, against and abstain, b. "2" - Who has voted and a tally of votes received, but not how members have voted, c. "3" - A tally of votes received only, d. "4" - A tally of for, against and abstain, or e. "5" - Nothing 5. An eVote can always be manually stopped by the minute taker but it can be configured to automatically stop by setting the Auto Stop Type parameter to one of a. "1" when all votes have been received, b. "2" when a set number of seconds elapse, as defined in the eVote Duration parameter, c. "3" the earlier of the first or second option, or d. "4" to prevent automatically stopping at all. 6. Decide whether to allow members to cast a vote only once or cast and recast their vote as often as time allows by setting the Allow Recasting parameter to "False" or "True" respectively. 7. Decide whether to allow a member to enter a reason for their vote or not by setting the Allow Vote Reason parameter to "True" or "False" respectively. The browser screen the member sees will show a reason text box accordingly. 8. Decide whether to display the first 1000 characters of the resolution or not to the member by setting the Show Resolution parameter to "True" or "False" respectively. The resolution will not always render "nicely" on the screen that the member sees. Running a division must be very rapid. Converting the resolution in Word into HTML takes a bit of time so Infocouncil simply flattens the Word into text and renders the text. This can result in some odd characters appearing. 9. The eVoting Port parameter

title: Configuring Load Clauses
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Configuring Load Clauses Load Clauses consists of 4 tools in its own group on the Infocouncil ribbon. Their purpose is to provide an easy means of inserting a clause into a council document for a set of standard clauses that are frequently used. To display these tools the Infocouncil.ini file must have a LoadClauses key equals yes under the Infocouncil sections. E.g. [InfoCouncil] LOADCLAUSES = YES There are other ini file settings, also located under the [Infocouncil] section, that affect Load Clauses Setting Value : Description LoadClausesFile= This is the path to the Word document that is the source of any clauses (or standard conditions) that may be inserted into Infocouncil documents. LoadClausesSort= 0 : Selected clauses are inserted in the order that they were selected. 1: Selected clauses are inserted in their item number order. LoadClausesRestartNumbering= 0: Does not restart list numbering for subsequent inserted clauses. 1: Allows list numbering to be restarted for subsequent inserted clauses. LoadClausesBoldNumbering= 0: The list numbering for inserted clauses will not be boldened. 1: The list numbering for inserted clauses will be boldened.

title: Creating Agendas
text: Navigation: How-to > Creating Agendas 1. To create an agenda, click on the Create Paper button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Create Paper window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . 3. Choose a Paper Type of Agenda . 4. Check/Uncheck the appropriate boxes to create the type of agenda you want. Depending on how the system is configured for your organisation, the titles of check boxes may be slightly different, and every option may not be configured in your organisation. · Check the box beside Confidential if you wish to create a confidential/closed agenda. · Check the box beside Urgent Items if you wish to create an Urgent/Late Items/Addendum agenda. · Check the box beside Draft if you wish to create a draft agenda. See note below concerning items in a Draft Agendas. · Uncheck the box beside Include Attachments if you wish to create an agenda without any attachments. 5. Using the drop down list in the bottom right of the window, filter to Show Unauthorised Only . The reports displayed need to be fully authorised before they can be included in the agenda (except for drafts), so: 1. Either, click on the Notify button. You will be asked if you wish to notify all authorisers who have not yet authorised the report. Click OK . Next you will be given the option to enter a note to send with the email, so type a note then click OK . An reminder email will be sent to each authoriser. A message stating the number of officers notified will appear. Click OK to clear the message. 2. Or, s elect an unauthorised report and click on the Authorisers button. The Report Authorisers form opens. You could phone or email the authoriser who is "dragging the chain" and "hurry them along", or if they give you permission (and the processes within your organisation allow it) you can click on the Override Authorisation button. Click Close to close the Report Authorisers window and return to the Create Paper window. Repeat this task for each report that is not fully authorised. 6. Set the filter back to Show Authorised & Unauthorised . 7. Check the box in the Include column for those reports you wish to include in the agenda. Any items can be included in a Draft Agenda (whether fully authorised or not) but only fully authorised items can be included in other agendas types. 8. Order the items by selecting a report and clicking on the up or down arrows to the right of the grid. You can only order items within an agenda section. Changing a report to a different section must be done on the report's Report Data form. 9. When all the reports are included and ordered, click on the Create Paper button. Depending on the number of reports included the process can take several minutes. Much twitching and flashing will occur as each report and its attachments are opened, read, incorporated into the agenda and closed. 10. If an issue is detected, a message will appear. Click the Print Screen key to capture the message (useful so you can go back and re-read it if necessary, or l

title: Creating a Joint Committee
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meetings > Creating a Joint Committee Creating a Joint Committee is a task that only needs to be done once. Once a Joint Committee is created then you can create Joint Meetings as often as required, and they can be made up of any combination of any other committees that your organisation has. For example, at some point you may want a joint meeting of the "Arts" and "Finance" committees and a few weeks later you may want a joint meeting of the "Parks" and "Heritage" committees. This topic explains the 3 processes involved in creating a 'generic' Joint Committee: · Creating a 'generic' Joint Committee · Allocating Report Types to Agenda Sections · Adding Members to the Joint Committee After creating the 'generic' joint committee then you go to the Meeting Dates form to set up a specific instance of the meeting. At that point you not only set date, time and location but you also choose the committees that are to be involved. Creating a 'generic' Joint Committee 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meetings . The Meeting window opens. 2. Use the search options to select a meeting that is closest in layout to your new joint committee. This is likely to be a committee rather than your council which probably has a more complicated structure of agenda sections. 3. Click on the Clone button to clone the existing committee. 4. When asked for a new meeting name, type Joint Committee then click OK . A new committee called Joint Committee is created. 5. On the General tab , ensure that Joint is checked. Edit other details if required, but you should not need to edit much since you used the Clone functionality.. 6. On the File Save Paths tab , enter the save paths for Reports, Agendas, Minutes, Attachments and Action sheets for the new committee. These directories should already exist for each path. Only used by server-based organisations (non-EDRMS) . 7. On the Meeting Text tab , carefully replace the words Joint Committee with the text [ComitteeNames] (including square brackets). This tells the system that in places like the agenda cover and headers you want the names of the committees to appear, not the generic words Joint Committee. See Identifying a Meeting below. 8. On the Insert Pages tab , check that it populated when you cloned the committee. You should not need to edit this tab because you cloned a committee, but if edits are required, please contact Infocouncil for advice. You have now set up the "shell" of the "generic" Joint Committee. Next, you need to: · tell the system what report types and agenda items are valid for the Joint Committee. · add members to the Joint Committee. Identifying a Meeting At step 7 above, when you replace the generic words Joint Committee with the text [CommitteeNames] then all the following screens which show the Meeting Name and Date will show the constituent committee names in a tool-tip when the user hovers their mouse over the Meeting Name.

title: Creating a Joint Meeting
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meeting Dates > Creating a Joint Meeting You can create Joint Meetings as often as required, provided a Joint Committee already exists. Joint Meetings can be made up of any combination of any other committees that your organisation has. For example, at some point you may want a joint meeting of the "Arts" and "Finance" committees and a few weeks later you may want a joint meeting of the "Parks" and "Heritage" committees. 1. To create a joint meeting, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates . The Meeting Dates window opens. 2. The window opens ready for a new meeting to be entered but if you have been viewing other meetings click on the New button. The right of the window will be cleared so you can enter details of the new meeting. 3. On the General tab, choose Joint Committee from the Committee drop down list. This must have been previously set up and may have been called something other than Joint Committee. 4. Set the Meeting Date 5. Edit remaining details on the General tab, if required. 6. On the Dates and Times tab, enter details for the meeting. If your organisation is using Deadline Dates. 7. On the Descriptions tab, enter details for the meeting. If your organisation is using Descriptions. 8. On the Joint Meetings tab, select the names of the two (or more) meetings that will be combining. 9. Use the up and down arrows to order the meeting names, or the cross button to delete a meeting if you make a mistake. The order you list the committee names here are the order in which they will appear in places like the cover and headers of the meeting's agenda. 10. Click on the Save button to save details of the joint committee meeting. Identifying a Meeting When the Joint Committee was created, the name of the committee was probably changed to [CommitteeNames]. If this was done, then in all of the following windows that show the Meeting Name and Date, the system will display the names of the constituent committees in a tool-tip: · Report Data · Document Menu · List Reports for a Meeting · Clone Report · Defer/Refer a Report · Create Paper · Generating Actions · Meeting Speakers · Setup Cover Sheets · Minutes Index Report · Minutes by Resolution Report Users can read the tool-tip by hovering their mouse over the Meeting Name. See Also Creating a Joint Committee - How To Adding Members to a Committee - How To

title: Creating a Minutes Document
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Creating a Minutes Document This task can be performed any time after the agenda (and any addendum/late/supplementary agendas) has been created. You do not need to wait until just before the meeting to create it. Preparation Check that any Leave of Absence or Apologies from members have been entered onto the appropriate tabs on the Members window. Steps 1. To create a Minutes document, click on the Create Paper button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Create Paper window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . 3. Choose a Paper Type of Minutes . 4. Check the box beside Confidential if you wish to create a confidential/closed minutes document. 5. Check the box beside Pro-Forma if you wish to create a pro-forma document. A page break is inserted between each item so Pro-forma versions are useful if you will be taking Minutes by hand during a meeting. May not be configured in your organisation . 6. Check the box beside Include Attachments if that is required. May not be configured in your organisation . 7. Check the box in the Include column for those reports you wish to include in the minutes. 8. Click on the Create Paper button. Depending on the number of reports included the process can take several minutes. Much twitching and flashing will occur as each report and its attachments are opened, read, incorporated into the minutes document and closed. A completion message will appear when done. 9. Scroll through your minutes document to ensure it is as you expected. It should be ready for you to take to the meeting. 10. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon to save your minutes document for the first time. Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, you may be offered one, two or three choices for saving your agenda. 11. Add any new items that have been received since the Agenda was created. For example, some organisations accept Public Input items or Notices of Motion until a few hours before the meeting. 12. Print a document to take it to the meeting. It is always wise to have hard-copy in the meeting in case technology fails! 13. If the meeting is to be held away from your organisation's network, prepare the minutes for going offline/remote . Remote minutes may not be configured in your organisation . See Also Document Menu Taking Minutes - How To

title: Creating an Attachments Document
text: Navigation: How-to > Creating Agendas > Creating an Attachments Document 1. To create an attachments document, click on the Create Paper button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Create Paper window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . 3. Choose a Paper Type of Attachments . 4. Check/Uncheck the appropriate boxes to create the type of attachments document you want. Depending on how the system is configured for your organisation, the titles on check boxes may be slightly different, and every option may not be configured in your organisation. · Check the box beside Confidential if you wish to create a confidential/closed attachments document. · Check the box beside Urgent Items if you wish to create an Urgent/Late Items/Addendum attachments document. · Check any or none of the other Exclude Options check boxes, as required. · Check the box beside Draft if you wish to create a draft attachments document. See note below concerning items in a Draft Agendas. 5. Check the box in the Include column for those reports whose attachments you wish to include in the agenda. Any items can be included in a Draft Attachments document (whether fully authorised or not) but only fully authorised items can be included in other document types. 6. Order the items by selecting a report and clicking on the up or down arrows to the right of the grid. You can only order items within an agenda section. Changing a report to a different section must be done on the report's Report Data form. 7. When all the reports are included and ordered, click on the Create Paper button. Depending on the number of attachments in the reports included the process can take several minutes. Much twitching and flashing will occur as each report and its attachments are opened, read, incorporated into the attachments paper and closed. 8. If an error occurs, a message will appear. Click the Print Screen key to capture the message (useful so you can go back and re-read it if necessary, or log a Help desk job). Click OK to clear the message then follow whatever instructions it contained. 9. If no errors occur, a message saying document creation is complete appears. Click OK to clear the message. 10. Scroll through your attachments document to ensure it is as you expected. 11. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon to save your attachment document . Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, you may be offered one, two or three choices for saving your agenda. 12. If any of the reports are confidential/closed, or is any of the open reports have confidential attachments, remember to also create a confidential attachment document. 13. You should get your document checked before publishing or distributing it. Some organisations expect you to send it for formal authorisation . Other organisations are happy for you to have it peer-reviewed. Draft Attachment Documents Draft Mode may be used to generate draft versions of agenda and attachment documents: · A Draft

title: Creating an HTML Document
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Creating an HTML Document 1. To create an HTML document, first open your completed Agenda, Minutes or Attachments document then click on the Publish ribbon menu and choose Create HTML Document . 2. You will be asked if you are ready to create an HTML version of this business paper. Click Yes . 3. Much twitching and flashing will occur as pages of the document are opened, read, incorporated into the HTML document and closed. When completed the HTML document will be open for you to inspect. 4. Inspect the outline on the left of the screen to ensure it is as you expected and does not contain any extraneous entries. If you find any, close the HTML document and fix the problem in the Word version of your document before recreating the HTML document. 5. Scroll through your document to ensure it is as you expected. 6. Close the HTML document. The system has already saved it. See Also Creating a PDF Document - How To Publishing to the Web - How To Unpublishing from the Web - How To Publishing to LG Hub - How To

title: Creating a PDF Document
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Creating a PDF Document 1. To create a PDF document, first open your completed Agenda, Minutes or Attachments document then click on the Publish ribbon menu and choose Create PDF Document . The Create PDF Version of the .. . window opens. 2. Select the first conversion choice, unless you are experiencing problems. 3. To optimise the PDF document for minimum size, making it more suitable for the web, check the Optimise for minimum size box. This results in a smaller, but possibly less legible, result. 4. To embed original versions of attachments in the PDF document, check the Embed the original attachments box. This is generally used for publishing to LG Hub (or other tablet based apps). 5. Much twitching and flashing will occur as pages of the document are opened, read, incorporated into the PDF document and closed. When complete the PDF document will be created for you to inspect. 6. Inspect the outline on the left of the screen to ensure it is as you expected and does not contain any extraneous entries. If you find any, close the PDF document and fix the problem in the Word version of your document before recreating the PDF document. 7. Scroll through your document to ensure it is as you expected. 8. Close the PDF document. The system has already saved it. Tip Infocouncil uses the in-built ability of Word to create the PDF version and the ability of Infocouncil to create the navigation pane (outline). You may opt for an alternate conversion method which may require Adobe Acrobat to be installed. Alternatives are limited by Word version installed and Acrobat availability. [See the Help button on the Create PDF Version of the... window]. See Also Create PDF Version of the... - Screen Help Creating an HTML Document - How To Publishing to the Web - How To Unpublishing from the Web - How To Publishing to LG Hub - How To Paper Conversion to PDF Choices - Concept

title: Creating a Publish Destination
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Creating a Publish Destination Steps 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Publish Destinations to open the Publish Destinations screen. 2. Normally the screen will be in a state ready to add a new destination. If not, click the New button and make sure the Details tab is selected (not the Configuration tab). 3. Enter a unique name and a description for the destination. 4. Choose the Type of destination: "FTP", "File Server", "SharePoint", "LG Hub", or "Web API". This choice will cause fields to appear in the Configuration tab that are relevant to the destination type. 5. Select the meetings whose papers are valid to be published to this destination, or leave all selected. 6. Choose what can be published to the destination. At least one of PDF or HTML format must be selected. At least one of open or confidential (closed) must be selected. 7. If the destination has the Infocouncil Publishing Website sitting on top then you will want to select Publish Reference Files. This provide the website necessary information about the meeting types and meeting dates. 8. Depending on the destination type chosen, some fields may be disabled. For example, HTML papers cannot be published to LG Hub, nor can Reference Files. 9. Now switch to the Configuration tab and fill out the fields relating to communicating and working with the destination type. 10. At this point you will have a publish destination defined which needs to be tested to ensure configured correctly. 11. Finally, if you wish to override any of the options on the details or the configuration tabs, for an individual meeting, you may do this by a. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meetings to open the Meetings screen. b. Select the meeting that you wish to override options for. c. Click the Publish Destinations button to open the Meeting Publish Destinations screen. d. The list of valid destinations will automatically appear for the meeting. e. Select the one you just added. f. Now override any options as desired within the constraints of what is overridable for the destination type. LG Hub Publish Destination Type Notes Before setting up an LG Hub publish destination you should ensure you have an LG Hub account and it is set up correctly: A. Obtain a corporate account for LG Hub. You will be provided with a URL to access your LG Hub corporate account. It will be in a format like http://rccdashboard.randwick.nsw.gov.au. You will also be provided with a user account to access LG Hub at the URL. B. Using your browser log into LG Hub at the URL using the user account you were provided with. C. Within LG Hub create user accounts to match Infocouncil users. Infocouncil users have their email addresses recorded via the Author maintenance screen. User accounts in LG Hub need to be set up for these email addresses. Set the passwords to whatever you wish. D. Within LG Hub create your desired tabs and channels. We recommend you create one chann

title: Customising the Infocouncil Ribbon to Add Your Own Button
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Customising the Infocouncil Ribbon to Add Your Own Button Occasionally a client wishes to modify the Infocouncil Ribbon. We don't encourage it but we acknowledge that the ability to add an extra button or two to do specific things related to working with an Infocouncil document can be valuable. It is definitely possible to add a new button group and a custom button to the Infocouncil Ribbon. It is also possible to control where the button group is positioned on the ribbon. It is possible (but not verified) that certain Infocouncil button groups can be made invisible and we strongly recommend against this because it will only generate support calls from users trying to find certain Infocouncil functions. The following details how to customise the Infocouncil ribbon tab by adding a button to it to call a custom macro. 1. Create a Word document 2. Add your macro to the template ensuring it is a public subroutine containing one parameter as per the following example Public Sub InsertAutoNumField(ByVal control As IRibbonControl) 3. Save it as a dotm format file. Do not save it in the Word startup directory (C:\Users\timf\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP) whilst developing your template. Move it in only once you're happy with it. 4. Download and install the Office Custom UI Editor from the web site at http://openxmldeveloper.org/blog/b/openxmldeveloper/archive/2009/08/07/7293.aspx 5. Run the Office Custom UI Editor and open up your dotm file 6. Select the Office 2007 Custom UI Part from the Insert menu 7. Paste the following XML into the editor and then customise it as described below < customUI xmlns = " http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/2006/01/customui " xmlns: shared = " Infocouncil.ExpressAddin " > < ribbon > < tabs > < tab idQ = " shared:tabInfocouncil " > < group id = " grpKogarahOnInfocouncil " label = " Kogarah " insertBeforeQ = " shared:grpInfocouncilCreate " > < button id = " cmdInsertAutoNumField " label = " Insert Auto Number " size = " large " imageMso = " HappyFace " onAction = " InsertAutoNumField " /> </ group > </ tab > </ tabs > </ ribbon > </ customUI > Notes & Customisations * It is common to see in ribbon modification XML, the following line with 2009/07 in it instead of 2006/01. This did not work in our testing so use 2006/01. xmlns = " http://schemas.microsoft.com/office/2006/01/customui " * The following line of XML identifies the NameSpace of the Infocouncil ribbon tab. It is essential if your XML is to be able to reference the Infocouncil tab and some of the button groups in it. Note that the name space is assigned to the word "shared". Any references to Infocouncil ribbon components must be qualified with the word "shared". xmlns: shared = " Infocouncil.ExpressAddin " * The following line addresses the infocouncil ribbon tab . idQ is the "qualified" identifier of the tab, "shared" is the qualifier to the Infocouncil name space and tabInfocouncil is the identifier to the

title: Editing A Meeting
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meetings > Editing A Meeting 1. To edit a meeting, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meetings . The Meeting window opens. 2. Locate the meeting you are interested in by searching all meetings or doing a partial name search. 3. Edit the agenda item as required. 4. Click Save and then Close button to exit the window. See Also Viewing a Meeting Cloning a Meeting Creating a Joint Committee

title: Editing an Attachment
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Editing an Attachment 1. To edit an attachment to a report, first open the report then click on the Attachments button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Attachments window opens. 2. Select the name of the attachment you wish to edit. Its details will be displayed on the right of the window. 3. Edit the details, as appropriate. 4. Click Save to save the edited details of your attachment. 5. Click Close to close the Attachments window. 6. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon to save the change to your report. Tip If you wish to change an attachment (perhaps because details in the attachment have been updated), then at step 3 (above), you must enter the File Location of the updated attachment. You may type or paste the new file path or the new EDRMS number, or click on the folder button to browse to the updated attachment, or click on the EDRMS button to locate the updated attachment in your organisation's EDRMS. See Also Adding an Attachment - How to Removing an Attachment - How to Making an Attachment Confidential - How to

title: Enable Add-In Auto Upgrading
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Enable Add-In Auto Upgrading From Infocouncil version 8.7.0, the Add-In can automatically upgrade itself if an administrator updates the database version. To enable this ability: 1. Make a network location that all Infocouncil users can read. 2. Copy any Infocouncil 32 and/or 64 bit installation files (msi files) into the location. 3. Update the system parameter "Auto Upgrade - Local Upgrade Server" to the location. 4. Update the system parameter "Auto Upgrade - Enabled" to True. Now when the database is upgraded, any Infocouncil Add-In clients connecting to that database will detect a database version mismatch and ask the user if they wish to auto upgrade. Remember: any version 8.7.0 or greater can be upgraded or downgraded to any version 8.7.0 or greater. Versions below this do not have auto upgrade functionality. E.g. 8.7.5 can upgrade to 8.8.2 or downgrade to 8.7.2; 8.6.4 cannot upgrade or downgrade and 8.7.5 cannot downgrade to 8.6.4.

title: Exporting Documents from Infocouncil
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Exporting Documents from Infocouncil The Export function will copy all reports and business papers for a month or a meeting to an exported location 1. Click on the Functions ribbon menu and choose Export . The Export Facility window opens. 2. Choose which Documents to Export. 3. Choose a Confidential Status 4. Choose either a Month or a Meeting and select the appropriate parameters. 5. Set the To file path. This defaults to the path specified by the system parameter System Export Path. If exporting to an EDRMS, see Exporting to an EDRMS for details. 6. Check or uncheck Options , as required. 7. Click on the Export button to proceed or the Cancel button to exit without exporting any documents. See Also Export - Screen Help Altering System Parameters - How To Migrating Documents - How To Export Metadata - Concept

title: Finalising Documents
text: Navigation: How-to > Finalising Documents The finalisation process will finalise documents in an EDRMS. This function is not available for file based councils, only councils who use an EDRMS. This function will tell the EDRMS to finalise or complete the set of documents defined by the forms fields. 1. Click on the Functions ribbon menu and choose Finalise . The Document Finalisation window opens. 2. Choose which Documents to Finalise. 3. Choose a Confidential Status 4. Choose either a Month or a Meeting and select the appropriate parameters. 5. Click on the Finalise button to initiate the process. Progress will be detailed in the pale box. Already finalised documents cannot be finalised again but will be skipped and this will be noted in the progress box. If a document cannot be found in the EDRMS then it will be skipped and this will be noted in the progress box. 6. Click on the Close button to exit the window. Unfinalising Documents At present there is no restriction on what documents may be finalised, so please take care. If you finalise something unintended, you will need to un-finalise it in your EDRMS to be able to continue change it in Infocouncil (e.g. including a report in a meeting).

title: Going Remote with Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes Offline and Online > Going Remote with Minutes Prerequisites The remote machine (laptop) MUST have SQL Express installed. We recommend that the version of SQL Express matches the version of SQL Server on the database server or is a more recent version of SQL Express (e.g. SQL Express 2008 R2 on the laptop can be used with SQL Server 2005 on the database server but SQL Express 2005 on the laptop cannot be used with SQL Server 2008 R2 on the database server). Steps 1. To go remote with minutes, first open Word but close all Word documents. Go File > Close to close documents until you are left with only Word's ribbon: 2. Click on the black arrow beside the Functions ribbon button and choose Remote Minutes . The Remote Minutes window opens. 3. Use the Search Criteria to find the Minutes document(s) you would like to work on. 4. Tick to select the required Minutes. 5. Click on the Take Remote button to flag the documents you would like to work on remotely. You can select multiple documents. 6. When the selected minutes are listed in the bottom part of the window, click on the Remote button to take all the selected document(s) offline. You will be asked for confirmation before Infocouncil takes the document(s) offline. 7. When the download is complete, you will receive a message confirming your system is now ready for remote minute taking. You must close Word and reopen in order to continue. 8. Disconnect from the network and work on the Minutes document(s) away from your office. Recommendation Whilst offline the minutes document(s) should be left where it is and not moved to other media or computers. However, you will not be prevented from moving the document. When returning the document back online, it must be back at the same location for the Infocouncil to function correctly. See Also Remote Minutes - Concept Returning Online with Minutes - How To Overriding Offline Minutes - How To

title: Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Importing a New Reference Type Version > Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet · The data must be in Excel format. · The worksheet to import must be the first worksheet of the workbook. · The first row must be a header row. · The first column must be the Hierarchy Position. · The second column must be the Summary. · Other fields can be in any column, they will be found by the position of the heading. · Each data row must define one item at any level. · The data rows must be in Hierarchy Position order. · Hierarchy positions SHOULD BE ENTERED AS TEXT. By default, entering 6.10 will see Excel treat it as a number and change it to 6.1. Either format the cells as text or precede the number with a single quote to force Excel to treat it as text. · Any number of items may be specified. · Items that contain no items may appear at any level. That is, it does not have to be a 3-level hierarchy, where all such items only appear at the third level. · If providing data in the List Number column, provide data for every row preferably, but at least into every bottom level item. This will enable Infocouncil to render the data well, without gaps, into its documents. · If providing data in the Full Text column, provide data for every row preferably, but at least into every bottom level item. This will enable Infocouncil to render the data well, without gaps, into its documents. · If providing data in the Report Level Hierarchy Position, the data must be a valid Hierarchy Position in the spreadsheet. · If providing data in the Report Level Hierarchy Position, the item being referred to must have an Agenda Section. · It can contain the exact following headings. Hierarchy Position Mandatory This is Infocouncil's means of understanding the structure of the data. All top-level items will be 1, 2, 3, etc respectively. All second level items will be n.1, n.2, n.3, etc, where n is the number of the top-level item that the second level item belongs to. All third level items will be n.m.1, n.m.2, etc, where n is the number of the top-level item that the second level item belongs to and m if the number of the second level item that the third level belongs to. And so forth for deeper levels. Summary Mandatory, 500-character limit This is the item text that is displayed on the Report Data form when viewing and selecting items. Pre 7.9.0 of Infocouncil, this was all we had. The summary can be just that, a summary, whist the Full Text can contain the complete text and the List Number can contain the list number. List Number Optional, 30-character limit Clients have their own way of numbering their hierarchical lists, this field contains that representation. It can be anything and it does not have to be contiguous or in order. Pre 7.9.0 clients that wanted the list number rendered in a document had to have it in the Summary. Now that it is separate, we have full control to show, Summary

title: Importing a New Reference Type Version
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Importing a New Reference Type Version 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type you wish to import a new version for. 3. Enter a unique version label for the reference type. 4. Type in, or click the browse button to locate, the spreadsheet to import the items for the new version from. 5. See Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To for full detail of the spreadsheet requirements. 6. Click import. 7. If any issues are found with the spreadsheet, they will be reported directly on this screen. Rectify any issues found and retry. 8. On success you will be taken to the Reference Type Version Maintenance screen. Add any extra details, see Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To. 9. Click Save. 10. You will be returned to the Reference Type Maintenance screen. 11. On finishing with the version, order the new version amongst the existing versions on the Details tab as you wish them to appear on the Report Data screen in the Reference Type versions combo box. Typically, the newest version should top the list. 12. Click Save. See Also Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To Reference Types - Screen Help Reference Type Versions - Screen Help Import Reference Type Version - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Adding a reference Type - How To Modifying a Reference Type - How To Removing a Reference Type - How To Adding a Reference Type Version - How To Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To Removing a Reference Type Version - How To Importing an Existing Reference Type Version - How To

title: Importing an Existing Reference Type Version
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Importing an Existing Reference Type Version 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type containing the version you wish to modify. 3. Select the version you wish to modify. 4. Click Edit. 5. You will be taken to the Reference Type Version Maintenance screen. 6. Click Import. 7. You will only be able to import over the top of an existing version if none of its items are referenced by any reports. 8. Enter a unique version label for the reference type. 9. Type in, or click the browse button to locate, the spreadsheet to import the items for the new version from. 10. See Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To for full detail of the spreadsheet requirements. 11. Click import. 12. If any issues are found with the spreadsheet, they will be reported directly on this screen. Rectify any issues found and retry. 13. On success you will be taken to the Reference Type Version Maintenance screen. Add any extra details, see Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To. 14. Click Save. 15. You will be returned to the Reference Type Maintenance screen. 16. On finishing with the version, order the new version amongst the existing versions on the Details tab as you wish them to appear on the Report Data screen in the Reference Type versions combo box. 17. Click Save. See Also Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To Reference Types - Screen Help Reference Type Versions - Screen Help Import Reference Type Version - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Adding a reference Type - How To Modifying a Reference Type - How To Removing a Reference Type - How To Importing a New Reference Type Version - How To Adding a Reference Type Version - How To Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To Removing a Reference Type Version - How To

title: Maintaining Agenda Items
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Agenda Items The agenda items were set up to customise the system for your organisation when it was implemented. Please do not alter any Agenda Items without guidance from the Infocouncil team. 1. To maintain Agenda Items, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Agenda Items . The Agenda Items window opens. 2. Search all agenda items or search by partial name to find the agenda item you are interested in. 3. Edit the agenda item as desired. 4. An important aspect of Agenda Item maintenance is defining what meeting an agenda item is to be used by. Go to the Meetings and Sections tab and add the meetings as required. For each meeting you can then define what agenda section(s) reports for this agenda item may appear in. Also for each meeting you can define any default actions that you would like automatically generated when report items for this agenda item are uploaded from a minutes document. 5. Click Save and then Close to exit the the window.

title: Maintaining Agenda Sections
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Agenda Sections 1. To maintain an agenda section, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Agenda Sections . The Agenda Sections window opens. 2. Search all agenda section or do a partial name search to locate the agenda section you wish to maintain. 3. Edit the fields as required. 4. Click Save and then Close to exit the window.

title: Maintaining Authors
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Authors The Authors window can be used to: · add a new author · edit an existing author · make an existing author inactive · delete an existing author To add a new author Before adding a new author, check that you have all the details you need. In particular, double-check their Login ID (network login). The most common reason for a new user's first attempt at using Infocouncil to fail is because their Login ID does not match their network login. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Authors . The Authors window opens. 2. Click on the New button. The right of the window will be cleared so you can enter details of the new author. 3. Enter details of the new author on the Details tab. 4. If required, enter details on the other tabs, but for most authors this will not be necessary. 5. Click on the Save button to save details of the new author. To edit an existing author Perhaps the most common reason for needing to edit an existing author is if their job title changes or they move to a different role within the organisation. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Authors . The Authors window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to locate the author you wish to edit. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual author to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Edit their details as required on any of the five tabs. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To mark an existing author as inactive Once an author has used the system to write or authorise a report, or if they have been a member of a meeting, you should never delete them because the system will have associated their name with meeting documents. Instead, you should mark them as inactive which will remove their name from lists. For example, the list of authors on the Report Data Entry window. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Authors . The Authors window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to locate the author you wish to mark as Inactive. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual author to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Uncheck the Active box. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. In the future, if you wish to find their name again, remember to check the Inactive box amongst the search criteria. To delete an author Normally you should mark an author as inactive and never delete them. But there may be instances when they have been created but never used the system. Then you may delete them. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Authors . The Authors window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to locate the author you wish to delete. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the wi

title: Maintaining Confidentiality Clauses
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Confidentiality Clauses The confidentiality clauses were set up for your organisation when the system was implemented. You should not need to change them unless your State/Territory changes its legislation. If this should happen, seek guidance from the Infocouncil team. 1. To maintain a confidentiality clause, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Closed Reasons to open the Closed Reasons maintenance screen. 2. Enter the act that contains the clause you wish to maintain and click search. 3. Edit the clause (reason) fields as required. 4. Click on save then close to exit the window. If a new Act comes in then you will need to go to the Closed Acts maintenance screen to add the new act. Then you will need to go to the Closed Reasons maintenance form to enter the clauses/reasons for the new act.

title: Maintaining Custom Help
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Custom Help 1. First, create a file containing the help you want to make available to your users. Commonly this will be a pdf or html, but it can also be an executable. For example, an hh.exe (the Windows help file CHM viewer). It cannot be a Word document because it could not be opened at the same time as many of the Infocouncil windows. Save it to a location where users will be able to access it. 2. In Word, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Custom Help Links . The Custom Help Links window opens. 3. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing help links. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 4. In the list of the left, select the individual help link to edit. The details will be displayed on the right. 5. Click on the folder button beside the Help Link field to navigate to and select the file you created in step 1. 6. Enter Arguments to automatically navigate to a specific page. If linking to an executable . 7. Click on the Test Link button to test the link you created. 8. Close the file that opened while testing the link. 9. Click on the Save button to save your changes. Or click on the Undo button to undo any details that you began to enter. 10. Click on the Close button to close the window. Notes: · See the "Custom Help - Concept" topic for help understanding Ribbon Help Links or adding a CHM file . · You cannot add items to the list on the left of the Custom Help Links window. The list is maintained by Infocouncil. · You cannot edit the Note field on the right of the window. It provides additional information about the help link wherever the link is not immediately intuitive. See Also Custom Help - Concept

title: Maintaining Deadline Types
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Deadline Types 1. To maintain a deadline type, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Deadline Types . The Deadline Types window opens. 2. To edit an existing deadline type, select it then click on the Edit button. This copies the existing text into the field below the Edit button. Edit the text then click on the tick button to save your changes. Or, click on the cross button to cancel the edit. 3. To add a new deadline type, click on the New button. This places the cursor into the field below the New button. Type the name of the new deadline type then click on the tick button to save the new deadline type. Or, click on the cross button to exit the field without saving the new deadline type. 4. To delete a deadline type, select it then click on the Delete button. This deletes the deadline type. Note: You will not be able to delete the deadline type if it is associated with a meeting type. 5. Click on the Close button to close the Deadline Types window. See Also Meeting Types Meeting Dates Maintaining Meeting Dates - How To Maintaining Meeting Types - How To Deadline Dates - Concept

title: Maintaining Divisions
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Divisions The divisions were set up for your organisation when the system was implemented. You should not need to change them unless your organisation undergoes a restructure. If divisions are re-named or a new one created, seek guidance from the Infocouncil team. However,within an organisation people change, so you may need to change the name of the Divisional (Directorate) Head. 1.
