To maintain a division, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Divisions . The Divisions window opens. 2. Locate the division you are interested in by searching all divisions or doing a partial name search. 3. To change the Divisional Head , select the name of the new Divisional Head. The drop down list contains all active authors . 4. Click Save and then Close button to exit the window.

title: Maintaining Meeting Dates
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meeting Dates The Meeting Dates window can be used to: · add a new meeting · edit an existing meeting · delete a meeting To add a new meeting 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates . The Meeting Dates window opens. 2. Click on the New button. The right of the window will be cleared so you can enter details of the new meeting. 3. Enter details of the new meeting on the General tab. 4. If your organisation is using Deadline Dates, enter details for the meeting on the Dates and Times tab. 5. If your organisation is using Descriptions, enter the details on the Descriptions tab. 6. Click on the Save button to save details of the new meeting. To edit an existing meeting 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates . The Meeting Dates window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing meetings. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the meeting to edit. Its details will be displayed on the right. 4. Edit details as required. Note: If you change the date of an existing meeting, you should check whether any reports have been written for that meeting. If they have, you will need to open each of those reports and update the Meeting Date in the Report Data screen. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To delete a meeting You cannot delete a meeting if it already has reports put to it. First, use the Document Menu or Transfer Reports functionality to remove all reports from the meeting. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates . The Meeting Dates window opens. 6. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing meetings. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 7. In the list of the left, select the meeting to delete. Its details will be displayed on the right. 2. Click on the Delete button to delete the meeting. See Also Deadline Types Meeting Types Maintaining Deadline Types - How To Maintaining Meeting Types - How To Deadline Dates - Concept Creating a Joint Meeting - How To

title: Maintaining Meetings
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meetings The Maintaining Members window can be used to: · View a Meeting · Edit a Meeting · Clone a Meeting · Creating a Joint Committee

title: Maintaining Meeting Types
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meeting Types The Meeting Types window can be used to: · add a new meeting type · edit an existing meeting type · delete a meeting type To add a new meeting type 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Types . The Meeting Types window opens. 2. Click on the New button to position the cursor in the Description field. 3. Type a Description /name for the new meeting type. 4. Enter details of the new meeting on the General tab. 5. If your organisation is using Deadline Dates, enter details for the meeting on the Dates and Times tab. 6. If your organisation is using Descriptions, enter the details on the Descriptions tab. 7. Click on the Save button to save details of the new meeting. To edit an existing meeting type 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Types . The Meeting Types window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing meetings. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the meeting to edit. Its details will be displayed on the right. 4. Edit details as required. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To delete a meeting type Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Types . The Meeting Types window opens. 1. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing meetings. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 2. In the list of the left, select the meeting to delete. Its details will be displayed on the right. 3. Click on the Delete button to delete the meeting. See Also Deadline Types Meeting Dates Maintaining Deadline Types - How To Maintaining Meeting Dates - How To Deadline Dates - Concept

title: Maintaining Members
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Members The Maintaining Members window can be used to: · add a new member · edit an existing member · make an existing member inactive · delete an existing member It can also be used to: · Synchronise Members and Memberships · Record a Leave of Absence · Record an Apology To add a new member Before adding a new member, check that you have all the details you need. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Click on the New button. The right of the window will be cleared so you can enter details of the new author. 3. Enter details of the new member on the Details tab. 4. Enter details of the new member on the Membership tab. Choose each meeting of which they are a member. Choose their position they hold in that meeting. Check the Ex-Officio box, if appropriate. Type their title for the meeting. 5. If required, enter details on the other tabs. 6. Click on the Save button to save details of the new member. To edit an existing member 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing members. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual member to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Edit their details as required on any of the five tabs. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To mark an existing member as inactive Once a member has been a member of a meeting, you should never delete them because the system will have associated their name with meeting documents. Instead, you should mark them as inactive which will remove their name from lists. For example, the lists of members in the Meeting Minutes tools. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to locate the member you wish to mark as Inactive. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual member to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Uncheck the Active box. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. In the future, if you wish to find their name again, remember to check the Inactive box amongst the search criteria. To delete a member Normally you should mark an member as inactive and never delete them. But there may be instances when they have been created but never been been associated with any meeting. Then you may delete them. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to locate the member you wish to delete. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual member to delete. Their details will be displaye

title: Maintaining Procedural Motion Types
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Procedural Motion Types The Procedural Motion Types window can be used to: · add a new procedural motion type · edit an procedural motion type · delete a procedural motion type To add a new procedural motion type 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Procedural Motion Types . The Procedural Motion Types window opens. 2. Click in the Name field and type a name for the new procedural motion type. 3. Enter the appropriate Text . Include Tags where relevant. They are listed in the Procedural Motion Types topic .. 4. Ensure Active is checked unless you do not wish this type to be available yet. 5. Enter any default actions you wish to cause actions to be automatically created when a minutes document with this procedural motion type is uploaded. 6. Click on the Save button to save details of the new procedural motion. To edit an existing procedural motion type 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Procedural Motion Types . The Procedural Motion Types window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing procedural motion types. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the procedural motion type to edit. Its details will be displayed on the right. 4. Edit details as required. 5. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To delete a procedural motion type Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Procedural Motion Types . The Procedural Motion Types window opens. 1. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing procedural motion types. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 2. In the list of the left, select the procedural motion type to delete. Its details will be displayed on the right. 3. Click on the Delete button to delete the procedural motion type. 4. If the deletion fails because this type has been used in a minutes already, edit the procedural motion type instead and make inactive. This will prevent the type appearing in the procedural motion minutes tool. See Also Procedural Motion Type

title: Maintaining Reference Types
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types Infocouncil will be set up for you with none, one or more reference types that a report type may use such that its reports can select items within the reference type to display in the Report. Each reference type can have one or more versions where a version contains a flat or hierarchical list of items. Select from the following list of tasks for more information How To Adding a reference Type How To Modifying a Reference Type How To Removing a Reference Type How To Importing a New Reference Type Version How To Adding a Reference Type Version How To Modifying a Reference Type Version How To Removing a Reference Type Version How To Importing an Existing Reference Type Version See Also Reference Types - Screen Help Reference Type Versions - Screen Help Import Reference Type Version - Screen Help Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To

title: Maintaining Subscribers
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Subscribers 1. To maintain subscribers, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and Subscribers . The Subscribers Maintenance window opens. 2. Search for a subscriber by meeting type or by a partial name search to locate the subscriber you are interested in. 3. Select a subscriber on the left to display their details on the right of the window. Update their details as appropriate then click on the Save button to save the changes you made. 4. Check and update their Meeting list that they subscribe to. 5. To unsubscribe the selected subscriber from a list, click on a meeting to which they already subscribe then click on the Delete button. 6. To add the selected subscriber to another meeting, choose the name of the meeting in the Meeting drop down. 7. To add a new subscriber, click on the New button then fill in their details on the right of the window and choose the meetings you wish them to subscribe to. Click on the Save button to save the changes you made. 8. To delete a subscriber, first select their name in the subscribers list on the left then click on the Delete button. 9. To undo changes to a subscriber, click on the Undo button. 10. To save your changes, click on the Save button. 11. Click on the Close button to exit the window.

title: Making an Attachment Confidential
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Making an Attachment Confidential When you make a report confidential then all the attachments to that report are automatically also confidential. However, sometimes you may wish to create an open report with a confidential attachment attached to it. 1. To make an individual attachment confidential , first open the report then click on the Attachments button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Attachments window opens. 2. Select (or add) the attachment that you wish to make confidential. With its details displayed on the right of the window, click on the padlock button beside the Confidentiality field. 3. Enter Confidential Reasons , as you would if you were making a report confidential . 4. On the Confidential Reasons form, click OK so the reasons you chose appear in the Confidentiality field of the Attachments window. 5. Click Save to save the details of your attachment. Its title will appear in the list on the left of the Attachments window. 6. Click Close to close the Attachments window. 7. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon to save your report. See Also Making a Report Confidential - How to Adding an Attachment - How to Editing an Attachment - How to Removing an Attachment - How to

title: Making a Report Confidential
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Making a Report Confidential 1. To make a report confidential, first open the report then click on the Confidential Reasons button ("confidential" may be customised at your site) on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Confidential Reasons window opens. When you make a report confidential then all its attachments are automatically also made confidential. While adding attachments you can click on the Confidentiality button to open the same window, so it is possible to create an open report with a confidential attachment . 2. Select an applicable Act/Regulation . Often you will have only one choice, but you may have several to choose from. 3. Check the clauses that are applicable to your report. To help you choose, hover the cursor over a clause so you can read the full text of the clause in the tool tip. 4. Enter a Code Reason in the grid beside each code that you select. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it . 5. Enter a Reason . This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. 6. Check the component(s) that confidentiality applies to . It may be the report, its attachments, its resolutions or any combination of these. It cannot apply to nothing. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it . 7. Select a Review Date . This is the date upon which the confidentiality of the report will be reviewed to see whether it should remain confidential or not. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it . 8. Type a Review Event . This is an event upon which the confidentiality of the report will be reviewed to see whether it should still be confidential or not. For example, following the next general election. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. 9. Click the Review Assistance button to display a help PDF document. Applicable to only some organisations . 10. Click OK to update your report. Depending on how the system is configured for your organisation you will probably see the clauses you selected or the word 'Confidential' or 'Closed' appear somewhere in your report. 11. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon. This is the action that actually makes your report confidential. See Also Confidential Reasons Making an Attachment Confidential - How to Removing Confidentiality from a Report - How to

title: Migrating Documents
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Migrating Documents Show/Hide Hidden Text Infocouncil allows reports and papers to be stored on the file server, in an EDRMS or on SharePoint. This topic discusses how to move your documents from one of these locations to another. For any migration, be clear on how to do it, rehearse in a test environment and back up the production database. Once started, interrupting and winding back the migration can be quite fiddly. Migrating from an EDRMS to File Server or SharePoint When Infocouncil is configured to work with reports and papers in an EDRMS, documents can be migrated to the file server or to SharePoint. The process sees the following major steps occur · Reports, Papers and possibly their attachments are copied (not moved) to the new location, · The Infocouncil database is updated to point to the new location of each document, · Original documents in the EDRMS are edited, where possible (if not read only), to clear the Infocouncil identification, · Migrated documents are edited so where they identify the location of attachments, the location is updated to their new location. When complete, the configuration to allow Infocouncil to work with the EDRMS needs to be removed. For this reason you want to migrate all documents out of the EDRMS, otherwise Infocouncil will no longer be able to work with documents left behind. If you open and edit a document migrated outside the EDRMS, whilst the EDRMS configuration is still in place, Infocouncil will save the document back into the EDRMS. This is something that must be managed. If you open but do not edit, the document will not be moved. We recommend you approach the task in the following way after performing a practice run in your test environment A. Configure the new File Server or SharePoint locations in Infocouncil (this will not impact the status quo with using the EDRMS) B. Migrate historical documents, e.g. anything older than 6 months. Ensure your users are careful not to touch those documents until you complete the entire migration. C. Have a few users involved in the migration of the remaining "modern" documents. The more users doing so in parallel, the faster the process. D. Get everyone off Infocouncil for a few hours. You will get a feel for how long it will take based on step B. E. Migrate the remaining documents. F. Remove the EDRMS configuration. G. Allow users back into Infocouncil. Alternatively you may combine step B into step D and do all documents together. The following are the sub-steps for A. Configure the new File Server or SharePoint locations 1. Determine the structure for how the documents will be stored for papers, reports and attachments. Refer to documents "Infocouncil Installation and Training.pdf" (File Server and SharePoint) and "Guide to SharePoint Integration with Infocouncil.pdf" (SharePoint only). Call Infocouncil help desk and request these to be provided to you. 2. Create this structure of folders in your file serv

title: Minutes Tags

If a report type item tagReportAuthorLastNameFirstName The name of the author in the format surname space first name. If a report type item tagTitle The title of the report tagResolution The complete resolution for the item. A formatting tag may be included (e.g. <tagResolution,0_8_Arial> - will leave bold, underline etc. but change size to 8 and font to Arial) tagStatus N/A / Carried / Lost NOT AVAILABLE YET tagMover The movers name tagSeconder The seconders name tagActionOfficerInitials The initials of each action officer tagActionOfficerNames The names of each action officer tagActionInitialDate The initial target date tagActionTargetDate The revised target date tagActionCompletionDate The date action completed tagActionEDMSNo The actions EDRMS Record ID For EDRMS organisations only tagNotes Insert the list of officers and action notes. Any number of these formatting options may be added (comma delimited): o Type - ALL for all notes OR types of notes delimited by as semi-colon (;) - (User, Target Date Revision, Reallocation, Completion, Email) (e.g. <tagNotes,User;Completion> - will show only User and Completion type notes). The Type tag must be in the first position after the tag name. All other formatting tags below can be in any order delimited by commas. o OneLine - note

title: Modify or Create Report Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modify or Create Report Templates Infocouncil depends on Word templates containing tags to produce a number of reports . A tag is place holder that is swapped with data when the report is produced. Each report type has a finite list of tags that can be used in a template. Each report type may have multiple templates which can define different layouts and different tags. Templates must be named a certain way and located in your Infocouncil installation folder, under Templates or the Templates\Reports folders. Note: If there exists a Reports folder under the Templates folder, then Infocouncil will not look for templates in the Templates folder. So either have them all in the Templates folder without a Reports folder, or have them all in the Reports folder. The report types are as listed and not those that are produced to make up an Agenda. Click on one for specific details on that report type. · List Reports from a Meeting · Yearly Meeting Schedule · Meeting Timetable · Minutes Index · Minutes by Resolution · Monitor Action Sheets · Meeting Events Reports The best way to create a new template for a report type is to copy a template of the same type, then rename and modify it. Understanding the general structure of a template and its tags is essential to being able to modify a template. General Structure A report template generally consists of a header and footer and body. The body will contain a table with either 2 or more rows. The first row is always the header row. The subsequent row or rows, the data row(s), display the detail for one entity (e.g. for one action or for one minute item). When a report is generated, the subsequent rows are repeated for as many times as there are entities. Headers & Footers A report template may or may not utilise the header and footer. Generally they are utilised to display such information as a title, today's date and page numbering. They can also employ document variable fields which will be substituted for data when the report is produced. These differ from tags in that they are non-repeating data. For example they might be used to display the name of the person producing the report or the meeting type that the report's data is for or a date range. Single Row or Multi Row A report can be structured to have either a header row plus one data row, or a header row plus many data rows. The advantage of a single row report is that it executes much faster. The advantage of a multi-row report is that it can accommodate much more complexity in the layout. Single Row Example Multi Row Example Tag Syntax Tags are placed within a cell in a report inside angle brackets (e.g. <tagCommittee>). Data Tags Other than General Tags, all tags are data tags. They identify to Infocouncil the type of data that the tag will be substituted with when the report is generated. General Tags There are two general tags that can be used for any report. They do not result in data being substitute

title: Modify Action Sheet Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modifying the Email Templates > Modify Action Sheet Templates Generally we encourage an organisation to contact Infocouncil help desk to modify an action sheet template. However this topic provides many of the details on how to do this. Make any changes in a test environment and validate prior to making changes in production. Infocouncil depends on two Word templates containing tags and an optional mask to produce action sheets . A tag is place holder that is swapped with data when the report is produced. There is one template for the version of the action sheet which is used for emailing and the other template is used when an action sheet is printed or committed to an EDRMS. The mask is a script which Infocouncil staff may have provided. Using script provides much more power in the way the action sheet is generated. It may not be needed if your action sheets are not complex. On the other hand the template may contain very little if the script is doing almost all the work. The templates are in doc or docx format and must be named XXX _ActionSheet.doc(x) and XXX _ActionEmail.doc(x), where XXX is the council code. They must be located in the Templates folder. General Structure A template generally consists of a header and footer and body. Any header and footer for the email template is ignored. The body can be in any format desired and may incorporate tags, bookmark fields, hyperlinks and document variable fields. Sample Action Sheet Template Headers & Footers A template may or may not utilise the header and footer. They could be utilised to display such information as a title, today's date and page numbering. They can also employ document variable fields or tags which will be substituted for data when the report is produced. Tags Tags identify to Infocouncil the type of data that the tag will be substituted with when the report is generated. Unlike for other reports that use tags (e.g. Monitor Actions Report), the list of tags available for use must be mapped through the tblFormatTemplateSwapStrings database table. Tags have no specific syntax. Any string can be a tag but it must be mapped to something Infocouncil recognises. So the template may have "<MEETINGDATE>" in it, which the table maps to "minDateMeeting", which tells Infocouncil that "<MEETINGDATE>" is to be swapped with the actual meeting date. So when adding or changing a tag to a template it can be called anything at all (it does not require angle brackets around it). See below for the list of possible internal data identifiers that a tag may be mapped to. Special Tags There is one tag which requires no mapping, <OfficerList>. If there is more than one Action Sheet associated with the Minute Item this returns a list of the Responsible Officer names comma delimited. Bookmark Fields If a bookmark named Proceedings is present, then the Resolution pertaining to the Minute Item is inserted at this location. Hyperlinks Hyperlinks can be added t

title: Modifying a Reference Type
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Modifying a Reference Type 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type you wish to modify from the list on the left. 3. The label may be changed as desired so long as it is unique for the reference type. 4. The version label for the reference type may be changed as desired. On the Report Data form, when this type is selected the label for its versions will be changed to this. 5. The item label may be changed as desired. On the Report Data form, when this type is selected the label for its list of items will be changed to this. 6. Go to the Report Types tab and change which Report Types will use this reference type. Selected types will appear in the Report Data screen when the report types are selected. If you remove a report type to no longer use a reference type, then any reports that no longer use that type will not be affected. 7. For each report type using the reference type a. Changing any of the following rules will not affect existing reports. However should such a reports data be modified it may be revalidated and the changed rules might cause a prior item selection to no longer be valid. b. Change the Reference Type Order as desired. You will see all reference types the selected report type uses, and you will be able to set the order as they will appear on the Report Data form here. c. Set what items can be selected for the reference type and report type d. Set whether selection is prohibited from the same lineage. If true, then if a user selects an item, then they will be prevented from additionally selecting one of the items ancestors (parent, grandparent, etc) or descendants (child, grandchild, etc). e. Set the agenda defaulting rules i. Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to true, if you want the agenda section to be defaulted on the Report Data form, when a reference type item is selected and either the item has a default agenda section specified, or the item has a report level specified which references another item which does have a default agenda section specified. ii. Set Agenda section selection defaults reference type item to true, if when the agenda section is selected on the Report Data form, you want any reference type items with a matching default agenda section, to be automatically selected. 8. Click Save 9. Versions may now be added or modified. See Adding a reference Type - How To or Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To. 10. On finishing with the versions, order the versions on the Details tab as you wish them to appear on the Report Data screen in the Reference Type versions combo box. 11. Click Save See Also Reference Types - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Adding a reference Type - How To Removing a Reference Type - How To Importing a New Reference Type Version

title: Modifying a Reference Type Version
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Modifying a Reference Type Version 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type you wish to edit a version for. 3. Select the version you wish to edit. 4. Click the Edit button 5. You will be taken to the Reference Type Version Maintenance screen. 6. Modify the label for the version as desired. Make sure it is unique to the reference type. 7. Add items as required, delete items if needed. 8. Move items to different locations in the tree as required. 9. Each selected items details can be modified a. A list number is optional but would typically match the numbering used in the organisations source document (is any used at all). Maximum 30 characters. Be aware, if you already have a version with the Summary being prefixed with list numbers, separating the list numbers out into this field will have no benefit, unless the scripting to produce the report is also changed to utilise the List Number field. b. The Summary is mandatory. It can be up to 500 characters and it is the text that is shown in the tree for this screen and also for the Report Data screen. c. Optionally enter the Full Text if the summary is a summarised version of the full text. Maximum 4000 characters. Be aware, if you already have a version with the Summary captured but not the Full Text, unless the scripting to produce the report is also changed to utilise Full Text, adding Full Text will not have any benefit. d. Optionally enter a Report Level and/or Agenda Section. i. If an Agenda Section is provided 1. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to true, then selection of this item for a report will see the agenda section defaulted to this items agenda section. If the agenda section is not provided for the item, then it will look to see if a Report Level is defined. See point ii. a. If there is no agenda section provided and a report level is not provided, then an exception will result. 2. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to false, then any agenda section will be ignored 3. If the Agenda section selection defaults reference type item to true, then if a matching agenda section is selected for the report, then this item will automatically be selected. ii. If a Report Level is provided, then it must be pointing to another item in the reference version that has an Agenda Section provided. 1. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda section to true, then selection of this item for a report will see the agenda section defaulted to the Report Levels items agenda section. a. If no agenda section is provided, an exception will result. 2. If the reference type rule has the Set Reference type item selection defaults agenda sectio

title: Modify or Create List Reports from a Meeting Report Template
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modify or Create Report Templates > Modify or Create List Reports from a Meeting Report Template Ensure you have read the Modify or Create Report Templates topic before this one. Available From: The List Reports icon in the Main group of the ribbon. Also from the Reporting list in the Administration section. Purpose: Lists details of reports for a meeting Type: User Configurable / Tag-Driven / Singe or Multi row Template: [ Council Prefix_ ]List Reports[ - name ].docx · Council Prefix and Name are optional · Examples: o List Reports - one row per item.docx o List Reports - attachments in second row.docx o List Reports - detailed with recommendation.docx o List Reports - recommended items.docx Available Tags Tag Name Description Note tagCount The sequence number 1,2,3 etc. tagCommittee The committee name tagMeetingDate The date of the meeting tagSpecial Special meeting True or False tagFileNumber The report file number As entered on the Report Dialog tagEDMSNumber The EDRMS Document ID For EDRMS councils (Trim, InfoXpert, DataWorks) tagFolder The EDRMS Folder number or name For EDRMS councils tagFileName The name of the Word document e.g. ORD120321001SR_1.DOC tagFullFilePath The path of the Word document For NON-EDRMS councils tagItemNumber The reports item number tagReportType The type of report e.g. Standard Report tagSectionName The agenda section of the report tagAuthors The names of the author(s) of the report tagAuthorsAndTitles The names of the author(s) of the report with titles e.g. Joe Smith General Manager tagApprovers The names of the authorisers(s) of the report tagApproversAndTitles The names of the authorisers(s) of the report with titles e.g. Joe Smith General Manager tagSubject The reports subject/title tagPurpose Text from the Purpose text box Text is entered into this control on the Report Dialog for some councils only tagCouncil Text from the Council text box Text is entered into this control on the Report Dialog for some councils only tagCreated The date the report was created tagModified The date the report was last modified tagApproved Authorised (Yes/No) Has the report been fully authorised? tagClosed Closed (Yes/No) Is the report confidential? tagLate Late (Yes/No) Is the report late (supplementary)? tagIncluded Included (Yes/No) Is the included in the meeting? tagPresentation Is there a presentation for the report (Yes/No) Presentations are updated on the Report Dialog for some councils only tagUtility Utility Checkbox 1 (Yes/No) This checkbox will be available on the Report Dialog for some councils only (the column should be given an appropriate name) tagUtility2 Utility Checkbox 2 (Yes/No) This checkbox will be available on the Report Dialog for some councils only (the column should be given an appropriate name) tagAttachmentsCount The count of attachments Will be suffixed with an asterisk if any closed attachments tagAttachments The list of attachments A formatted list of

filter criteria. dvDateTo Any meeting date to entered in the filter criteria. dvMeetingDates Any meeting date range the meeting events are filtered by. dvOpenAndOrConfidential Whether meeting events for open or closed minutes/minute items are included in the filter criteria. dvEventCategories Any meeting event type categories the meeting events are filtered by. dvEventTypes Any meeting event types the meeting events are filtered by. dvMembers Any member names the actions are filtered by. dvSortOrder Any sort order applied to the meeting events dvCount The number of meeting event meeting the filter criteria Available Tags Tag Name Description Note tagEventId The Id number of the meeting event tagEventCategoryId The Id of the meeting type category tagEventCategory The meeting type category tagEventTypeId The Id of the meeting event type tagEventType The meeting event type tagCommittee, tagCommitteeText The meeting name followed by a space then the meeting date tagMeetingDate The meeting date tagMeetingTime The meeting time tagSpecial Special meeting tagClosed Closed minutes tagMeetingDateId The ID of the meeting tagEventDate The date the meeting event occurred tagEventTimeToSeconds The time the meeting event occurred in 24 hour time to seconds tagEventTimeToThousandthsOfSecond The time the meeting event occurred in 24 hour time to thousandths of a second tagEventDescription The description of the meeting event tagEventMemberName The meeting event's associated member's name tagEventMemberFirstName The meeting event's associated member's first name tagEventMemberSurname The meeting event's associated member's surname tagCount The sequence number 1,2,3 etc. Also all Minutes Tags . title: Modify or Create Meeting Timetable Report Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modify or Create Report Templates > Modify or Create Meeting Timetable Report Templates Ensure you have read the Modify or Create Report Templates topic before this one. Available From: Administration group of ribbon Reports List Purpose: Lists all the meetings for a time period or for a cycle as one meeting per row. The report can include deadline information from the second tab on the Meeting dates screen Type: User Configurable / Tag-Driven / Single or Multi row Template: Multiple templates are allowed. They must start with [Council Prefix_] Meeting Timetable[ - Name ] · Council Prefix and Name are optional · Examples: o Meeting Timetable - meeting details.docx o Meeting Timetable - with deadlines.docx Notes: The Meeting Timetable report handles different formatting of meeting dates and times. For example, the report may require one column to show the day as "Mon", another column to show the date as "1-Jan", another to show the time as 4.00, another to show the pm or am, and many more. Note that the Meeting Time field ( tagMeetingTime ) is a text field and therefore may not be able to take advantage of the format feature unless entered in the correct format into the Meeting Dates form. If the time is entered in the format of h:mm any format nominated will be applied in the report. Note the colon vs the period as the period notation is not a valid time hour/minute delimiter. The tags for all deadline dates are based on the Deadline Type names. By removing the spaces, prefixing it with "tag" and suffixing it with "Date" or "Time" (e.g. "Final Reports Required" becomes both "tagFinalReportsRequiredDate" and "tagFinalReportsRequiredTime". The Description 1 and Description 2 text fields on the Meeting dates screen will be substituted in place of the Meeting Date and Meeting Time fields when they are not empty. Available Tags Tag Name Description Note tagYear The year entered on the report screen Would be in header of report tagCycleNumber The cycle number entered on the report screen Would be in header of report tagStartDate The start date entered on the report screen Would be in header of report tagEndDate The end date entered on the report screen Would be in header of report tagCommittee The name of the meeting type (committee) tagMeetingDate The date of the meeting May be substituted with the text from the Description 1 field on the Meeting dates screen tagMeetingTime The time of the meeting May be substituted with the text from the Description 2 field on the Meeting dates screen Dealine Date tags The tags for all deadline dates are based on each Deadline Type name. This is done by removing the spaces, prefixing the name with "tag" and suffixing it with "Date" or "Time" (e.g. "Final Reports Required" becomes both "tagFinalReportsRequiredDate" and "tagFinalReportsRequiredTime".

title: Modify or Create Monitor Actions Report Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modify or Create Report Templates > Modify or Create Monitor Actions Report Templates Ensure you have read the Modify or Create Report Templates topic before this one. Available From: Infocouncil Ribbon Actions Print button. They must start with [Council Prefix_] Actions Report[ - Name ] · Council Prefix and Name are optional · Examples: o Actions Report - Standard.docx o Actions Report - Item Number.docx o Actions Report.docx o Actions Report - Further Reports.docx Available Document Variables Fields Field Name Description dvReportText Describes the basic search criteria selected for the set of actions being reported on. It is a combination of dvReportText, dvCommitteeText Any committee names the actions are filtered by. dvDivision Any division names the actions are filtered by. dvOfficer Any action officer names the actions are filtered by. dvList Any of the report types ("Report", "New Item", "Set Item") that the actions are filtered by. dvOutstandingFinalisedOverdue What action states are included. Either "Outstanding/Finalised" or "Overdue" or "Outstanding" or "Finalised" dvType What action types are included. Either "For Action/Information" or "For Action" or "Information" dvActionInformation What action types are included. Either "For Action/Information" or "For Action" or "Information" dvOpenAndOrConfidential Whether included actions are open, closed or both. Either "Open/Confidential" or "Confidential" or "Open" dvMinuteResolutionUserdefined What included actions relate to. Either"Minute/Resolution/User Defined" or "Minute/Resolution" or "Minute/User Defined" or "Resolution/User Defined" or "Minute" or "Resolution" or "Resolution" dvFurtherReportRequired Whether actions are filtered by those including or excluding further reports, or including further reports but limited to certain committees. dvFutureCommittee Any further committees that the actions are filtered by. dvActionSubTypes Any action sub types the actions are filtered by. dvTitleOrResolution Any title or resolution text the actions are filtered by. dvDateFrom Any meeting date from entered in the filter criteria. dvDateTo Any meeting date to entered in the filter criteria. dvMeetingDates Any meeting date range the actions are filtered by. dvTargetDates Any target date range the actions are filtered by. dvFinalisedDates Any completed date range the actions are filtered by. dvMinuteNumber Any minute number the actions are filtered by. dvBranch Any branch the actions are filtered by. dvReportTypes Any action officer names the actions are filtered by. title: Modify or Create Yearly Meeting Schedule Report Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modify or Create Report Templates > Modify or Create Yearly Meeting Schedule Report Templates Ensure you have read the Modify or Create Report Templates topic before this one. Available From: Administration group of ribbon Reports List Purpose: Lists all the meetings for a 12 month period for each Meeting Type (Committee) and within each month or within each cycle (for cycle based councils) Type: Headings, footers and text may be added but the report itself is non-configurable / Multiple Layouts Template: Multiple templates are allowed. They must start with [Council Prefix]_Yearly Meeting Schedule (e.g. Yearly Meeting Schedule (Year).docx) Available Tags None

title: Modifying the Email Templates
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Modifying the Email Templates Infocouncil generates several emails from different points in the system. The body of these emails are based on HTML templates. The subjects of these emails (where they are customisable) are based on plain string templates. The tblFormatTemplates database table stores the exact file name of the HTML templates and if not stored in the main Templates directory (as defined in System Parameters - System - Templates Directory) then the path is also stored with the file name. The same table stores the string templates for the subjects. Each record in this table is for one template and will either point to a template file or contain the string template. Someone with a basic understanding of HTML can open up a relevant HTML template file in Notepad or an HTML editor (Word can be used but it tends to bloat the template file with unnecessary code) and customise it as required. This includes adding and formatting , logo and so forth. String templates may be directly customised in the table Each template (HTML or string) allows variables to be embedded into the template which Infocouncil will detect and swap out for certain values from the database. Templates are categorised into · Approvals Mail Templates · Subscriber Notification Templates · Action Sheet Templates · Actions Mail Template · Presentations · Report Mail Template · eVoting Mail Template The follow assists how to find the desired templates from the tblFormatTemplates database table. And importantly, what variables are available to be inserted into the templates. All variables begin with "varsub_" and end with "_varsub" to avoid any confusion with raw HTML code. Approvals Mail Templates Within the tblFormatTemplates database table, where the forCategory field contains "Approvals Mail Template", these are the approvals mail templates. The fotPurpose field identifies the purpose of each of the templates and they are described more below. A single template may be used for multiple purposes. None of the subjects for these emails are customisable. Purpose Description Notify Report Authoriser For notifying the next authoriser of a report that their authorisation is requested. Notify Paper Authoriser For notifying the next authoriser of a paper that their authorisation is requested. Authorise Report For notifying the one who notified that the report has been authorised. Authorise Paper For notifying the one who notified that the paper has been authorised. Return Report For notifying the one who notified that the report has been returned and not authorised. Return Paper For notifying the one who notified that the paper has been returned and not authorised. Email Author For emailing the author from the Create Paper screen. The following are the variables available to these templates Variable Name Description Note MeetingDate The meeting date in "dd MMM yyyy" format concatenated with the Meeting Time. If the meeting is special t

title: Monitoring Action Sheets
text: Navigation: How-to > Monitoring Action Sheets Monitoring Action Sheets is made up of several individual tasks. Most of the tasks are optional, but often you will want to add a note. The one task that you should perform is to finalise the action. · Adding Notes to an Action Sheet · Revising the Target Date for an Action · Allocating an Action to Another Officer · Completing an Action

title: Moving a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Printing a List of Reports for a Meeting > Moving a Report 1. To move a report, first consider the circumstances and your access rights. 2. If you have edit access to the report and it has not yet been compiled into an agenda, simple open the report, click on the Report Data button on the Infocouncil toolbar and change the meeting name and/or date. Re-save the report before closing. 3. Or, if the report has been already been put to a meeting (i.e. agenda and minutes of original meeting have been completed) then the meeting administrator can defer or refer the report. This option is used when the members of the original meeting resolve to defer/refer a report to another meeting. The item remains in the agenda and minutes of the original meeting, but the report itself moves to the new meeting. 4. To defer or refer a report, first open the report. 5. Check the Title bar . The report will have a title comprising a unique collection of letters and numbers representing the meeting it was originally put to (e.g. CO20161001_... went to the council meeting held on 1 Oct 2016) 6. Click on the black arrow beside the Move/Copy button on the Infocouncil ribbon and select Defer/Refer . The Defer or Refer a Report window opens. 7. Select the Name and Date of the meeting you wish to put the deferred/referred report to. 8. Also select the Report Type and Agenda Section . The choices available will depend on how the system has been configured for your organisation . 9. Click OK . 10. Wait while Infocouncil moves and saves the report. The system will inform you when the item has been successfully processed. 11. Click OK to clear the message. 12. Check the Title bar of the report. The moved report will have details of the new meeting in its file name (e.g. CO20161108_... will go to the council meeting to be held on 8 Nov 2016) 13. The moved report now needs to be put through the authorisation process. See Also Shifting Reports Between Meetings - Concept Save Sending a Report for Authorisation - How To

title: Overriding Offline Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes Offline and Online > Overriding Offline Minutes It is possible to override being offline if, for example, a disaster befalls the laptop that you offline the minutes to, or you simply want the documents quickly brought back online (the laptop will still think it is offline until it attempts to go online again). 1. To override offline minutes, c lick on the About Infocouncil button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The About Infocouncil window opens. 2. Click on the Disable Remote button. 3. All the documents you took offline will be released. (you will lose any changes you have made to the documents). You can now start again. See Also Remote Minutes - Concept Going Remote with Minutes - How To Returning Online with Minutes - How To

title: Previewing Attachments
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Adding Attachments > Previewing Attachments 1. To preview attachments, first write your report, add the attachments and re-save your report. 2. With your report still open, click on the Other ribbon menu and select Preview Attachments . The Cover Sheet Report form opens, with Append Attachments checked. 3. Check the Include Cover Sheet box if you wish to precede your preview document with a page of statistics. 4. Click on the OK button. 5. Wait while Infocouncil assembles your report and its attachments into a Word document that you do not need to save. The title of the document displayed will be Document1 (or similar), indicating it is the throw-away document. Inspect your task bar - you should have two documents open - your report (title is a combination of letters and numbers) and the temporary document . 6. Print the throw-away document if you wish. 7. Close the throw-away document without saving it. 8. Close your report. You should not need to save it because the system forced you to do so before you began. Tip Before step 7, you could save the throw-away document if you want to. But you need to be aware that you will be saving it outside of the system. Any changes you make in the throw-away document will NOT be reflected in your Infocouncil report. If anyone edits the throw-away document saved outside Infocouncil then those same edits will need to be made to the report saved in Infocouncil. See Also Preview Attachments

title: Printing a List of Reports for a Meeting
text: Navigation: How-to > Printing a List of Reports for a Meeting 1. To print a list of reports for a meeting, click on the List Reports for a Meeting button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The List Reports for a Meeting window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting Type and Meeting Date . 3. If the list you want is for a meeting that occurred more than 6 months ago, check the All Dates box to add dates to the Meeting Dates list. 4. Choose a filter... 5. Choose a format... 6. Click on the Continue button. 7. Wait while Infocouncil assembles the data and displays it in a Word document. 8. Edit or print the document if you wish. It is a 'throw-away' document that you should not need to save. See Also Document Menu Shifting Reports Between Meetings - Concept Moving a Report - How To

title: Releasing, Printing, Emailing or Committing Action Sheets
text: Navigation: How-to > Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes > Releasing, Printing, Emailing or Committing Action Sheets 1. To Release, Print, Email or Commit (to an EDRMS) action sheets, click on the Generate Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Generate Actions window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting , Meeting Date and Minutes Type of the minutes document containing the minute items with the action sheets. 3. If the minutes document has never been uploaded then follow the steps in Generating Action Sheets . 4. Select the minute items you wish to process by checking the check box in the row of the items, or 5. Click the Select button to open the Select Minute Items window. Select by Section (s), Item Type (s) and/or Officer (s) then click OK to close the window. Minute Items will then be selected to match the criteria you chose. 6. Click the Process Action Sheets menu button and select the relevant process. 7. Wait for the process to complete. A message informing you of the number processed will appear. Click OK to close the message. See Also Generate Actions Select Minute Items Generating Action Sheets - How To

title: Processing Destination Folders
text: Navigation: How-to > Processing Destination Folders Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Process Destination Folders opens the Process Destination Folders window. The processing destination folders task involves moving all the documents for a meeting from current/working/draft folders/containers to official/destination folders/containers. This function is not available for file based councils, only councils who use an EDRMS. This function will tell the EDRMS to finalise or complete the set of documents defined by the forms fields. 1. Click on the Functions ribbon menu and choose Process Destination Folders . The Process Destination Folders window opens. 2. Choose a Meeting and Meeting Date . 3. Click on the OK button to initiate the process. A log of the process appears in the centre of window. The process is complete when the value of Finished appears. See Also Draft and Official EDRMS Locations - Concept

title: Publishing Papers
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers Prior to publishing, destinations to publish to must be configured first. Any number of destinations may be configured. A destination has a type which defines what technology is used to upload files to the destination. Supported types are FTP, SharePoint, File Server, LG Hub and Web API. · Create a Publish Destination Next you require something to publish. Papers can be converted to an HTML or PDF format or both, and these formats may be published. · Creating an HTML Document · Creating a PDF Document Now the HTML or PDF format of a paper can be published. · Publishing to the Web · Publishing to LG Hub Unpublishing from the Web. · Unpublishing from the web See Also Publish - Screen Help Publish Destinations - Screen Help Meetings Publish Destinations - Screen Help Publish Destinations - Concept

title: Publishing to LG Hub
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Publishing to LG Hub Background When publishing a business paper to LG Hub, you will have control over which channel it is published to. All papers published for the same meeting and to the same channel will reside in the same story. If any papers for the same meeting are published to a different channel then a separate story will contain the papers in that channel. The channels that can be published to can be restricted by using the Allowed Channels field available on the Publish Destinations window for LG Hub destination types. Default channels can be set up for an LG Hub type publish destination via the Default Open Channel and Default Closed Channel fields on the Publish Destinations window. Any of these can be overridden for individual meetings via the Meeting Publish Destinations window. It is also important to note that if a channel filter is in place using the Allowed Channels field that does NOT included the desired default channels then these will not appear for selection on the publish screen. The title and description of a story and the title of any document uploaded, will be defaulted based on the Story Title Format, Default Cover Notice Text Format and Document Title Format fields on the Publish Destinations window for LG Hub destination types. These can be overridden for individual meetings via the Meeting Publish Destinations window. Note that the Story Title Format and the Default Cover Notice Text Format fields may contain HTML. An example of using HTML in the story description is shown below, it includes "%m", "%d.." and a couple of other "%" formats, which will be substituted for the meeting name, date and other values (see the Publish Destinations window for more detail): Official Meeting Notification Notice is hereby given that a meeting of the %m will be held on %d(dd MMM yyyy) commencing at %mt in the %ml An agenda is attached. A light supper will be provided. Please note the following: A site visit will be conducted at Uluru commencing 4:30pm. A presentation will be delivered by a company When the business paper is published, the created story will look similar to: Please note that the BigTinCan console available at https://app.bigtincan.com.au provides a reasonable editor to author a story title or description which can then be obtained by selecting the 'Source' button on the editor and copying the content. You can then substitute the mark-up with Infocouncil variables as required and enter into the respective fields on the Publish Destinations or Meeting Publish Destinations windows or on the LG Hub Cover Notice field on the Descriptions tab of the Meeting Dates window. Steps 1. To publish an Infocouncil business paper to LG Hub ensure your business papers have been prepared for publication by converting to PDF and then click on the Publish ribbon menu and choose Publish . The Publish Business Papers . window opens. 2. Choose the business paper to publish. Only PDF documents may

title: Publishing to the Web
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Publishing to the Web 1. To publish an Infocouncil Business Paper to the web, first open your completed Agenda, Minutes or Attachments document then click on the Publish ribbon menu and choose Publish . The Publish Business Papers . window opens. 2. Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, you may have several publishing targets. Select the destinations by checking the box in each column for each item (or check the box in the column header to select all items for a column). 3. If you wish to retain the source document, uncheck the Delete source files after publish button. For example, you may wish to publish to one site first, and then another later. When you have finished you can then delete the source files by clicking the trash-can button at the end of each line. 4. Click on the Publish button to publish the documents. The Progress log and Status field will show you progress as the files are published. 5. Finally, go to the web, open the files you have just published and check that all is as you expected. See Also Creating an HTML Document - How To Creating a PDF Document - How To Unpublishing from the Web - How To Publishing to LG Hub - How To

title: Recording an Apology
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Members > Recording an Apology 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing members. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual member to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Click on the Apologies tab. 5. Choose a Meeting. 6. Choose a Meeting Date date. 7. Click on the Add button to list the apology in the bottom of the window. 8. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To Remove an Apology 1. Select the listed Apology. 2. Click on the Remove Apology button. 3. Click on the Save button to save your changes. See Also Recording an Apology Maintaining Members

title: Recording a Leave of Absence
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Members > Recording a Leave of Absence 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Members . The Members window opens. 2. Use the Search Criteria across the top of the window to generate a list of existing members. The results of your search will be listed on the left of the window. 3. In the list of the left, select the individual member to edit. Their details will be displayed on the right. 4. Click on the Leave of Absence tab. 5. Choose a From date. 6. Choose a To date. 7. Click on the Add button to list the absence on the right of the window. 8. Click on the Save button to save your changes. To Remove a Leave of Absence 1. Select the Leave of Absence on the right of the window. 2. Click on the Remove Leave of Absence button. 3. Click on the Save button to save your changes. See Also Recording an Apology Maintaining Members

title: Removing an Attachment
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Removing an Attachment 1. To remove an attachment from a report, first open the report then click on the Attachments button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Attachments window opens. 2. Select the name of the attachment you wish to remove. Its details will be displayed on the right of the window. 3. Click on the Delete button. 4. You will be asked if you are certain you want to delete this item. Click Yes . Details of your attachment will disappear from the Attachments window. This only breaks the link between attachment and report. It does not delete the actual attachment. 5. Click Close to close the Attachments window. 6. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon to save the change to your report. See Also Adding an Attachment - How to Editing an Attachment - How to Making an Attachment Confidential - How to

title: Removing a Reference Type
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Removing a Reference Type 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type you wish to delete from the list on the left. 3. Click delete. 4. Click yes to the confirmation message. 5. You will be prevented from deleting the reference type if any of its items are referenced by any report. 6. If deletable then all versions and items for the reference type will also be deleted. See Also Reference Types - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Adding a reference Type - How To Modifying a Reference Type - How To Importing a New Reference Type Version - How To Adding a Reference Type Version - How To Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To Removing a Reference Type Version - How To Importing an Existing Reference Type Version - How To

title: Removing a Reference Type Version
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Reference Types > Removing a Reference Type Version 1. Click on Reference Types under the Maintenance drop down, in the Administration group of the ribbon. The Reference Types Maintenance window opens. 2. Select the reference type you wish to edit a version for. 3. Select the version you wish to delete. 4. Click the cross button next to the list. 5. Click yes to the confirmation message. 6. The version and all its items will be deleted. 7. You will be prevented from deleting the reference type version if any of its items are referenced by any report. 8. Click Save. See Also Reference Types - Screen Help Reference Type Versions - Screen Help Import Reference Type Version - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Adding a reference Type - How To Modifying a Reference Type - How To Removing a Reference Type - How To Importing a New Reference Type Version - How To Adding a Reference Type Version - How To Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To Importing an Existing Reference Type Version - How To

title: Removing Confidentiality from a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Removing Confidentiality from a Report 1. To remove confidentiality from a report, first open the report then click on the Confidential Reasons button ("confidential" may be customised at your site) on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Confidential Reasons window opens. 2. Click on the Clear button to remove all confidentiality data. 3. Click OK to update your report. Depending on how the system is configured for your organisation you will probably see the clauses you selected or the word 'Confidential' or 'Closed' disappear from your report. 4. Click on the Save button on the Infocouncil ribbon. This is the action that actually removes confidentiality from your report and returns it to being an open report. See Also Confidential Reasons

title: Resetting Item Numbers
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Resetting Item Numbers 1. To reset item numbers, click on the Functions ribbon menu and choose Next Item Number Allocation . The Next Item Number Allocation window opens. 2. Use the Find Year, Month, Cycle drop downs to select the period that you wish to edit. 3. Edit the Next Number that needs changing. 4. If you wish to reset all numbers to 1, click on the Reset All Numbers to 1 button. 5. Click Save then Close to exit the window.

title: Retaining Revisions and Restoring
text: Navigation: How-to > Retaining Revisions and Restoring You will be storing your Infocouncil reports and papers to either a file server, SharePoint or an EDRMS. Retaining Revisions EDRMSs and SharePoint allow file versions/revisions to be retained. If you store reports and papers in such locations you will need to go into those platforms to turn on version/revision retention and configure. Infocouncil has no control or interface to do so. For EDRMSs Infocouncil has a system parameter "Force Revision On Save" which forces a revision to be written each save (reports and papers), instead of the first save and each time the document is closed. For EDRMSs Infocouncil has a system parameter "Force Revision On Authorisation" which forces a revision to be written when reports or papers are authorised. Where reports and papers are stored on a file server, Infocouncil has a system parameter "Retain Revisions For File Based Councils" that allows a basic form of revision retention. Set this to "1" or "2" to retain revisions of reports only, not papers. · When set to "1", Infocouncil will retain only a copy of the report on return from the Create Report screen. · When set to "2", Infocouncil will retain a copy each time the report is saved by the user. For both options, Infocouncil creates a folder at the same location where the report is saved. Report writers must have access to create folders at the report save locations (including confidential locations). The folder will be named after the report's id, which can be viewed from the Document Menu or Report Detail screens. If the system parameter "Retain Revisions For File Based Councils" is set to "1" or "2" and you are storing your documents in SharePoint, then Infocouncil will retain revisions of reports as described above, independently of any SharePoint versioning. Under normal circumstances Infocouncil should not be configured this way. Restoring a Revision/Version Whether restoring a document from an EDRMS, SharePoint or a file server, there are two steps to the process. Step 1 For EDRMSs and SharePoint, use those platforms to restore the file of the revision you are after. For file server, locate the folder where the revisions are being stored in File Explorer. Choose your revision. Copy it into the folder above, renaming it to match the current report's file name, ensuring the current file is deleted (or you can move it into the revision folder). Step 2 Open the document with Infocouncil. Make a simple change like adding and removing a space and click save. For reports, if any attachments or attachments information has changed in anyway between the versions, prior to saving, go into the Attachments screen and make a little change (add a space to an attachment title), click Save to exit the attachments screen, then save the report. This is necessary in order for Infocouncil to synchronise data in the document with data in the database. Failure to do step 2 can result in 1. inconsistent data appearing o

title: Retrieving an Infocouncil Document
text: Navigation: How-to > Retrieving an Infocouncil Document Simple Search for Reports or Papers Very often a simple search will suffice. This search is excellent for finding documents for current meetings. 1. Click on the Document Menu button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Document Menu window opens. 2. Choose the Document Type you wish to find - whether Reports or Papers . 3. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . The grid in the middle of the window will refresh itself to display the results of your search. 4. Double-click on the document you wish to open, or select it and use the buttons along the bottom of the window to perform whichever task you want. Advanced Search for Reports Many more options become available if you use the Advanced search options when searching for reports. 1. Click on the Document Menu button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Document Menu window will open. 2. Choose a Document Type of Reports . 3. Click on the Advanced button to display the extra search criteria. 4. Select criteria as appropriate to find the document you are looking for. 5. Click on the Search button to populate (or refresh) the grid in the middle of the window with the results of your search. 6. Double-click on the document you wish to open, or select it and use the buttons along the bottom of the window to perform whichever task you want. See Also List Reports for a Meeting Document Menu Report Detail

title: Returning a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Returning a Report 1. When you receive an email requesting that you authorise a report, click on the Open Report link in the email. The report opens. 2. Review the report and its attachments to determine whether to reject/return (or authorise) the report. At this stage you have edit rights to the report so you could fix a small typing error. But if you make any significant changes then you should add your name as an author (using the Report Data button) or at least inform the original author of the changes you have made to their report. 3. Click on the black arrow under the Authorise ribbon button and choose Return (or Authorise). The Create Mail form opens, already addressed to the author and pre-populated with most of the information about your report. 4. Enter an Optional Note to tell the author why you are returning their report and the changes you require them to make. 5. Click on the OK button to send the email. 6. Wait while the report is automatically saved and closed. You will lose your rights to edit it and the author will have their edit rights reinstated. 7. You should expect another email again asking that you authorise the report because once the author has made changes to the report they must put it through the authorisation process again. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Authorising a Report - How To

title: Returning Online with Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes Offline and Online > Returning Online with Minutes 1. To return online with minutes, c lick on the black arrow beside the Functions ribbon button and choose Remote Minutes . The Remote Minutes window opens, but only the bottom part of the window you saw when you took minutes offline is displayed. 2. Click on the Online button to return all documents back online. You will be asked for confirmation before Infocouncil returns the document(s) online. You are not offered a choice of documents to take back online . 3. When returning the documents is complete, you will receive a message confirming your system is now ready to return online. You must close Word and reopen in order to continue. Note Any documents which were overridden by an administrator while offline (see next section) will be ignored and left on your laptop. See Overriding Offline Minutes . See Also Remote Minutes - Concept Going Remote with Minutes - How To Overriding Offline Minutes - How To

title: Re-Uploading a Minutes Document or Part Thereof
text: Navigation: How-to > Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes > Re-Uploading a Minutes Document or Part Thereof Note that re-uploading any minute items, deletes the old minute items from the Infocouncil database and more importantly, any action sheets related to them. If the action sheets have been worked with at all by the action officer (or anyone else) those changes are lost. 1. To upload a minutes document, click on the Generate Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Generate Actions window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting , Meeting Date and Minutes Type . 3. If the minutes has never been uploaded before then follow the steps in Generating Action Sheets . 4. To re-upload the minutes, click the Upload button 5. To upload just those items that have been added or changed (or remove those that have been deleted) in the minutes, click Upload All Changed Items from the Upload menu button. 6. To upload just those items that have been added to the minutes, click Upload Any New Items from the Upload menu button. 7. To re-upload selected minute items a. Select the minute items you wish to re-upload by checking the check box in the row of the items, or b. Click the Select button to open the Select Minute Items window and enter criteria by which to automatically select minute items after pressing OK on this window. c. Click Upload Selected Items from the Upload menu button 8. To re-upload selected minute items but only if they have changed in the minutes document a. Select the minute items you wish to re-upload by checking the check box in the row of the items, or b. Click the Select button to open the Select Minute Items window and enter criteria by which to automatically select minute items after pressing OK on this window. c. Click Upload Selected Changed Items from the Upload menu button 9. To delete just those items that have been removed from the minutes click Delete Items No Longer In Minutes from the Upload menu button. 10. To delete selected minute items a. Select the minute items you wish to delete by checking the check box in the row of the items, or b. Click the Select button to open the Select Minute Items window and enter criteria by which to automatically select minute items after pressing OK on this window. c. Click Delete Selected Items from the Upload menu button 11. Wait while Infocouncil completes the chosen task. This may involve opening up the Minutes document, uploading or re-uploading minute items to the Infocouncil database and generating or re-generating any action sheets pre-configured to be generated for the minute items. 12. A message box will appear notifying the number of minute items affected, click OK . 13. The list of minute items will be refreshed according to the chosen task. See Also Generating Actions Select Minute Items Generating Action Sheets - How To Configuring What Action Sheets Are Generated - Concept

title: Revising the Target Date for an Action
text: Navigation: How-to > Monitoring Action Sheets > Revising the Target Date for an Action 1. To revise the target date for an action, click on the Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Actions window opens. 2. Enter search criteria as desired to locate the action. 3. Select the action whose target date you wish to revise then click on the Revise Target Date button. The Revise Target Date form opens. Button only appears if you have rights to change a target date . 4. Choose a revised Date . 5. Enter a Reason for revising the date . 6. Click on the Continue button. The revised date and reason will be recorded in the list of Action Notes for that action. 7. Click on the Close button to close the Actions window. See Also Actions Adding Notes to an Action Sheet - How to Allocating an Action to Another Officer - How to Completing/Acknowledging an Action - How to

title: Sending a Report for Authorisation
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Sending a Report for Authorisation 1. Once you have completely finished with your report, and with it still open, click on the Authorise button on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Notify Report Authorisers form opens. 2. Select the first authoriser with a status of To Be Notified . 3. Click on the Notify button. The Create Mail form opens, already addressed to the authoriser and pre-populated with most of the information about your report. 4. Enter a Note , if you wish. 5. Click on the OK button to send the email. 6. Click on the Close button to close the Notify Report Authorisers form. Your report will be automatically closed, and you will lose your rights to edit it. However, if the authoriser rejects it then you will be notified by email and your edit rights will be reinstated. 7. Whether the authoriser authorises or rejects your report, you will receive an email informing you of their decision. Notes Once an Authoriser has been Notified of an authorisation request, the report will be made read only except for: · The Authoriser · Infocouncil Administrators · Divisional Users where rights have been granted so Divisional Users can Authorise or Return reports on behalf of the Authoriser when the Divisional User belongs to the same Division as the Authoriser. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Authorising a Report - How to Returning or Rejecting a Report - How to

title: Setting Up Cover Sheets
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Setting Up Cover Sheets This piece of functionality works with the Subscribers functionality so before setting up coversheets you should ensure the subscribers lists are up to date. 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates . Choose the meeting you wish to set up a coversheet for then click the Create Coversheet button. The Coversheet Setup window opens. 2. Click on the Browse button if you want to include an image. 3. Either leave the All Subscriber Types box checked, or uncheck it and choose a Subscriber Type . 4. If you chose to include a picture, enter a Image Caption for it. 5. Enter Coversheet Text . 6. Click on the Save button to save the Coversheet you have set up. 7. Click on the Preview button to preview the cover sheet. 8. Click on the Create Coversheets button to create a single Word document containing all the coversheets.
