From here you can print the coversheets. 9. Click on the Close button to exit the window then click the Close button to close the Meeting Dates screen.

title: Setting up Security
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Setting up Security <Still to be written> The security model can be modified by each council. Security Maintenance consists of two screens, Security Group Maintenance and Security Function Maintenance. See Also User Security - Concept

title: Setting Up Subscriber Notifications
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Setting Up Subscriber Notifications Subscribers are individuals or organisations external to the organisation that are interested in the business occurring in meetings. Subscribers may wish to be notified when an agenda or minutes is available to the public. Infocouncil allows subscribers to be notified of their availability and to receive links to these papers immediately after they have been published. The Publish screen provides a mechanism to automatically email subscribers following a publishing operation. Notifications will only occur for papers published to either the primary or secondary FTP locations. For this to work there are some tasks that need to be undertaken first. 1. Create the subscriber list. a. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Subscribers to open the Subscribers Maintenance Form. b. Add each subscriber making sure an email address is captured and that it is subscribing to one or more meetings. When a meeting's papers are published only subscribers that are subscribed to that meeting will be notified. c. Each subscriber must have a subscriber type which is just a label to categorise subscribers (as defined by your organisation). Subscriber types are defined by clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Subscribers Types to open the Subscriber Types Maintenance Form. 2. Make sure the relevant system parameters are set. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose System Parameters to open the System Parameters Form, expand the "Publication - Destination" node. a. Make sure the "Allow Automatic Email Notifications To Subscribers When Publishing" system parameter is set to True. b. Make sure either one or both of the "Primary FTP Public URL" and the "Secondary FTP Public URL" have a valid setting that maps to their corresponding "Primary FTP Publish Server" or "Secondary FTP Publish Server" respectively (the later being the address accessible from with the organisation, the former being the address accessible to the public). 3. On the Publish screen check the "Allow" subscriber notifications check box. Now when papers are published to the primary or secondary FTP locations, any subscribers to the meetings of those papers will be emailed to notify them of the paper being available and a link directly to the paper. See Also Subscribers - Screen Help Subscriber Types Maintenance - Screen Help Publish - Screen Help Modifying the Email Templates - How To

title: Set Up and Configuration
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration The setting up of Infocouncil is a significant and complex task. Infocouncil will be installed fully configured to your requirements. Some configuration changes may be achieved by the organisation. The topics under this topic cover some of these tasks. If in doubt however, call Infocouncil Support for assistance.

title: Specifying a Parameter Format
text: Navigation: How-to > Specifying a Parameter Format A number of System Parameters and EDRMS Parameters describe formats of titles, email subjects and other things. These formats include format specifiers which are one or more characters preceded by the % character. These are replaced by certain information such as a meeting date, whether confidential, etc. The list of possible specifiers is rich but limited by what the parameter relates to. For example, the Report Authorisation Email Subject Format system parameter includes format specifiers that relate to a report or to the meeting that the report is for. It does not include any that relate to a paper or an action because the parameter does not relate to those entities. A format can consist of any number of format specifiers as well as other desired text. For example "Title: %m - %md (%c)", where %m is the meeting name, %md is the meeting date and %c is the confidentiality text, transforms to "Title: Ordinary Council - 1st January 2021 (Closed)". Note that where a format specifier has no value nothing will appear and the format specifier will be removed. Infocouncil applies some intelligence when transforming: to remove empty round or square brackets; to reduce two consecutive hyphens or underscores with a space between them to one hyphen or underscore; to reduce two spaces to a single space; and to trim any leading or trailing spaces. For example " Ordinary Council () - - 1st January 2021 [] " would end up being "Ordinary Council - 1st January 2021" The following table lists all format specifiers and what they relate to. Format Specifier Description Relates To %a attachments - the term for "attachments included" if attachments are included in the paper Paper %ab attachments (True or False) Paper %ac attachment's code or position (1, 2, etc) Attachment %afaab action's first author's abbreviation Action %afabr action's first author's branch Action %afadi action's first author's division Action %afaem action's first author's email address Action %afafi action's first author's first name Action %afafl action's first author's first then last name Action %afala action's first author's last name Action %afalf action's first author's last then first name Action %afati action's first author's title Action %al attachment's code or position as alpha (A, B, etc) Attachment %c confidential - the term for "confidential" if the attachment, paper or report is confidential Attachment, Paper, Report %cb confidential (True or False) Attachment, Paper, Report %cr confidentiality reasons Attachment, Report %crd confidentiality review date Attachment, Report %d meeting date formatted as 1 January 2021 Meeting %d(date format) meeting date with specific format e.g. %d(d/M/yy). See standard date format strings and custom date format strings for valid date formats. No quotes are required within the brackets. Meeting %e item number Report %ea action's item number Action %f filename Paper, Report %fe filename extension Pap

title: Synchronising Authors
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Authors > Synchronising Authors Infocouncil will need to be configured by Infocouncil Helpdesk prior to this feature being available. The processes for synchronising Authors is to address a requirement of being able to keep Infocouncil in synch with an external system. Infocouncil allows synchronisation of Author information with Active Directory. Alternatively the Author information can be in a spreadsheet in which case you will need to run an extract of authors from your external system and get the extract into Excel format. The following topics are explained below: · The Authors' Extract File Format · The Authors' Synchronisation Process The Authors' Extract File Format The following table shows fields which can be synchronised but what is achievable depends on the source data. Field In Order Mandatory Description Login Id Yes First Namer Yes Surname Yes Title Yes Job title Phone No Email Address No Division Yes The name of the division the author is in. It must match the spelling in Infocouncil Abbreviation No Usually the first and last name initials but up to council how they use it. If not provided, it will default to the initial of first and last name together. Security Level No Active No Defaults to true. Is Councillor No Defaults to false. SMTP Server No When user's email service is different from the organisation, this identifies that service SMTP User Name No When user's email service is different from the organisation, this identifies user's login to that service SMTP Password No When user's email service is different from the organisation, this identifies user's password to that service EDRMS Login Id No EDRMS login if different from Login Id EDRMS Password No EDRMS password if EDRMS login is different from Login Id Signature File Path No Location of signature picture file Branch No Is Web Administrator No Default false Authorising Officer 1 No Login Id of first authorising officer Authorising Officer 2 No Login Id of second authorising officer Authorising Officer 3 No Login Id of third authorising officer Authorising Officer 4 No Login Id of fourth authorising officer The Authors' Synchronisation Process The authors synchronisation process first either directly talks to Active Directory or alternatively requests the location of the Excel file from which to synchronise. The Excel file will then be checked to see it is a valid file. Then the Authors Synchronise screen will be shown. The screen will display all records found in Active Directory or the file, plus any authors that are Active in Infocouncil and were not found in Active Directory or the file. The user can review the list then proceed to synchronise. The list shows all authors found in Active Directory (AD) or the Excel file. To identify an author that already exists in Infocouncil the author's login id will be compared. Where they match with a row in AD or the file then it will be treated as updating an authors d

title: Synchronising Members and Memberships
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Members > Synchronising Members and Memberships Infocouncil will need to be configured by Infocouncil Helpdesk prior to this feature being available. The processes for synchronising members & membership are to address a requirement of being able to keep Infocouncil in synch with an external system. A membership is a relationship between a member and a committee. In order for memberships to be able to be synchronised, members need to be synchronised. Committees also need to be synchronised but they will be synchronised through manual means. In order to synchronise these details with Infocouncil, you will need to run an extract of members and an extract of memberships from your external system. How and when they are run is irrelevant to Infocouncil but it would be sensible to run them reasonably close together so that there are no discrepancies. Within an hour would be reasonable, over a week would be unreasonable. Infocouncil can then be run to first synchronise with the members' extract file and secondly to synchronise with the memberships' extract file. There will be nothing to stop the administrator from running these synchronisations in the reverse order. Nor is there anything to stop them from being run at different times. For example, the members' synchronisation could be run today and the membership synchronisation next week. But for consistency it would be desirable to run them straight after one another. The following topics are explained below: · The Members' Extract File Format · The Members' Synchronisation Process · The Memberships' Extract File Format · The Memberships' Synchronisation Process The Members' Extract File Format The external file to be imported is to be in Excel format with one line per member providing the following data. Field In Order Mandatory Description Name Prefix No Text that may appear before the members name e.g Councillor or Mayor, Mr, Mrs, Ms, Dr First Name Yes Last Name Yes Initials Yes Usually 2 or 3 initials of the member. Name Suffix No Any text appearing after the name e.g. JP Active Yes Either True for an active member or False" for an inactive (retired/resigned/deceased) member. Email No Phone No Phone Alternate No This is not the mobile number but a second land line. It could be a second mobile if desired. Mobile No Fax No Default Membership Position No This can any of the following: · Title of Chairperson · Title of Deputy · Title of Member It will default to "Title of Member" if not provided. When a new committee membership is identified for a member their position will be defaulted based on this. Address Line 1 No Address Line 2 No Address Line 3 No Suburb No State No Postcode No Electorate No The Members' Synchronisation Process The members synchronisation process first requests the location of the Excel file from which to synchronise. The file will then be checked to see it is a valid file and then the Members Synchronise screen will b

title: Taking Minutes Offline and Online
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes Offline and Online Going remote, and returning and/or overriding, are processes that are only relevant for those organisations where meetings are held away from the organisation's network. · Going Remote with Minutes · Returning Online with Minutes · Overriding Offline Minutes

title: Taking Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes > Taking Minutes Preparation Before taking minutes of a meeting you must first create a minutes document . Many minute-takers do this in their office before they go to the meeting so they can add in any apologies, public input items, or similar that have been received since the agenda was created and distributed. Then, when they get to the meeting they only need to open the document they have already created. Steps 1. To take Minutes, click on the Document Menu button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Document Menu window opens. 2. Choose the Document Type of Papers . 3. Choose the Meeting and Meeting Date . The papers' list will refresh itself to display the results of your search. 4. Double-click on the minutes document you wish to open, or select it and click on the Open button. 5. Throughout the meeting, use the Minutes tools to view an outline of the items, resolve items, record a division or whatever other actions you require. Remember the Other ribbon menu because it also contains useful tools such as move item, block resolve and member names. 6. Save your minutes document regularly. Networks have been known to fail at the most inopportune moments! 7. After the meeting, check and tidy up your minutes. You will probably have extra paragraph marks that you wish to delete, but take particular care that you don't accidentally delete a bookmark. 8. When ready, you should get the minutes checked before publishing or distributing them. Some organisations expect you to send them through the formal authorisation process. Other organisations are happy for you to have them peer-reviewed. 9. If your organisation keeps a signed hard copy of the minutes as their official meeting record, now would be a good time to print the copy in preparation for taking it to the next meeting for signing. Rules for Editing Minutes Documents The Minutes document is a standard MS Word document that you may edit in exactly the same way as you might edit any Word Document. However, because it is necessary to maintain a specific structure within the document (so that Web documents can be created automatically), users editing the Minutes document must obey these rules: Bookmarks The system creates the minutes document with a number of bookmarks. For example, there is a bookmark at the beginning of each report and at the beginning of each recommendation. In addition, bookmarks are added during the creation of the document, for example, when a New Item is inserted. Do not delete any bookmarks . To prevent this, you should always work with the bookmarks turned on. They will be automatically turned on when the document is opened. Go to File > Options > Advanced , scroll to Show document content and check Show bookmarks . Moving or copying items If you wish to move or copy an item to another position in the document, use the Move Item tool on the Other ribbon menu because this will copy/move the entire item - including its bookmarks and form

title: Transferring Reports
text: Navigation: How-to > Transferring Reports 1. Click on the Functions ribbon menu and choose Transfer Reports . The Transfer Reports window opens. 2. Select the Source Meeting and Meeting Date . 3. Select the Destination Meeting and Meeting Date . 4. Check the box beside each report that you wish to transfer. Use the Include All or Exclude All buttons to help you select. 5. Click on the OK button. 6. Much twitching and flashing will occur as each report is opened, moved to the new meeting re-saved and closed. A green progress line will indicate the progress in the bottom left of the window. 7. When the process is complete a message saying Transfer Complete appears. Click OK to clear the message. See Also Shifting Reports Between Meetings - Concept

title: Unpublishing from the Web (Manual)
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Unpublishing from the Web (Manual) This applies to FTP publish destinations only. For WebAPI publish destinations please see Un-Publishing from the Web (via Infocouncil) . This task can be performed by authorised staff whose login has been configured to revoke the publication of papers. Preparation Before anyone can unpublish from the web, the following must be in place: 1. The Infocouncil website engine must be being used for presentation of published papers, and it must be a reasonably current release of the ASP.Net engine. 2. The UnPublish facility requires a shared password to be pre-configured for each council. If this is not already configured for your council, please ask the help desk to arrange this for you. The Infocouncil help desk does not keep copies of this password on file. 3. Appropriate staff must have been configured with website login details. This comprises of the network login as defined on the first tab of the Authors maintenance form and the Web Password configured on the second tab (Web/EDRMS) of the Authors maintenance form. The data on this form is pushed to the website every time a paper is published. Steps 1. To unpublish from the web, browse to the UnPublish web page. The web address will be similar to http://staging.infocouncil.<your council>/unpublish.aspx 2. Log in with your network login and the password configured at 3 above: 3. Filter for and then check each paper that needs to be un-published: 4. Enter the shared UnPublish password in the box at the bottom of the page then click the UnPublish button: 5. The system will then move the selected papers to the UnPublish directory on the website and log everything that is happening and who authorised it. 6. Remember to click on the Logout button to log yourself off. See Also Creating an HTML Document - How To Creating a PDF Document - How To Publishing to the Web - How To Publishing to LG Hub - How To

title: Un-Publishing from the Web (via Infocouncil)
text: Navigation: How-to > Publishing Papers > Un-Publishing from the Web (via Infocouncil) This applies to WebAPI publish destinations only. For FTP publish destinations please see Un-Publishing from the Web (Manual) . 1. To un-publish an Infocouncil Business Paper from the web, click on the Publish ribbon menu and choose Un-Publish . The Un-Publish Business Papers . window opens. 2. Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, you may have several publishing WebAPI destinations. Select the publish destination. If you only have one WebAPI publish destination configured, it will be selected by default. Only WebAPI publish destinations can be un-published from. 3. Select the Year and Month of publication, and click on the Search button. This will fetch a list of published business papers from your chosen publish destination. 4. Select the published business papers, you wish to un-publish, by ticking the corresponding check boxes in the Select column. 5. Click on the Un-Publish button to un-publish the documents. The Progress log and Status field will show you progress as the files are un-published. See Also Creating an HTML Document - How To Creating a PDF Document - How To Unpublishing from the Web - How To Publishing to LG Hub - How To

title: Generating Action Sheets
text: Navigation: How-to > Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes > Generating Action Sheets 1. To upload a minutes document, click on the Generate Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Generate Actions window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting , Meeting Date and Minutes Types . 3. Click the Upload button. 4. Wait while Infocouncil opens up the Minutes document, uploads its minute item to the Infocouncil database and generates any action sheets pre-configured to be generated for the minute items. 5. A message box will appear notifying the number of minute items uploaded, click OK . 6. The list of minute items uploaded will be displayed. 7. All items will be selected (their check box will be checked) ready to Print, Email or Commit (to an EDRMS). Notes · Action sheets will only be generated for minute item types that the system has been configured to generate. See the concept topic Configuring What Action Sheets Are Generated . · Action sheets will not be generated for minute items that have been flagged to prevent actions generation. This can occur if the system parameter Papers - Show Prevent Actions Generation Minutes Tool is set to True, and the minute taker used the Prevent Actions Generation Tool to prevent an item automatically generating actions. See Also Generate Actions Re-Uploading a Minutes Document or Part Thereof - How To Configuring What Action Sheets Are Generated - Concept Prevent Actions Generation Tool - Screen Help Releasing Actions - Concept

title: Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes Select a task: · Generating Action Sheets (and Uploading Minutes) · Adding Additional Action Sheets · Releasing, Printing, Emailing or Committing Action Sheets · Working With an Individual Action Sheet · Re-Uploading a Minutes Document or Part Thereof

title: Use Roles With HPCM
text: Navigation: How-to > Set Up and Configuration > Use Roles With HPCM Roles can also be used with HP Records Manager/Content Manager integration enabled (not TRIM or any other EDRMS integration). In this case, a role refers to the name of either a Position or Group location in RM/CM. This allows for one or more groups of authors to be granted access rights in RM/CM with very few actual access control entries being created against each document. This improves performance greatly for larger organisations and alleviates the 64 entry restriction introduced by HP CM 9.3.2. 1. Click on Administrator - Maintenance - System Maintenance in the ribbon. Under EDRMS set the system parameter, Set Record Level Security, to True. 2. Click on Administrator - Maintenance - System Maintenance in the ribbon. Under System set the system parameter, Use Role Based Confidential Folders, to True. 3. Restart Word\Infocouncil to enable Roles to appear in the Administrator - Maintenance menu of the ribbon. 4. Click on Administrator - Maintenance - Roles in the ribbon to enter Role Maintenance. 5. Enter roles to match what you wish to use, as defined in HP CM. For example, set the Name and Folder to match the name of the Position or Group location that it relates to in CM. The Folder must match. The Name can be anything you wish. 6. Enter an additional role which will be the default role. When Roles are in use in Infocouncil, every author must be identified to a role, even if no specific role exists in CM. The default role must have a Folder with "." in it. You might give it a Name of "Default" or "None", whatever you wish. 7. Now enter Author Maintenance by exiting Roles and clicking Administrator - Maintenance - Authors in the ribbon. 8. For each author, update their Role field. The Role field is a drop down showing the Name of each role as you defined it in Role Maintenance. Remember that for those authors with no corresponding role in CM, set it to the Default role. The system is now configured to use roles with CM See Also Roles - Screen Help Authors - Screen Help System Parameters - Screen Help

title: Viewing A Meeting
text: Navigation: How-to > Administering Infocouncil > Maintaining Meetings > Viewing A Meeting 1. To view a meeting, click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meetings . The Meeting window opens. 2. Locate the meeting you are interested in by searching all meetings or doing a partial name search. 3. View the details for the meeting on any of the six tabs. 4. Click on the Close button to exit the window. See Also Editing a Meeting Cloning a Meeting Creating a Joint Committee

title: Maintaining Meeting Speakers
text: Navigation: How-to > Maintaining Meeting Speakers 1. Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Meeting Dates to open the Meeting Dates screen. On the Main Menu , click on the Meetings link to open a Meetings sub-menu , then click on the Maintain Who Will Address a Meeting link. The Meeting Speakers window opens. 2. Search for the meeting that you wish to maintain speakers for and select that meeting. 3. Click the Speakers button to open the Meeting Speakers window. 4. List all speakers or search for a particular speaker to see who is listed to speak at a meeting. Or add a new speaker. 5. Fill in the details of the person who will address the meeting and choose the name of the report they will be speaking about. 6. Use the radio (round) buttons to indicate whether they will be speaking about the Recommendation or a Development , and whether they wish to speak For or Against the topic. 7. Click on the Save button to save their details. 8. Click on the New button to add another speaker. 9. If you have listed all speakers then you can use the Up or Down buttons to re-order the list to match their speaking order. 10. If you wish to delete someone from the list, select them then click on the Delete button. 11. Click on the Close button to exit the window.

title: Working With an Individual Action Sheet
text: Navigation: How-to > Generating Action Sheets and Uploading Minutes > Working With an Individual Action Sheet 1. To delete or edit an action sheet for a minute item, or to print, email or commit (to an EDRMS) an individual action sheet (including emailing or committing an action sheet previously emailed or committed), click on the Generate Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Generate Actions window opens. 1. Choose the Meeting , Meeting Date and Minutes Type of the minutes document containing the minute item. 2. If the minutes document has not yet been uploaded , do this before proceeding. 3. A list of minute items will appear. 4. Select an item then click the Action Sheets button. The Minute Item window for the selected item opens to the Action Sheets tab. Double clicking opens the same window but to the Details tab. 2. To delete an action sheet, a. Click to highlight the action to be deleted. b. Click the Delete button under the actions list, (not the Delete button under the list of notes). c. Click the Save button. 3. To edit an action sheet a. Click to highlight the action to be edited. b. Edit the desired field(s) with the highlighted action. Note the following once the action sheet has been emailed or committed (to an EDRMS): i. The action officer should be changed via the Actions form, Select the New Officer button. Changing it here will result in no record of who changed the officer nor provide the opportunity to record a note for why it was changed. ii. The target date should be changed via the Actions form, Revise the Target Date button. Changing it here will result in no record of who changed the date nor provide the opportunity to record a note for why it was changed. c. To add, edit or delete the action's notes i. The highlighted action's notes appear in the notes list below the actions list. ii. Click the Add button under the notes list to add a new note, the Action Note window opens. Add the note and click OK . iii. Click to highlight the note, then click the Edit button under the notes list to edit an existing new note, the Action Note window opens. Edit the note and click OK . iv. Click to highlight the note, then click the Delete button to delete the note. d. Click the Save button. 4. To print, email or commit (to an EDRMS) the action (it is not possible to re-commit an action already committed from the Upload Minutes screen, but it is possible here) a. Click to highlight the action. b. Select the appropriate task from the Process Action Sheet menu button. c. Wait for the process to complete. 5. For EDRMS interfacing organisations, clicking the EDRMS Record button displays the EDRMS record metadata in a new window, clicking the EDRMS Document button opens the action sheet in Word. See Also Generate Actions Minute Item Action Note Generating Action Sheets - How To

title: Working with Minutes
text: Navigation: How-to > Working with Minutes Working with Minutes is a process that is made up of several individual tasks: · Creating a Minutes Document · Taking Minutes Offline and Online - for organisations that take minutes away from the organisation's network · Taking Minutes o Attaching Tabled Documents to the Minutes

title: Writing a Report
text: Navigation: How-to > Authoring and Authorising Reports > Writing a Report 1. To create a new report, click on the Create Report button on the Infocouncil ribbon. The Report Data Entry window opens. 2. Choose the Meeting and Date to which you wish to put your report. 3. The Author (you) and your default Authorisers will appear. If required, use the drop down lists to add more names, use the up and down arrows to re-order them, and select a name then click the X button to delete a name. The system allows any author to be an authoriser. 4. Choose a Report Type . This determines the layout of the report you will write. Often it will default to something like 'Standard Report' because you have only one choice, but you may have several to choose from. 5. Choose an Agenda Section . This determines where your report will appear in the agenda. For instance, it would be inappropriate to have a standard report from an officer appear in the Notices of Motion section, so you will not be offered that choice! But depending on how the agenda sections are configured for your organisation you may be offered several sections to choose from. 6. Type a Title . This becomes the item title that appears in the Agenda and Minutes for the meeting. 7. Fill in other fields, as required by your organisation. You may also need to fill in fields on a second, third or fourth tab. For details of fields that may appear, see the Other Fields section in the Report Data Entry topic. 8. If you are running late with your report, do NOT check the Late Report / Urgent Business box. There is a whole process behind this check box. In most organisations the meeting administrator will be the only person who should use this check box. 9. Click OK . The Report Data Entry form will close and there will be a pause while the system populates metadata into the report template you chose. 10. The template opens with the cursor positioned at the first field ready for you to begin typing. Complete the report as you would any other Word document, but take note of the following guidelines: · Entering text into the Summary and Recommendations sections if entering a report summary or a recommendation, all text must be entered within the borders of the grey text box. Do not add text pertaining to the summary or recommendation outside the grey box. · Deleting section breaks do not delete any sections breaks that are present in the document when it is first loaded. The words Do not delete this line are present in the document to warn you that this is a line containing a section break. · Adding section breaks do not add any section breaks into the document. · Changing text in protected sections the first section and other sections containing attachment listings and signature blocks may be protected in your document. If you need to change data in these sections select Report Data from the Infocouncil ribbon. · Recommendations - Recommendation are entered directly into the report. A grey outline surrounds the

title: Screen Help
text: Navigation: »No topics above this level« Screen Help Screen help provides a topic for each screen as well as for the parts of the Infocouncil ribbon . It is organised based on the order of groupings and tools found in the Infocouncil ribbon. Screen help is a reference guide to: · identifying the purpose of the screen · identifying and describing each component on a screen · interacting with the screen Jump to the Infocouncil ribbon to locate the function or screen you want help on.

title: About Infocouncil
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > About Infocouncil Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the About Infocouncil button in the Main group on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the About Infocouncil form. You will probably only ever use this button if you are experiencing problems with Infocouncil, and either your organisation's technical support person or someone from the Infocouncil Help desk asks you to click on it. Under the Infocouncil logo, the window provides information about the Client Version and Database Version of Infocouncil that are installed. The Client Version is the application installed on your machine. The Database Version is the centrally located database that stores data from all users. Most of the time these versions match, but occasionally the Client Version may be higher than the Database Version. Module Displays the list of modules in the Infocouncil installation location and their versions. Verify Click the Verify button to display a list of technical data. Values to understand Some of the values that even the most "technically challenged" user should be able to check are listed below. Value Description Expected Database The version of the Infocouncil database that the system expected to find. Found Database The version of the Infocouncil database that the system actually found. For the system to work, these two need to be the same. Minimum Application Version The lowest Client Version of the application that will run against the Found Database. Recommended Application Version The highest Client Version of the application that will run against the Found Database. Actual Application Version The Client Version installed on the machine. The Actual version needs to be between (or equal to) the Minimum and Recommended versions. Environment User Name The User Name of the person currently logged on to the machine. Infocouncil Login The User Name of the person, as recorded in the Infocouncil Database. It should match the Environment User Name. Infocouncil User The Forename and Surname of the person, as recorded in the Infocouncil Database. User Email The Email address of the person, as recorded in the Infocouncil Database. Copy The Copy button appears after you have clicked on the Verify button. Click it to copy the list of information to the Clipboard. You can then paste the results into Word or Notepad to examine the list, or into an email to send to your organisation's Help Desk or Infocouncil's Help Desk. Log Level Offers a choice of entries to capture in the log file. This should be set to "0 - Always" at all times unless a support persons requests you to do otherwise. Items that can be logged within Infocouncil are flagged with a number from 0 to 9. If the logging level is set to 5 then all items flagged 5 or less will be logged. If the logging level is set to 9 then all items flagged 9 or less (i.e. everything) will be logged. The more logging that occurs, the more diagnostic information support can retrieve

title: Action Note
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > Minute Item > Action Note Clicking the Add or Edit buttons on the Action Sheets tab in the Minute Item form opens the Action Sheet Note form. Clicking the Add or Edit buttons on the User Action form through the Actions form opens the Action Sheet Note form. This form provides the means to enter a new action note or modify an existing one. Date Time The date and time that the note was first created. Not editable. EDRMS No Only visible for EDRMS based implementations. Enter an EDRMS number to associate with the note. EDRMS button Only visible for EDRMS based implementations. Click to open the EDRMS record for the EDRMS No entered. Search EDRMS button Only visible for EDRMS based implementations. Click to search and locate an EDRMS record Note The note text. Public note (comment) Click to flag note as public (available if the System Parameter-Actions-'Public Notes - Allow notes to be flagged as public' is True ). Note that the text can be changed (System Parameter-Actions-'Public Notes - Text for checkbox'). OK Click to accept any change and exit the screens. Undo Click to undo any changes. Note Click to cancel any changes and exit the screen.

title: Actions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking the Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Actions form. This form lists actions according to your search criteria so you can monitor, update and complete/acknowledge your actions. The action list will be automatically populated if the advanced options do not show on load of the form (a security right controls this). The form operates in two modes: · Basic Mode shows a basic set of search criteria. Clicking the button switches to Advanced Mode. · Advanced Mode shows additional search criteria available in 4 tabs. The user must click the search button to initiate a search. Clicking the button switches to Basic Mode. Search Criteria - Basic The following fields can be used to filter the list: Include · For Action - Include For Action type actions · Information Only - Include For Information type actions · Open - Include Open actions · Confidential - Include Confidential actions Status · Outstanding - Include outstanding (not completed) actions · Overdue - Include only overdue outstanding actions · Completed/Acknowledged - Include completed For Action actions and acknowledged Information Only actions Waiting for my approval Check this box to display actions waiting for my approval. This option will be displayed only if actions authorisations are being used. A delegate to an authoriser can also see the list. Search Click so the list of actions can be reloaded according to your selected search criteria. If this button is not visible then auto searching will occur each time a search criteria is changed. Clear Click to clear your selections and return to the default criteria. Click to expand or contract the search criteria available to you. If in basic mode clicking the button will switch to advance mode. Search Criteria - Advanced General Tab Meetings Select any number of meetings (committees) from the drop-down list. To remove a meeting select it then click on the X button. Click the Include inactive button to add inactive meetings to the list. Officers (disabled if no rights) Select any number of officers from the drop-down list. To remove an officer select it then click on the X button. Click the Include inactive button to add all inactive officers to the list. Divisions (disabled if no rights) - Note: This may be named differently depending in the naming convention used (e.g. Departments/Directorates/Groups) Select any number of divisions from the drop-down list. To remove a division select it then click on the X button. Title or Resolution (first 1000 characters) contains the text Search for actions that contain the this text in the title or resolution. The search is not case sensitive: Partial words may be searched for by enclosing text in double quotes. e.g: · "Events" - will return actions that contains 'event' or 'events' · Events - will return actions that contain specifically 'events' Multiple search phrases can be entered by separa

title: Actions Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > Actions Report Clicking the Print button on the Actions window opens the Action Report window. This window allows you to select a layout then an Actions Report in Word will be created. Report Layout Choose the layout of the report you wish to produce. Additional layouts may be configured. Standard layouts supplied at implementation are: · Further Reports · Item Number · Standard · Monitor Actions OK Click to produce the report. Cancel Click to exit without creating a report. See Also Modifying Action Report Templates

title: Action Sub Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Action Sub Types Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Action Sub Types opens the Action Sub Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit action sub types. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the action sub type name. List all Action Sub Types Select to view a list of all action sub types. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of action sub types matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new action sub type to be entered. Delete Select an action sub type in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the action sub type. NOTE : Prior to deleting the action sub type the system will perform a check to ensure that this action sub type is not referred to by any actions. The system includes a default action sub type called 'None' indicating that an action has no particular action sub type. The 'None' action sub type cannot be deleted. Details Name The name of the action sub type. The name must be unique. Active Indicates whether the action sub type is active or inactive.

title: Administration Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group If you have sufficient user rights, you will see some or all of the Administration Group on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Administration Group provides administrators with tools that can be used when creating and publishing papers. For those who have the rights there are also tools to help them with maintaining the system. For instance, authors, meeting dates, members and security functions. Tool Description Creates a new business paper including allowing the selection of reports for inclusion. Tips: · You ONLY need to click on the Create Paper button when you wish to create a paper. · To reopen a previously saved paper you should click on the Document Menu button in the Main group to locate it. See the Create Paper section. Publish : Publishes business papers in HTML or PDF format. See the Publish section. Create HTML Document : Creates an HTML version of a business paper. See the Publish section. Create PDF Document : Creates a PDF version of a business paper. See the Publish section. Un-Publish : Crejjjj See the Publish section. Generate Actions: Uploads items from a Minutes document and generate actions from them. See Generate Actions section. List Reports for a Meeting : Lists all reports for a particular meeting. It can include attachment and/or recommendation information. See the Meeting Timetable section. Minutes Index: Lists all minute items for a time period or for a cycle as one item per row.The Minute Number can appear as a link. See the Minutes Index Report section. Minutes Items for a Meeting: Lists minuted items for a meeting and should be run after the Action sheets for the meeting have been uploaded. See the List Minutes Items for a Meeting section. Meeting Timetable: Lists all meetings for a time period or for a cycle as one meeting per row. It can include deadline information. See the Meeting Timetable section. Yearly Meeting Schedule: Lists all meetings for a year or date range by meeting type. See the Yearly Meeting Schedule section. Meeting Events: Lists meeting events for none, one or more meetings all meetings, for any date range. It can filter by event type category or event type and also by member. See the Meeting Events section. Authors: Lists for view only, authors, their division, authorisers, security group and other details.. See the Authors Report section. Action Sub Types : Maintains action sub types. See the Action Sub Types section. Agenda Items : Maintains agenda items. See the Agenda Items section. Agenda Sections : Maintains agenda sections. See the Agenda Sections section. Authors : Maintains authors. See the Authors section. Confidential Acts : Maintains confidential acts ("confidential" may be customised at your site). See the Confidential Acts section. Confidential Reasons : Maintains confidential reasons ("confidential" may be customised at your site). See the Confidential Reasons section. Councils : Maintains councils. If configured to us

title: Agenda Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Agenda Group Whenever you open an Agenda, Infocouncil displays the Agenda group on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The same group of tools is displayed whenever you open an Attachments document or Minutes Attachment document. The group of tools are identical, but the name of the group changes. The Agenda Group provides administrators with tools that can be used when inspecting an Agenda. The document is a Microsoft Word document that may be edited in same way that any Word document may be edited. However, it is the intention of Infocouncil to create completed agendas, so little or no formatting should be required. or Tool Description Displays an outline of the items for a meeting. Select an item then go to its beginning or its recommendation, or print an outline of all the items for the meeting. See the Outline section. Notify Authorisers : Displays the list of authorisers for a report. Authorisers may be notified using this function. See the Notify Paper Authorisers section. Authorise : Allows an Authoriser to approve a report. See the Authorise or Return a Paper section. Return : Allows an Authoriser to return a report. See the Authorise or Return a Paper section. Save : Saves the report and commit details of the report to the database. See the Save section. Modify and Save : For EDRMS based councils. Allows the EDRMS to be updated with a different container, a memo/note or a new revision. See the Save section. Save As : Saves the report as another document (this will no longer be an Infocouncil document) See the Save section. Re-paginates the document based on the number of pages entered for each attachment. Used by councils who hand insert their attachments between reports in the Agenda. See the Page Number Reset section. This button is not visible by default. To make it visible, set the 'Show Page Number Reset Button' system parameter under Papers to True.

title: Agenda Items
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Agenda Items Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Agenda Items opens the Agenda Items window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit agenda items. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the agenda items name. List all Agenda Items Select to view a list of all agenda items. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of agenda items matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new agenda item to be entered. Delete Select an agenda item in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the agenda item. Up/Down The up and down buttons will only appear if ALL agenda items are being displayed. In this situation the agenda items are sorted in the order that they will appear in the drop downs throughout the system. Clicking the up and down buttons will shift the selected agenda item in that order. Details tab Agenda Item The name of the agenda item. Abbreviation The abbreviation of the agenda item. Can be used in such places as determining a file name for a report of this agenda item type. Display Name The user friendly name of the agenda item. Often this is the same as the Agenda Item field. Minutes Display Name The name of the agenda item as displayed in the Minutes document. Agenda Document Next Report Numbers Open Allows a number to be "seeded" from which each report created for this agenda item type will get the next number. This number will then increment accordingly. Closed Allows a number to be "seeded" from which each closed report created for this agenda item type will get the next number. This number will then increment accordingly. Late Allows a number to be "seeded" from which each late report created for this agenda item type will get the next number. This number will then increment accordingly. Report Type Divisional, Standard or Committee. Reports for a Standard agenda item type will end up in an agenda section based on what the user specifies in the Report Data form and as defaulted by the Meetings and Sections tab. Reports for a Divisional agenda item type will go to a divisional section based on the report author's division (divisional sections have to be set as well for this to work). Recommendation Section Identifies which section in the template utilised by this agenda item is the recommendation section. Summary Section Identifies which section in the template utilised by this agenda item is the summary section. Protected Sections Identifies which sections in the template utilised by this agenda item are protected sections. New Item Bookmark If this Agenda Item has the New Item field checked (on the Options tab) then new items of this agenda item type can be created. As they are created Infocouncil will place bookmarks into t

title: Agenda Sections
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Agenda Sections Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Agenda Sections opens the Agenda Sections window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit agenda sections. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the agenda sections name. List all Agenda Sections Select to view a list of all agenda sections. When all agenda sections are displayed sorting buttons are supplied next to the list to order items as required. This determines the sort order the sections will appear in the Agenda Section drop down on the Report Data Entry window, and in the Section column on the Document Menu window Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of agenda sections matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new agenda section to be entered. Delete Select an agenda section in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the agenda section. NOTE : Prior to deleting the agenda section the system will perform a check to ensure that this agenda section is not referred to in the Section Redirects tab. Details The Details Section contains three tabs showing all the details for a individual agenda section. Details tab The Details tab is illustrated above. Section A descriptive name for the Agenda Section. For example, Mayoral Minutes. Abbreviation A 2-3 character abbreviation for the agenda section. It must be unique. Header Display Same as Section Name. Report The name of the Report template. It will be a singular version of the Section Name. For example, Mayoral Minute. Type Describes the section type defining what reports can be submitted to this section. · Agenda Only Agenda type reports can submitted to this section. · Division Only Division type reports can submitted to this section. · Meeting Only Meeting type reports can submitted to this section. For example: · "Mayoral Minutes" and "Notices of Motion" are Agenda type reports. · A "Reports from General Manager" is a Division/Meeting type report. Division/Meeting Where the Division or Meeting Type is chosen, this allows selection of the Division or Meeting. Bookmark Name of Bookmark. Master Bookmark A unique code for the bookmark. It may not have any spaces. The Master Bookmark name should correspond to the area in the business paper where you want the section to appear (e.g. Master1). A good guide to follow here is to copy the Master number of a similar section/the section that you are copying. Starting Number This relates to the master bookmark field and can be referenced in scripts. Utility String This field may be used by Infocouncil staff for a variety of purposes. Section Redirects tab Confidential Name of the section where Confidential Reports will appear when submitted to this section. The drop down gives

title: Attachments
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Attachments When you have a report open, clicking on the Attachments button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Attachments form. Also, when in a minute item in a minutes document, clicking on the Attachments option under Other opens the same Attachments form. The Attachments form allows you to add, edit or delete attachments for a report or a minute item. Existing attachments are listed on the left and the details of the selected attachment, or the attachment being added, are on the right. Between the list and the details is a splitter bar which when dragged, enables you to adjust the space of each. List Up and Down arrow buttons Once you have added all your attachments, you can use the up and down buttons to re-order them. New Click on the New button to clear the details ready for a new attachment to be entered. Delete Select an attachment in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the attachment. Details File Location Click the folder button beside the File Location field to locate the file on a server or local disk , or if you are a SharePoint site and have the appropriate parameter set, the SharePoint browse dialog will be displayed. If your organisation is using an EDRMS system (e.g. Trim), a button will be available to search in the EDRMS. If it has not already been entered, the title of the EDRMS document will be automatically inserted in the Title box. If Infocouncil has been configured to automatically upload documents to the EDRMS on save of the report; the next time this item is viewed it will show the EDRMS Record ID (not the document path and name). Title Type a title for the attachment. It may (but need not be) the same as the file name. The title you type will appear in the attachments list in your report. Pages The number you type appears in the Attachments List in your report to inform readers about the size of the attachment. This option is only available for some organisations. Exclude Options There may be none, one or two options depending on your organisation's configuration. These options are useful if an attachment is very large, and may be better presented in a separate document. It would also help to reduce the size of the main agenda by excluding the attachment. To exclude the attachment from the main agenda and include it in a separate attachments document, check one of the exclude options boxes. The name of these options is also configurable by the organisation. Example names are 'Include only in attachment document' or 'Under separate cover'. Once excluded it is possible to produce business papers with just those excluded attachments from the Create Paper screen. PDF Conversion If you attach a PDF document then select the type of PDF document from the PDF Conversion drop-down list. Coloured files are many times larger than black and white or greyscale files, so use sparingly. Status When you f

title: Attendance
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Attendance When a minutes document is open, clicking the Attendance button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Attendance Form. The Attendance tool allows the minute taker to identify members of the meeting that are absent from the meeting. Members that are identified as absent are inactivated in all other Minutes tools that allow the minute taker to select Members from a list. For example, the Resolution tool, Division tool and Procedural Motion tool. They still appear in these lists, but their names are grayed out so cannot be selected and they are not included in results. Present members have no tick beside their name. Absent members have a tick beside their name and a reason for their absence recorded. Select Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. When you select the name of the absent member then a pop-up appears. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside any of the reasons. OK Saves the changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Authorise
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Authorise When you have either a report or a paper open, clicking the Authorise button, or clicking Notify Authorisers from the Authorise ribbon menu allows you to send a report or paper for authorisation, or authorise or return it. Depending on the processes within your organisation, reports may need to be authorised by one or more individuals before they can be included in a business paper. And in some organisations, papers need to be authorised before they are published and distributed. Infocouncil includes an electronic approval process which may be used to notify authorisers via email. Authorisers can then either approve the report, or return the report to the sender. Authorise a Report Authorise a Paper Notify Authorisers The Notify Authorisers option opens a form listing the Authorisers. From there you can choose an authoriser to notify. Authorise The Authorise option allows you to authorise the document and inform the person who sent the notification request that you have authorised it. If there are further Authorisers yet to be notified of an authorisation request, you will be asked if you wish to notify the next authoriser. Select 'Yes' so the next authoriser will receive an notification email. If integrated to an EDRMS and the system parameter Force Revision On Authorisation is True then a revision of the document is made. See Retaining Revisions and Restoring - How To. Return The Return option allows you to reject the document and return it unauthorised to the person who sent the authorisation request to you. See Also Notify Report Authorisers Authorise or Return a Report Notify Paper Authorisers Authorise or Return a Paper Retaining Revisions and Restoring - How To

title: Authorisation Notification Email
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Authorise > Authorisation Notification Email A notification email to authorise a report is generated when a report author notifies the first authoriser or when an authoriser finishes authorising a report and relays a notification to the next authoriser (if there are more authorisers). The notification email looks something like this: Subject This comprises two parts: · A request that you authorise the report. · The title of the report. Details/Body of Email This will be in two or three parts: · The first part will inform you of details of the meeting to which the report is being put, and may include a deadline date, if your organisation uses them. · There will be a link to facilitate opening the report that is the subject of the email. · There may be an optional note from the sender requesting that you pay particular attention to a specific part of the document to be authorised. For instance, if it has been difficult to write or the sender knows it is likely to be contentious or politically sensitive. Once you open and review the report you will have options to either authorise it or return it without authorising it. How to Approve a Report How to Return a Report

title: Authorise or Return a Paper
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Agenda Group > Authorise > Authorise or Return a Paper You will know when you you are being requested to authorise a paper because you will receive an email that looks something like this: Subject This comprises two parts: · A request that you authorise the paper. · The title of the paper. Details/Body of Email This will be in two or three parts: · The first part will inform you of details of the meeting to which it relates. · There will be a link to facilitate opening the paper. · There may be an optional note from the sender requesting that you pay particular attention to a specific part of the document to be authorised. For instance, if it has been difficult to write or the sender knows it is likely to be contentious or politically sensitive. Once you open and review the paper you will have options to either authorise it or return it without authorising it. How to Approve a Paper How to Return a Paper

title: Authors
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Authors Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Authors opens the Authors window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add and edit authors. The form has 4 parts: · Search Criteria across the top. · List of existing authors on the left. · Details of an individual author on the right. · Buttons across the bottom. Search Criteria Search by name Searches by any part of the author's name. List all Authors By default, lists all active authors . Show Inactive If checked, inactive authors are included in the list. Search Searches for authors matching the selected criteria . List Lists all authors matching the selected criteria. New Click on the New button to clear the details ready for a new author to be entered. Delete Select an author in the list above so their details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the author. You should ONLY delete an author if you accidentally enter an author twice or make some similar mistake. If an author has ever authored or authorised a document then instead of deleting them you should mark them as Inactive. Details This section of the window has 5 tabs and contains all the details about a single author: Details tab First Name Author's first name Last Name Author's last name Initials Author's initials Title Author's title Division Author's division/directorate Security Level Author's security level. The default options are: · Authors the author may view and edit their own unapproved reports · Divisional Users the author may view and edit unapproved reports from their own division · System Administrators the author may view and edit all reports. This should be reserved for administrators Note: It is possible to add more Security Groups if required or to change the names of the above groups. For more details about using Security Groups, contact Infocouncil who will supply you with a separate document. Email Address Author's email address Branch Author's branch. If branch details are used by the organisation. Login Id Author's network login Phone Author's phone number Role Author's role. Note: Field is only visible if system parameter Use Role Based Confidential Folders is on. Active Select if the Author is active/current. Member Select this option if the author is a member (councillor). An example of where this may be used is in the wording of automatic text when selecting authors of Notice of Motion type reports. Web/EDRMS tab View Closed on the Web Select this option if the author is able to view closed reports on the Web. Password Enter a password that the person must use to view closed items on the web. The person will be able to view closed items only after the next upload of a paper for viewing on the web. EDRMS Login Id Where using an EDRMS such as InfoXpert, where the login name is different to the domain login, enter it here. Clear EDRMS Password

title: Authors Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Authors Report Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and selecting Report Authors opens the Report Authors form. This form lists for view only, authors, their division, authorisers, security group and other details. The data may be copied to clipboard for paste into applications like Excel (use Excel's Paste | Text Import Wizard for best results) for analysis. List Displays details about the authors. The list can be customised but changes are not remembered next time you reopen this screen: · Drag the border between column headers to resize columns · Click on a column heading to re-sort the contents in ascending or descending order. A n up or down arrow will appear to indicate the sort. The columns available are. Columns Active Whether the author is active or inactive in Infocouncil. Login The windows login. FirstName First name. LastName Last name. Abbreviation Abbreviation. Title Title. Phone Phone. Email Email address DivisionId Infocouncil's identifier for the author's division. Division Name Division name. Branch Branch. IsCouncillor Is the author a councillor. AllowedToViewClosedOnWeb Indicates whether the author is allowed to view closed documents on the publish web site. EDRMS Login Login to an EDRMS (if integrated). Will be blank if the windows login is used instead. SharePoint Login Login to SharePoint (is integrated. Will be blank if the email address is used instead. SignatureFilePath The path to the file containing the author's signature image. LastUpdatedDate The date and time the author's record was last updated. RoleId Infocouncil's identifier for the author's role, if utilising roles. RoleName Role name. Authoriser1Id Infocouncil's identifier for the author's first authoriser. Authoriser1FirstName The author's first authoriser's first name. Authoriser1LastName The author's first authoriser's last name. Authoriser2Id Infocouncil's identifier for the author's second authoriser. Authoriser2FirstName The author's second authoriser's first name. Authoriser2LastName The author's second authoriser's last name. Authoriser3Id Infocouncil's identifier for the author's third authoriser. Authoriser3FirstName The author's third authoriser's first name. Authoriser3LastName The author's third authoriser's last name. Authoriser4Id Infocouncil's identifier for the author's fourth authoriser. Authoriser4FirstName The author's fourth authoriser's first name. Authoriser4LastName The author's fourth authoriser's last name. SecurityGroup Almost always, an author belongs to one security group. It is possible they belong to more than one. This will only ever show the first security group the author belongs to. Buttons & Other Copy Click this button to copy data from the grid to the clipboard as a tab delimited list, so you can paste to Excel or another application. Show Inactive By default only active authors are displayed. Checking this will refresh

title: Block Resolve
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Block Resolve When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Block Resolve opens the Block Resolve form. Block resolve allows for multiple items to be resolved at the one time. All unresolved items in the paper are displayed and any may be selected for a bulk resolution. Typically items would be selected across the paper where little or no discussion is required to speed up the business of passing their recommendations. Each of the selected items would then be automatically marked as resolved. However, there is an alternate usage for this tool. A small number of organisations wish to use the block resolution tool for a number of related items such as those from reports of committee. In this case they do not want each item marked as resolved. Instead they desire just the last item to be marked. There is a system parameter under Papers, called "Text In Last Block Resolved Item Only". Most organisations operate with this set to false. When true then the alternate usage will occur. Insert Minute Number / Resolve item fully The label is customised for your organisation in the "Text for check box on resolution screen" system parameters under Papers. Most commonly it is used to cause a minute number to be inserted with the resolution. It could be used for other purposes such as causing the item to be resolved fully. Alternatively, hit the space-bar. Mover Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Seconder Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Choose items to block resolve Select the items to be resolved by checking the box next to them. Include all Click to select all items. OK Closes the form and edits the recommendations in the Minutes document to change them into resolutions. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the form and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Clauses Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Clauses Group The Clauses Group is always visible on the Infocouncil ribbon tab, if it has been configured for your organisation. The Clauses Group provides all users with buttons to navigate within a report or paper and insert standard clauses wherever the cursor is positioned. Tool Description Opens the Standard Conditions window which allows the user to select one or more clauses for insertion at the cursor point in the active document. See the Load Clauses section. Moves the cursor forwards to the next item to edit. See the Next Item To Edit section. Moves the cursor backwards to the last item to edit. See the Return To Last Item section. Inserts a new manual clause at the cursor point. See the Insert Manual Clause section.

title: Clone a Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Clone a Report When you have a Report open, clicking Clone from the Move/Copy ribbon menu opens the Clone a Report form. The Clone a Report option allows you to put identical or almost-identical reports to multiple meetings (e.g. all Local Boards) or to a committee on a regular basis (e.g. monthly financial update report). You can do this any time before or after the original report is compiled into an agenda. The original report remains unchanged and in the original location. The cloned report is saved to the new meeting and can be edited as much as required. Meeting Select the name of the meeting to which you wish your report to be put. Meeting Date Select the date of the meeting to which you wish your report to be put. Report Type Select the report type you wish to use. Agenda Section Select the Agenda Section to which you wish your report to be put. Include Attachments Check if you wish the attachments to be cloned. OK Click to create the cloned report as a new Word document. Once created, you may then make changes to the document before saving it. Cancel Click to exit the window without cloning the report.

title: Confidential Acts
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Confidential Acts Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Confidential Acts opens the Confidential Acts window ("confidential" may be customised at your site) . This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit confidential acts. It will be set up by Infocouncil when the system is configured for your organisation and is never likely to need changing unless the relevant Act of Parliament changes. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the confidential Acts abbreviation. List all Confidential Acts Select to view a list of all confidential Acts. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of confidential Acts matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new confidential act to be entered. Delete Select a confidential Act in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the Act. Please note that the system will check if this Act is referred to by any confidential reasons. If it finds any then it will prevent the Act from being deleted. Details The Details Section contains two tabs showing all the details for a individual closed act. Details tab The Details tab is illustrated above. Abbreviation A short abbreviation or code for the confidential Act. This will display in the 'Act' drop down available from the Confidential Reasons screen. Summary A short summary of the Act if required. Text This field will be used by Infocouncil staff in script and included in reports and/or agendas as required. Reason Default Text Set by Infocouncil staff. Document Link The Confidential Reasons screen may show a Review Assistance button which will open up the document specified here. The document provides guidance on how to enter confidentiality against a report or attachment. The document is not authored or maintained by Infocouncil but by the council if they choose to use this feature. Browse Click to browse to a document on your network. Open Click to open the document in the default system viewer. Options tab The Options tab is shown below. These options dictate the behaviour of the Confidential Reasons screen as detailed below: Reason Check 'Visible' to make the Reason field visible. Check 'Required' to make it mandatory for a reason to be entered. Code Reason Check 'Visible' to make the Code Reason column visible. Check 'Required' to make it mandatory for a code reason to be entered. Confidential Review Date and Event Select the visibility and requirement choice from the drop-down list. Choices are: None The Date and Event fields will not be displayed Date Only / Not Required The Date field will be displayed but not required Date Only / Required The Date field will be displayed and will be required Event Only / Not Required The Event field will be displayed but not requ

title: Confidential Reasons
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Confidential Reasons Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Confidential Reasons opens the Confidential Reasons window ("confidential" may be customised at your site). This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit closed reasons. It will be set up by Infocouncil when the system is configured for your organisation and is never likely to need changing unless the relevant Act of Parliament changes. Search Criteria Search by Act Search by a particular Act to show all confidential reasons related to that Act. Please note that sorting buttons are provided to re-order reasons within an Act when searching by this method. List all Reasons Select to view a list of all confidential reasons. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of closed reasons matching your selection. List Up and Down arrow buttons When all reasons for an act is searched for and displayed, you can use the up and down buttons to re-order them. This will affect the order they appear in on the Confidential Reasons screen. New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new confidential reason to be entered. Delete Select an confidential reason in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the closed reason. Details The Details Section contains details for an individual confidential reason. Abbreviation A unique code for the confidential reason. Text The text to appear in the confidential reason text at the time it is inserted into a report. Summary The text to appear on the Confidential Reasons screen. Act The related closed Act. Section Enter the section relating to reason (usually 0).

title: Meeting Publish Destinations
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meetings > Meeting Publish Destinations Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meetings opens the Meetings window. Selecting a meeting, then clicking Publish Destinations opens the Meeting Publish Destinations window. This form provides the means for an administrator to make a publish destination valid or invalid for the meeting, and to override some configuration fields . The screen is a very similar interface to the Publish Destinations window because it presents the same data, together with any values overridden for the meeting. List The list automatically populates the set of publish destination for which the meeting is currently valid for. New Click on the New button to add another publish destination to the list of valid destinations for the meeting. If a meeting is valid for all defined destinations, then no new destinations will be able to be added to the list. When a new destination is added to the valid list, the Details section will populate with all the values that are visible on the Publish Destinations form for that destination. Some of the values will be overridable, allowing customisation of the destination for the meeting. Edit Click on the Edit button to override some field values, from how they were set in the Publish Destinations window for that destination, or to clear values overridden. Delete Select a publish destination in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to make the publish destination invalid for the meeting. The publish destination is not actually deleted. This can only be done from the Publish Destinations window. Details The Details Section contains two tabs showing all the details for a publish destination . When editing a publish destination · Fields whose values are greyed out, may not be altered. They may not be overridden and will show the value as entered on the Publish Destinations window. · Fields whose labels are in bold, have been overridden. Their value differs from the value as entered on the Publish Destinations window. · Fields whose labels are not bold and whose values are not greyed out, have not been overridden but may be. Their values are as entered on the Publish Destinations window. The screen shots of the Configuration tab for FTP and SharePoint below, illustrated these points. Details tab The Details tab is illustrated above. Name A name identifying the publish destination as entered on the Publish Destinations window . Not overridable. Description A description providing further detail about the publish destination as entered on the Publish Destinations window . Not overridable. Type The type identifying the type of destination as entered on the Publish Destinations window . The type dictates what fields are shown on the Configuration tab. Not overridable. Allows PDF If checked then PDF papers may be published to the

title: Confidential Reasons
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Confidential Reasons When you have a report open, clicking the Confidential Reasons button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Confidential Reasons form. Also, while adding an attachment, clicking the Confidential Reasons button opens the Confidential Reasons Form ("confidential" may be customised at your site).
