Confidentiality can be attributed to a report or an attachment with this window. Confidentiality can also be cleared. Act/Regulation Section Select a section from the Act/Regulation to populate the list of elements. When applying confidentiality at least one element must be selected and elements may only be selected for the one Act (not one from this Act and one from that Act). Note that organisations are subject to different Acts so the content in the Code and Summary lists vary, depending on the legislation that applies to your organisation. Elements List Check one or more elements that are applicable to the confidentiality. Having no elements selected is the same as marking it as not confidential. Hovering the mouse over an element shows a tool-tip with the full text of the element. Clicking a column header reorders the list by that column; clicking the same column header a second time reverses the order. Columns can be re-sized. Applicable Enables the checking or unchecking of an element. Code The clause or sub-clause in the section of the Act. Summary A summary of the element. Code Reason This is an editable field allowing justification to be entered for why this confidentiality element is applicable. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured it to be. It may be configured to be optional or compulsory to enter. Reason The justification for why confidentiality is being applied. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. It may be configured to be optional or compulsory to enter. Applies To Applicable only to report confidentiality (not attachment confidentiality). It indicates what the confidentiality applies to. It may be the report, its attachments, its resolutions or any combination of these. It cannot apply to nothing. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. Where it is not displayed, confidentiality applies to all components. Review Date It is the date upon which the confidentiality of the report will be reviewed to see whether it should still be confidential or not. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. It may also be configured such that at least one of the Review Date or the Review Event must be entered. Review Event It is an event upon which the confidentiality of the report will be reviewed to see whether it should still be confidential or not. For example, following the next general election. This will only be visible if your organisation has configured the selected Act to show it. It may also

title: Councils
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Councils This form defines a list of councils or organisations. Help is yet to be completed for this item.

title: Coversheet Setup
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Dates > Coversheet Setup Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Dates opens the Meeting Dates window. With a meeting date selected, click the Create Coversheet button from the bottom left of the window to open the Coversheet Setup window. Please note that the Create Coversheet button will only be visible if the system parameter Papers -> Use Coversheets is set to true. Also note that there are some preconditions that need to be satisfied before the screen will appear 1. The database table tblDocumentTypes must define a coversheet template. 2. A coversheet template must exists and be located with the other Infocouncil templates. 3. The Coversheet Path system parameter must be pointing to a valid location which will be where each meeting's coversheet is saved. 4. At least one subscriber must be defined for the meeting via the Subscribers Maintenance form. This form provides the means for an administrator to create coversheets before a meeting. This piece of functionality works with the Subscribers functionality so before setting up coversheets you should ensure the subscribers lists are up to date. Image Type the file path to an image, or... Browse for Image Click to browse to an image to insert. A thumbnail of the image will be displayed. View Click to view the selected image in the default system viewer. All Subscriber Types Checkbox Check to create coversheets that will be generated for all subscribers for the meeting, or.... Limit Print to Subscriber Type Uncheck 'All Subscriber Types' checkbox then select a type of subscriber to limit the coversheets generated. Image Caption Type an image caption. This will be inserted under the image. Coversheet Text Type or paste in other text to appear on the cover sheet. Buttons Undo Click to undo any changes you have made. Save Click to save the details you have entered. Saving will generate a preview of the coversheet. Preview Click to Preview the coversheet you have set up. Create Coversheets Click to create all the coversheets. They will appear as a Word document that you can print. Close Click to close the window.

title: Create HTML Document
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish > Create HTML Document This form will create an HTML version of a business paper (agenda, minutes or attachments documents) for publication from the Publish screen. The navigation Pane will be created automatically. The HTML document will be saved to the Publish directory (defined in System Parameters-Publication Generation-Publish Path). After Publication it will be deleted (by default) by the system. First open your agenda, minutes or attachments document in Word. Then click on the lower half of the Publish button on the ribbon menu and choose Create HTML Document to opens dialog asking if you wish to publish. Click Yes to initiate the process. When the process is completed the HTML version will be displayed in a browser on your computer. Quality check it to assure yourself it is correct and complete than close it. Infocouncil has already saved it to the Publish Business Papers list.

title: Create Mail
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Authorise > Notify Report Authorisers > Create Mail Infocouncil uses a Create Mail form in several places throughout the system. It will open whenever you use the system to send a message to other users. A Create Mail form opens when you wish to notify an authoriser that your report or paper is ready for them to authorise it. The same form opens if an administrator wishes to send a reminder email. When the form opens it is pre-populated with all the required information: · Recipient · Sender · Subject · Details that will appear in the body of the email. An Optional Note field allows you to add a comment or an instruction. Cc This button, if visible, allows cc recipients to be selected from a list of authors. Subject This comprises two parts: · The action you are requesting. · The title of the document. Details/Body of Email The pre-populated body of the email contains details of the meeting that the request relates to. It also contains a link that will open the document for the recipient. Note (None, Optional or Mandatory) If the You may wish to use the optional note to request that the authoriser pay particular attention to a specific part of the document to be authorised. For instance, if it has been difficult to write or you know it is likely to be contentious or politically sensitive. Refresh (F5) button If you write an Optional Note then clicking on the Refresh button, will cause your note to appear in the body of the email so you can see what it will look like in the email. But if you write an Optional Note and don't click on the Refresh button then the system will still include your note in the email as it sends it. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Modifying the Email Templates - How To

title: Create Paper
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Create Paper Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking the Create Paper button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Create Paper Form. This form provides the means for an administrator to initiate the process of creating agenda, minutes and attachments documents. Once initiated, the system takes over and creates the requested document automatically. Meeting This drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. New meetings are set up in another part of the system then they appear in this list. Meeting Date This drop-down lists meeting dates from 6 months ago and onward, for the meeting that you have selected. Many organisations populate this list with all the meeting dates for the year. If/when extraordinary or additional meetings are created in another part of the system then their dates appear in this list. Paper Type This drop-down lists the various types of documents that you can create: Agendas, Minutes or Attachments. The paper type selected controls what checkboxes are displayed to the right. A wide variety of documents can be created depending on the type and checkboxes selected. The names of the document types may vary slightly at your organisation and additional types, such as "Minutes - Attachments" or "Council Notice" may be present. Common checkboxes displayed for each paper type are: Agenda Confidential, Late/Supplementary, Include Attachments, Draft Minutes Confidential, Pro-Forma, Include Attachments Attachments Confidential, Late/Supplementary, Exclude Option 1, Exclude Option 2, Draft Checkbox descriptions: Confidential Check to create a confidential paper or uncheck for an open paper. Late/Supplementary Check to create a late or supplementary paper or uncheck for a "normal" paper. When checked a drop down button appears next to the checkbox. Clicking it displays the following pop up. The starting page number of the resulting paper can be modified here. By default all late reports will be selected for inclusion but any may be excluded here. Include Attachments Check to include attachments in your paper or uncheck to exclude attachments. Depending on how your system is configured, if checked, either images of the PDF attachment pages are created and included into the paper, or place holder pages for each PDF attachment page are included into the paper. Further, if and when the Word paper version is converted to PDF, the images or place holders may be swapped for the original PDF attachment page content. See the Infocouncil - Original PDF Attachments in Papers concept topic for more details on this. Draft Check to create a draft paper or uncheck for a "normal" paper. Specifics vary between organisations but the paper is likely to have: · 'DRAFT' stamped across each page · ALL items included, whether they are fully approved or not · Only the key features of each report included. For example, the title, author, executive summary and recommendation

title: Create PDF Document
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish > Create PDF Document This form will create a PDF version of a business paper (agenda, minutes or attachments documents) for publication from the Publish screen. The navigation Pane will be created automatically. The PDF document will be saved to the Publish directory (defined in System Parameters-Publication Generation-Publish Path). After Publication it will be deleted (by default) by the system. Prior to publication it could be copied from the Publish directory and used elsewhere. Creating a PDF version of a business paper converts the Word version exactly as it is with the one exception. If the system parameter PDF Attachments (1) - Insert original PDF attachments in the PDF version of papers is set to true, then any images or place holders of PDF attachment pages will be replaced with the original PDF attachment page content. See the Infocouncil - Original PDF Attachments in Papers concept topic for more details on this. First open your agenda, minutes or attachments document in Word. Then click on the lower half of the Publish button on the ribbon menu and choose Create PDF Document to open the Create PDF Version window. Infocouncil uses the in-built ability of Word to create the PDF version, and the ability of Infocouncil to create the navigation pane (outline). Convert with Word and outline with Infocouncil Use this conversion choice always, unless you experience a problem with it for a specific paper. It is the most reliable and robust and usually produces the best conversion and navigation pane. This choice should always be visible. If it is not please contact Infocouncil support. Convert with PDFMaker and outline with Infocouncil Only visible if Adobe Acrobat is installed and the PDFMaker add-in enabled in Word. Preoutline with Word and convert manually Always visible. For more information on conversion choices see the concept topic Paper Conversion to PDF Choices . Publish the original attachments (use for web browsers) Click to allow viewing of the original versions of attachments in PDF format. They will be available for viewing or download from the Outline (Bookmarks) list in the Navigation Pane of the PDF viewing application, and also, from the PDF button next to the attachment in the body of reports. They will open in another tab in the browser. This is generally used for publishing to the web. Embed the original attachments (use for LG Hub or similar applications) Click to embed original versions of attachments in the PDF document. They will be available for viewing or download from the Attachments list (e.g. the Paper Clip in Adobe Reader) in the Navigation Pane of the PDF viewing application. This is generally used for publishing to LG Hub (or other tablet based apps). Optimise for minimum size Click to optimise the PDF document for minimum size, making it more suitable for the web. The result will be smaller but possibly less legible. OK Click to in

title: Custom Help
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Custom Help Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Custom Help Links opens the Custom Help Links window. This window provides a mechanism for an administrator to link help that is authored by their organisation into Infocouncil. Custom help may be linked to none, some or all of the screens and also directly from the ribbon. However, not all Infocouncil screens are able to have help. There are still some older technology screens which are gradually being migrated to newer technology. Those older screens cannot have help links. Search by name Enter a part string to return all screens that match. For example, enter "maint to list Maintenance screens. Search Searches for all Help Links matching the string you entered in the Search by name field. List all Help Links Lists all help links. List Each point in the system where client authored help can be invoked from appears in the list. Help Links cannot be added to or deleted from the list. It is maintained by Infocouncil. Details Help Context Describes the point where the help will be invoked from. Help Link The location of the file containing the appropriate client authored help. Arguments Arguments can be provided where the help link is an executable. Note The Note field is not editable. It provides additional information about the help link wherever the link is not immediately intuitive. Buttons Test Link Attempts to open the file you have specified. Save Saves the client-authored help link you have entered. Undo Undoes any changes you made. Close Closes the window. Seeing the effects Where you have created a link to an Infocouncil screen, next time you open that screen you will see a button with a magenta coloured question mark at the bottom of the screen. Clicking this will open your help link. Where you have created a link to the Infocouncil ribbon, next time you restart Word you will see a button with a magenta coloured question mark in the Main Group of the Infocouncil ribbon. Clicking this will open your help link. Please see below note on the ribbon help links. See Also Custom Help - concept topic

title: Deadline Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Deadline Types Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Deadline Types opens the Deadline Types window. Also, when in the Meeting Types window, clicking on the Deadline Types button opens the Deadline Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to define the types of deadlines they wish to set for reports. A deadline type is just a label. When a deadline is being defined for a meeting type, first a deadline type is chosen, then it is defined for that particular meeting type. For instance, "Report Finalisation Date" may be defined as 3 days prior to a senior managers' meeting but 8 days prior to a council meeting. List Lists the deadline types that have already been created. Edit Click to edit the selected deadline type. New Click to create a new deadline type. Delete Click to delete the selected meeting type. Unlabeled field Name of deadline type being created or edited. Saves the new or edited deadline type. Deletes the new or edited deadline type. Close Closes the deadline types window. See Also Meeting Types Meeting Dates Deadline Dates

title: Defer/Refer a Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other > Defer/Refer a Report When you have a Report open, clicking Defer/Refer from the Other ribbon menu opens the Defer or Refer a Report window. The Defer or Refer a Report option allows a meeting administrator to defer or refer a report after the report has been put to the original meeting (i.e. agenda and minutes of original meeting have been completed). Deferring/Referring a report is used when the members of the original meeting resolve to defer/refer the report to another meeting. The item remains in the agenda and minutes, but the report itself moves to the new meeting. Meeting Select the name of the meeting to which you wish your report to be put. Meeting Date Select the date of the meeting to which you wish your report to be put. Report Type Select the report type you wish to use. Agenda Section Select the Agenda Section to which you wish your report to be put. OK Click to defer or refer the report to the new meeting. Cancel Click to exit the window without deferring or referring the report.

title: Define Grid Sort
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Document Menu > Define Grid Sort When you open the Document Menu form and have reports selected or you open the Actions form, clicking on the sort button located immediately above the left hand side of the grid, will open the Define Grid Sort form. The Define Grid Sort form allows you choose none, one or multiple columns to sort the data in the grid by. Add Level Displays a new row to define a sort criteria. Delete Level Deletes the current row and any sort criteria identified by it. Up Moves the current row up before its prior row, determining that the sort will be by the current row first then the prior row. Down Moves the current row down below its next row, determining that the sort will be by the next row first then the current row. Columns Column The column to sort by. Order Sort the column either in ascending or descending order Clear Clear all rows and sort criteria. OK Close this form and apply the sort criteria to the data in the grid on the calling form (the Document Menu or the Actions form). Cancel Click this button to close the form without applying or changing any sort order to the grid on the calling form (the Document Menu or the Actions form)

title: Divisions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Divisions Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Divisions opens the Division window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit divisions. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the divisions name. List all Divisions Select to view a list of all divisions. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of divisions matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new division to be entered. Delete Select a division in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the division. Details The Details Section contains all the details for a individual division. Division The unique name of the division. Abbreviation A unique abbreviation for the division (up to 5 letters). Division Head The name of the Divisional Head. Report Heading The heading to precede divisional reports in Business Papers. Bookmark The name of the bookmark used to place reports during Business Paper generation. Next Report No The next report number for the division (for councils using a report number based item numbering system).

title: Division
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Division When a minutes document is open, clicking the Division button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-3 ) opens the Insert Division Form. The Division tool allows the minute taker to record the results of divisions or votes held during the meeting. When OK is clicked, the division text will be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. A vote may be conducted manually or electronically (called an eVote). Manual voting involves the minute taker filling in this form as the members show their vote by a show of hands or by standing up. An eVote involves members using a device to cast their vote in such a way that this form is automatically filled in. When an eVote is completed there is always an opportunity for the minute taker to complete the vote manually if required. A vote could be conducted with some casting their votes electronically and some manually. All members of the meeting are listed. The Abstain vote check box will appear if the System Parameter - Papers - Abstain Visible For Divisions is set to True. Names of members who: · Are Absent, appear grayed and struck out . · Abstain, appear grayed out . · Vote For, appear in blue . · Vote Against, appear in red . · Have not yet voted, appear in black . The tally of votes appears along the bottom of the form. If a member was absent when the window was opened and they suddenly walk into the meeting then they may be included in the vote by un-ticking the Absent checkbox. Such a member will not be eligible to eVote straight away and will need to vote manually The red rectangle around the member's name indicates which member is able to have its vote cast via a simple key press: F1 to vote for, F2 for against and F3 for abstain. Once a vote is cast via this means the red rectangle advances to the next member allowing rapid vote recording with simple key clicks. Unanimous Click to mark all members as voting For the motion. When recording a division it is often quickest to use the Unanimous button to record all members as voting For then tick the Against or Abstain check boxes for those not wishing to vote For the motion. Unanimous For Click to mark all members as voting For the motion. Unanimous Against Click to mark all members as voting Against the motion. Remainder For Click to mark all members yet to vote as voting For the motion. Remainder Against Click to mark all members yet to vote as voting Against the motion. Unanimous For Click to advance the red rectangle to the next member without casting a vote. Upload All Changed Click to regress the red rectangle to the previous member without casting a vote. OK Closes the Division form and inserts the division in the Minutes document. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document. eVote Notes eVoting must be configured for your site before it may be used and an eVoting session must have been started via

title: Document Menu
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Document Menu Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking the Document Menu button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Document Menu Form. This form provides the means to retrieve Infocouncil documents. Search criteria are entered, matching documents are displayed and then a selected document may be opened or have details displayed for it. Entering search criteria will automatically display matching documents unless performing an advanced report search in which case you will need to click the Search button when you have completed entering the criteria. Document Type Use the radio buttons to view either a list of reports or a list of papers related to the meeting: the agenda, minutes, attachments documents, late/supplementary agenda, action sheets and more. Meeting The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. New meetings are set up in another part of the system then they appear in this list. A single meeting can be selected or select "<All meeting types...>" from the list to search across all meetings. Meeting Date The drop-down lists all meeting dates from the last 6 months onward for the meeting that you have selected. Older dates can be viewed by selecting "<More dates...>" from the list. Selecting "<Less Dates...>" will hide the older dates again. Selecting "<All documents...>" from the list will return all reports for the meeting selected. Selecting "<All meeting types...>" and "<All documents...>" might take some time depending on the number of reports in your system. Many organisations populate this list with all the meeting dates for the year. If/when extraordinary or additional meetings are created in another part of the system then their dates appear in this list. Inactive only If you tick this box then inactive meetings only will appear in the list. This is useful if, for instance, you are searching for historic documents that relate to a meeting that no longer meets and has therefore been made inactive. Advanced This is visible only for reports and if you click this button more criteria to search by appear. These are explained below . Refresh Click to refresh data (reports or papers) in the grid below. Sort This is visible only for reports and if you click this button the Define Grid Sort screen displays to allow control over how the report list is ordered. More complex sorting can be achieved via this screen or simple single column sorting can be achieved by clicking a column heading. Either method cancels any sort defined by the other. Where a multi column sort is defined, only the header of the first column defined in the sort will show an up or down arrow in it. Document List Displays details about the matching documents. Details differ for reports and papers. Double click on a document to open it or select a document and click the open button. Hovering the mouse over a column header or document detail will display the full text in a tooltip where

title: EDRMS Parameters
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > EDRMS Parameters Show/Hide Hidden Text Only available if configured to interface to an EDRMS. Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing EDRMS Parameters opens the EDRMS Parameters window. It can also be accessed from the Meeting Date and the Meeting (Committee) Maintenance forms. EDRMS Parameters control how Infocouncil works with an EDRMS. When shown from the Meeting Date or Meeting screens, only parameters for configuring the default locations where documents will be saved to in the EDRMS are displayed. When shown from the Maintenance ribbon menu, parameters other than those for configuring default locations are displayed. Some parameters describe formats of custom fields and other things. The Specifying a Parameter Format topic provides much detail on how to set these parameters. EDRMS Parameters from Maintenance Ribbon Menu Go: Administration Group > Maintenance > EDRMS Parameters. All the system-wide parameters for Actions, Custom Fields, Record Types and System Settings are defined here. The System Custom Fields grouping contains 20 Custom Field and Value parameters. These facilitate passing a specific piece of data from Infocouncil to the EDRMS, usually to populate some metadata in the EDRMS record that holds the Infocouncil document. To explain this further: when a new document is saved to an EDRMS, the EDRMS creates a record and associates the document to the record. Records contain metadata describing the document and how the document is classified and controlled. Metadata can include the obvious fields like Author and Security attributes. Also, all EDRMSs allow custom metadata to be defined - for example, meeting type, meeting date, closed or open, etc. So a custom field in our EDRMS parameters might be set up so that we can populate the Author and/or the Meeting type metadata in the EDRMS. In the below table, look at the Description column for the Value parameters. You will see various formats that can be entered in the value which tells Infocouncil - "For this document I want you to get this bit of data and push it through to the EDRMS". EDRMS Parameters from the Meeting (Committee) Maintenance Form Go: Administration Group > Maintenance > Meetings > EDRMS Parameters button. Infocouncil is set up so it knows where to save the reports and papers to: · In a file-based system this is done on the File Save Paths tab of the Meeting form. · For an EDRMS-based system, Infocouncil uses EDRMS parameters to determine where to save each Infocouncil document created. Each meeting (committee) can have specific locations set up for each document type, one for all confidential documents and one as a default for papers which do not have an explicit location set up. If a confidential location is not set up then the document will go to the location set up for its document type. The different options are shown in the below screen shot. The reason for the Curr

title: Email Report Link
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Email Report Link Email Report Link Email an officer with details of the active report. There may be a link in the email that opens the report in Word. This screen will presented: The subject of the email may be edited. Clicking OK will show the Create Mail screen where an optional note may be entered before sending the email.

title: Email Vote Link
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > eVoting > Email Vote Link When a minutes document is open, and an eVoting session is running , clicking on the eVoting ribbon menu and choosing Email Vote Link opens the Email Vote Link form. The Email Vote Link form allows the minute taker to send an email to any members with the link to the currently running eVoting session. This is useful if a member who was initially absent to the meeting arrives and needs to be able to participate in an already established eVoting session. It can also be used if a member accidentally deleted the original email they may have received when an eVoting session was initially started. to insert text such as conflicts of interest, code of conduct, or any other predefined text. Choose members... Click the radio button beside their names. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their names. OK Click to send the email to selected members and exit the form. A sample email is shown but it can be customised so may look different at your site. The link takes the member to a login page to participate in the eVoting. Cancel Click to exit without sending any emails. See Also Modifying the Email Template - How To eVoting Members Screens - Screen Help eVoting - Screen Help

title: eVoting Member Screens
text: Navigation: Screen Help > eVoting Member Screens Members at a meeting where Infocouncil eVoting is in use (not Dicentis eVoting) will interact with eVoting through a browser and will see a number of screens. The members interface to Dicentis eVoting is outside the scope of the Infocouncil Help. When the minute taker initiates an eVoting session, or when the minute taker uses the Email Vote Link tool a member can receive an email that provides a link to connect to the eVoting web server. The member will see the following screen: If a member signs in with the correct PIN, they will be redirected to the voting page as per the image below: If a member enters an incorrect PIN 3 times then they will become locked out. The only way to unlock a member is to regenerate the member's PIN from the Members Maintenance screen, communicate the new PIN to the member and then resend the invitation email to the member using the Email Vote Link tool under the eVoting button on the ribbon. The minute taker can initiate an eVote using the Division Tool . When an eVote commences, a voting members screen will change with details of the vote and controls allowing the member to cast their vote. The screen will resemble the below: The member can cast their vote by selecting 'For', 'Against' or 'Abstain' and can optionally enter a reason. The reason text box will only be displayed if the Allow Vote Reason system parameter (defined at either the system or meeting level) is configured to "True". When Cast has been clicked the text "A vote has commenced." will change to "Vote cast - awaiting acknowledgement...". All controls on the screen will become disabled. When the vote is received by the Infocouncil Division tool, an acknowledgement will be sent back to this screen and the text will change to "Your vote has been received.". The item title will always be displayed but the first 1000 characters of the resolution text will only be displayed if the Show Resolution system parameter (defined at either the system or meeting level) is configured to "True". If the Allow Recasting system parameter (defined at either the system or meeting level) is configured to allow recasting of a vote then the Cast button will change to Recast, the controls will become enabled again allowing the member to change their vote up until the eVote completes. When the eVote completes the screen will return to: Finally when the minute taker ends the eVoting session for the meeting, the member will see a screen telling them they have been logged out. No further voting may take place even if the minute taker starts up another eVoting session. In this case the user will need to click the link in the email that is generated to open a new browser window and login again. See Also eVoting Tool - Screen Help. Email Vote Link - Screen Help. Division Tool - Screen Help. Members Maintenance - Screen Help. Conducting eVoting - How To. Configuring eVoting - How To. eVoting - Concept.

title: eVoting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > eVoting When a minutes document is open, clicking the eVoting button on the Infocouncil ribbon initiates an eVoting session. The eVoting session allow divisions to held electronically by enabling member votes to be recorded via a device. Electronic voting functionality (or eVoting) allows for votes to be conducted within Infocouncil using either purpose built or generic devices. In terms of purpose built devices, Infocouncil has integrated with the Dicentis system developed by Bosch. Infocouncil has also developed a solution that is technology agnostic and can run on any device capable of reasonably rendering a web page in a browser. Hereafter these solutions will be known as Dicentis eVoting and Infocouncil eVoting respectively. If eVoting has been configured in your environment and is not currently active, then pressing this button will commence an eVoting session. The button will remain depressed until the eVoting session is terminated. This action does not start a vote, it simply prepares for votes to occur. If eVoting is active, pressing this button will terminate the eVoting session. When an eVoting session commences the user will be asked to confirm that they wish to start eVoting as follows: Clicking OK will complete the initialisation of the eVoting system. Initialising a session involves two main tasks: · the starting of a web server to facilitate eVoting; · the emailing of a link to the web server to members eligible to participate in eVoting. It is important to note that the list of eligible voters is established at this time. Those members who are marked as absent from the meeting or who do not have an email address will not be included in this list and will therefore be unable to participate in an electronic vote. If members are marked as absent when eVoting is started but are subsequently marked as in attendance they can participate in eVoting by either stopping and starting eVoting, or if Infocouncil eVoting is being used, use the Email Vote Link tool to explicitly send the link the web server to the relevant member(s). This tool appears under the eVoting ribbon button when eVoting is started. eVoting can now be conducted via the Division tool. When eVoting is no longer required the minute taker can again press the eVoting button again to close the eVoting session. The following confirmation will be displayed: Clicking OK on this dialog will close the eVoting session. If Infocouncil eVoting is in use the eVoting web server will be closed and any connected members will be logged out. If the minutes document is closed normally without the eVoting session being closed, Infocouncil will close it automatically. See Also Division - Screen Help. Email Vote Link - Screen Help. Conducting eVoting - How To. Configuring eVoting - How To.

title: Export
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Export Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Export opens the Export Facility window. This form provides the means for an administrator to export documents. The window gives you options as to what to export and where to export it to. What Reports Check to export reports. Papers Check to export papers. Attachments Check to export attachments associated with the reports identified in the 'For' filter. See Exporting Attachments below for more details. Paper Attachments Check to export attachments associated with the papers identified in the 'For' filter. See Exporting Attachments below for more details. Open Check to export open (public) documents. Closed Check to export closed (confidential) documents. For Specify whether to export documents for a month, a single meeting or for a custom selection of meeting dates. If A month is selected you will see Month and Year The month and year of the meetings to export documents from. If A Meeting is selected you will see Meeting The meeting (committee) to choose a meeting date from. Meeting Date The meeting date to export documents from. If Custom is selected you will see Meetings Select meetings (committees) to export documents from. Inactive Select to list active meetings instead of just active ones From Select to export documents from meetings with a meeting date greater or equal this date To Select to export documents from meetings with a meeting date less or equal this date Export Attachments To Only visible if Attachments has been chosen to export. Select where the attachments will be exported to. See Exporting Attachments below for more details. To Specify where to export documents to. If Document Export Path is selected Document Export Path This will be defaulted from the system parameter 'Export Export Path' and may be set to any path to provide an alternate export location or simply copy of a set of documents out from Infocouncil to somewhere else. Type a new path in or click the browse button to choose a new path. If Default File Save Paths is selected Documents will be exported to the paths set up for each meeting in the Meeting Maintenance screen in the File Save Paths tab. Potentially useful if migrating documents out of an EDRMS or if changing the file save paths and wanting the documents to move to the new paths. If Other EDRMS is selected (this option is only available for file based customers that have an EDRMS configured but are not integrated with the EDRMS. See Exporting to an EDRMS below for details.) Configure EDRMS Allows quick access to the System Parameters and also the only way to access the system / meeting / meeting date EDRMS Parameters. Exported documents will be placed into the default folders defined in the EDRMS parameters. Options Overwrite document if found at export destination Check if you wish to overwrite documents at the export location. Create export d

title: Finalise
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Finalise Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Finalise opens the Finalise window. This form provides the means for an administrator to finalise documents in an EDRMS. This function is not available for file based councils, only councils who use an EDRMS. This function will tell the EDRMS to finalise or complete the set of documents defined by the forms fields. What documents to Finalise Choose whether to finalise reports, papers or both. The default is both. With Confidential Status Choose whether to finalise documents with a confidential status of open, closed or both. The default is open. For Specify whether to finalise documents for a month or for a single meeting. Save Log After clicking the Finalise button the progress will be detailed in the pale box. Already finalised documents cannot be finalised again but will be skipped and this will be noted in the progress box. If a document cannot be found in the EDRMS then it will be skipped and this will be noted in the progress box. Finalise Click to initiate the finalising process. Take care. At present there is no restriction on what documents may be finalised. Should you finalise something unintended, you will need to un-finalise it in your EDRMS to be able to continue change it in Infocouncil (e.g. including a report in a meeting). Close Click to close the window without finalising any documents.

title: Functions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions If you have sufficient user rights, you will see the Functions ribbon menu on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Functions ribbon menu provides administrators with tools that can be used to help them with exporting business papers to another location, finalising documents in a EDRMS, taking minutes at a remote location and several other tasks. Export Copies reports and business papers to another location. See the Export section. Finalise Finalises documents in an EDRMS where configured to interface to an EDRMS. See the Finalise section Next Item Number Allocation Allows changing or resetting of item numbers. See the Next Item Number Allocation section Process Destination Folders Compares differences between the draft and final destinations of reports for a meeting and processes them. See the Process Destination Folders section. Remote Minutes Takes Minutes documents away from the office network, updates and then returns them. See the Remote Minutes section. Send Email Send an email to a list or subscribers, authors or members. See the Send Email section. Transfer Reports Transfers reports from one meeting to another. See the Transfer Reports section.

title: Go To
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Go To When you have a report open, click the Go To ribbon menu to go to places in the document. Recommendations Goes to the recommendation(s), if there are any, in the document. Start of Document Goes to the start of the document. End of Document Goes to the end of the document.

title: End of Document
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Go To > End of Document Go To End of Document Goes to the end of the document.

title: Recommendations
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Go To > Recommendations Go To Recommendations Goes to the recommendation(s), if there are any, in the document.

title: Start of Document
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Go To > Start of Document Go To Start of Document Goes to the start of the document.

title: Help
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Help Clicking a Help button anywhere in Infocouncil will open a Help topic . The topic displayed will depend on where you are and which colour help button you click on. There are blue and possibly magenta Help buttons in Infocouncil: · or - blue - authored by Infocouncil to provide you with help about the system. · or - magenta - authored by someone within your organisation to provide you with organisation-specific help. Note: To go to Word help press the F1 key or click the button under the Maximise, Minimise and Close buttons. Magenta help buttons will only appear if your organisation has authored help for a particular screen or document type, and linked it to Infocouncil via the Custom Help maintenance screen. Clicking Help From the Ribbon Clicking on either the blue or the magenta question mark on the Infocouncil ribbon will open a help file. Button Description Displays help authored by Infocouncil. If you have a non-Infocouncil document open (e.g. a blank Word document) then the help will open to the Welcome topic. Anywhere in the Infocouncil help system can be navigated to. However, if you have an Infocouncil document open then the help will open to a topic relevant to the document type. For example, if you have an Infocouncil report open then the help file will open to the topic on Reports. If present, displays help authored by someone in your organisation. It should display relevant help to the document type that is active but this depends on how it has been set up at your site. See the concept topic on Custom Help for more detail. Clicking Help From an Infocouncil window Where a window has no help, then no help button will be displayed. Where a window has help authored by Infocouncil, then a help button (the blue question mark) will be displayed and clicking it or pressing F1 will display that help. Where a window has help authored by your organisation, then an alternate help button (the magenta question mark) will be displayed and clicking it will display that help. Pressing F1 will not display the client authored help. Where a window has help authored by Infocouncil as well as help authored by your organisation, then both help buttons will be displayed. Pressing F1 will display the Infocouncil-authored help. The help topic displayed will be relevant to the screen from which the help was invoked. For Infocouncil authored help, once in the help system the user can then navigate to anywhere else in the Infocouncil authored help. Where an Infocouncil help topic is displayed it will be directly relevant to the screen where the help button was clicked. It will describe the fields and buttons on that screen. From this topic anywhere else in the help system can be navigated to. Where a help topic is displayed from your organisation it will generally also describe the screen you clicked the help button from, but it is likely to be specific to the way your organisation works with that s

title: Import Reference Type Version
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Reference Types > Import Reference Type Version Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Reference Types opens the Reference Types window. Search for, or list all reference types and select one to import a reference type version for. Either c licking on the Import New button opens the Import Reference Type Version window, or clicking on Edit opens the Reference Type Version window and then clicking on Import opens the Import Reference Type Version window. This form provides the means for an administrator to import a new reference type version or override an existing one. Version Label Plan Year Enter a label for the version unique to the reference type. This might be the year the version relates to for a master plan or it might be anything that identifies this version from other versions for the reference type. Import Spreadsheet Type the file path to the spread sheet, or click on the Browse button to locate it. Prefix the summary with the list number The summary text is prefixed with the list number as it is imported. Import Click to import the selected Excel spread sheet. The import is expecting a specific layout. See Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To for full detail of the spreadsheet requirements . Cancel Click to close the window. See Also Import Format of Reference Type Version Spreadsheet - How To Reference Types - Screen Help Reference Type Versions - Screen Help Maintaining reference Types - How To Importing a New Reference Type Version - How To Adding a Reference Type Version - How To Modifying a Reference Type Version - How To Removing a Reference Type Version - How To Importing an Existing Reference Type Version - How To

title: Include in Meeting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other > Include in Meeting When an administrator has a report open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Include in Meeting opens this form. It allows the report to be marked for inclusion in a meeting or if already included, it allows it to be excluded. This action achieves the same as the administrator marking it for inclusion on the Create Paper form. If, however, the report is not fully authorised, the administrator will be informed that inclusion is prevented. And if the report had already been included then the administrator will be informed, and given an opportunity to exclude it from the meeting. Include in Meeting Marks the report for inclusion in the meeting. Exclude from meeting Marks the report for exclusion from the meeting. Cancel Closes the Report Authorisers form without making any changes and returns you to the report.

title: Infocouncil Ribbon
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon The Infocouncil ribbon tab is the entry point to all screens. It provides access to all the functions that Infocouncil offers, from creating a report and managing actions assigned to you to generating an agenda, from minute taking tools to all the many configuration options. It will only be visible in the Microsoft Word ribbon if Infocouncil has been installed on the workstation and it is an active add-in . Different groups within the Infocouncil Ribbon will be visible depending on the type of the active document. Different tools will be visible depending on each users security access. Click on the hotspots on the relevant ribbon command below to drill down to the relevant screen. Alternately use the table of contents to navigate to the topic of the screen you require help on. The Main & Tools groups are always visible on the Infocouncil ribbon tab. Some or all of the Administration group will be visible if you have the rights to use them. The Clauses group will be visible if your organisation has chosen to use it. The Report group is visible whenever an Infocouncil Report is the active document ("Confidential" may be customised at your site). Note: This group is labelled "Attachments" or "Minute Items Attachments" if this is the paper type of the active document. The Agenda group is visible whenever an Agenda is the active document. Its name changes whenever an Attachments or Minutes Items Attachments document is the active document. The Minutes group is visible whenever an Infocouncil Minutes is the active document Not What You Expect Sometimes the ribbon will not show what is expected. When Infocouncil is run for the first time or when a user runs Infocouncil for the first time after their permissions to various ribbon tools change, the ribbon will not be as it should. The ribbon will self "right" itself after one of the Infocouncil ribbon tools are used for the first time. This, while undesirable, allows Infocouncil to avoid touching its database until an Infocouncil tool is first invoked. And this is very desirable for those users who disconnect from the network and do not want to wait more than 30 seconds for Infocouncil to try to communicate to the database which is no longer able to be connected to. See The Infocouncil Ribbon - Startup, Shut Down and In Session for a more technical discussion on this. See Also The Infocouncil Ribbon - Startup, Shut Down and In Session - Concept Topic

title: Insert Manual Clause
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Clauses Group > Insert Manual Clause Clicking on the Insert Manual Clause button on the Infocouncil ribbon inserts a manual condition at the cursor point. The Insert Manual Clause is useful when you wish to insert a unique clause that is not contained in the clauses "dictionary" for your organisation. Indenting the new Manual Clause Place the cursor on a new blank line at the same indent level as the clause required then click the Insert Manual Clause button. For example:-

title: Insert Minutes Text
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Insert Minutes Text When a minutes document is open, clicking the Insert Minutes Text button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-8 ) opens the Insert Text form. The Insert Minutes Text tool allows the minute taker to insert text such as conflicts of interest, code of conduct, or any other predefined text. Choose text to insert Select from item to insert (or press the number associated with the item) Choose a member... Click the radio button beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. A member may not be required for some items. This is a flag on the Text Definitions Maintenance form. OK Click to initiate the change requested. Cancel Click to exit without making any changes. See Also Text Definitions Maintenance

title: Insert Text
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Tools Group > Insert Text When any document is open, clicking the Insert Text button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Insert Text Form. The Insert Text form allows the user to insert predefined text ta the cursor position of the current document. List Select the text from the list that you wish to insert. Texts available here are maintained through the Text Definitions Maintenance form. OK Closes the form and inserts the text into the active document at the current cursor position. Cancel Exits the form without doing anything.

title: Item Numbering Options
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Create Paper > Item Numbering Options When an agenda is created, that contains reports for an agenda item type where the Update Item Number checkbox is checked, it will display the Item Numbering Options form. It allows the user to control what the item numbering starts from. Starting No Item numbering begins at the specified number. Retain item numbers already assigned If checked then items with a number already, remain as is. OK Item numbering is affected and the agenda creation continues. Cancel Item numbering is unaffected and the agenda creation continues.

title: Left The Meeting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Left The Meeting When a minutes document is open, clicking the Left The Meeting button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-5 ) opens the Who left the meeting form . The Left The Meeting tool allows the minute taker to quickly enter a statement noting the time and name of the member who left. Infocouncil then marks them as inactive for the purposes of moving, seconding or voting. Select Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. OK Click to complete the process. Text similar to the illustration below (but in the organisation's wording and format) will be appear wherever the cursor was positioned: Cancel Click to exit without making any changes. See Also Return to Meeting

title: List Minute Items for a Meeting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > List Minute Items for a Meeting Clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and choosing Minute Items for a Meeting opens the List Minute Items for a Meeting window . This report lists all minuted items for a meeting. You can choose sort sort order, either Open or Closed/Confidential items, items to list and the layout of the report. Meeting Type The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. Meeting Date The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. Sort Order Order Number Sorts in the order in which items were taken in the meeting. Minute Number Sorts by minute number. Open/Closed Open Click to list only Open/Public items. Closed Check to list only Closed/Confidential items. List Choose between Reports, New Items or Set Items. Layout Choose between Action details, One row per item or Resolution in second row. OK Click to create the report. It will be displayed as a "throwaway" Word document that you can edit if you wish. Cancel Click to exit without creating a report. Example Minutes Items for a Meeting Report for the Council meeting held 30 September 2016. See Also Modifying and Creating Report Templates

title: List Reports for a Meeting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > List Reports for a Meeting Clicking on the List Reports for a Meeting button in the Main Group on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the List Reports for a Meeting form. Also, clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and choosing List Reports for a Meeting opens the same form . This form allows you to select a particular meeting and create a Word document about reports that have been put to that meeting. The form is divided into three parts so you can select a specific meeting then choose the reports to include and layout of the report. Select meeting and date Meeting Type The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. Meeting Date The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. Select criteria Show reports for the meeting This is the default option. It includes all reports for the meeting in the report. Show reports recommended to this meeting This option lists reports referred or recommended to the meeting you chose. Drop down List Select a particular type of report to include in the list. For instance you may choose all the Standard or Decision or Information-Only reports. Options in the list depend on the report types that have been created for your organisation . Layout Attachments in second row This is the default option. It appears as a landscape document containing a table with about 8 columns. Typically it is a subset of data from the Document Menu. The name and location of each attachment is also listed for each report. Specific data shown depends on what has been set up for your organisation. Detailed with recommendation This option appears as a portrait document containing a series of tables. Each shows data relating to a specific report. Typically it is a subset of data from the Document Menu. The report's Recommendation is also included. Specific data shown depends on what has been set up for your organisation. One row per item This option appears as a landscape document containing a table with about 8 columns. Each row shows data relating to a specific report. Typically it is a subset of data from the Document Menu followed by the body of each report. Specific data shown depends on what has been set up for your organisation. Recommended items This option appears as a landscape document containing a table with about 13 columns. Typically it is a subset of data from the Document Menu. Specific data shown depends on what has been set up for your organisation. OK Click to produce the list of reports. It appears as a "throw-away" document in Word format. Cancel Click to exit the window without creating a list of reports. See Also Document Menu Modifying and Creating Report Templates

title: Load Clauses
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Clauses Group > Load Clauses Clicking on the Load Clauses button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Load Standard Conditions window. The Load Standard Conditions window allows you to select one or more pieces of set text to insert into your document at the cursor point. The first time the window is loaded a document containing a "dictionary" of clauses used in your organisation is also opened and made invisible. The list on the left is a "dictionary" of clauses/pieces of set text that has been created for your organisation. The list of number on the right represent clauses that will be inserted into your document. Note the checkboxes on the right: · To flag a clause for editing after insertion, check the box beside the selected condition number. Clauses containing form fields will automatically be checked for editing. · If unchecked the clause will be inserted into your document but not marked for editing. Moves the selected clause from left to right. Moves all the clauses in the same section left to right. Moves all the clauses in the same section right to left. Moves the selected clause from right to left. Moves selected clause on the right up. Moves the selected clause on the left down. Restart List Check to restart the list numbering from 1 when the current conditions are inserted. Insert Inserts the clauses listed on the right into the document. Hide Hides the form but retains the currently selected conditions. Use this button if the process of entering conditions (for one or more documents) has not been completed and you expect to return to the form to finalise your action. Cancel Cancels the insertion of conditions and closes the Standard Conditions document (which has been open but invisible). Use this button once the process of entering conditions is completed. See Also Clauses Group Next Item To Edit Return to Last Item Insert Manual Clause

title: Main Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group The Main Group is always visible on the Infocouncil ribbon tab. The Main Group provides all users with buttons to create and retrieve reports, list reports for a meeting and list actions. The "i" button provides information about the version of Infocouncil that is installed for the client and has a Remote Support button for direct support of clients via TeamViewer. Button Description Begins creating a NEW report. You do not click on this button when you wish to retrieve a report that has already been saved. See the Create Report section. RETRIEVES a report for viewing or modification. A form requesting the meeting name and date is presented. Select them then select the report from the list. See the Document Menu section. Displays a list of all outstanding actions for the current user. You can add notes to the actions and complete the actions from this window. See the Actions section. Lists reports for a meeting. A form requesting the meeting name and date is presented. Select them then the system creates a Word document with details of all reports created for a meeting. The Word document is available in summary or detailed formats. See the List Reports for a Meeting section. Seeks information about the version of Infocouncil that is installed on this client. The window also has a Remote Support button for direct support of clients via TeamViewer. See the About Infocouncil section. Displays help authored by Infocouncil. To go to Word help press the F1 key or click the button under the Maximise, Minimise and Close buttons. See the Help section. Displays help authored by your organisation. See the Help section.

title: Maintenance
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance If you have sufficient user rights, you will see the Maintenance ribbon menu on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Maintenance ribbon menu provides administrators with tools that can be used to help them with maintaining the system. For instance, authors, meeting dates, members and security functions. Action Sub Types Maintains action sub types. See the Action Sub Types section. Agenda Items Maintains agenda items. See the Agenda Items section. Agenda Sections Maintains agenda sections. See the Agenda Sections section. Authors Maintains authors. See the Authors section. Confidential Acts Maintains confidential acts ("confidential" may be customised at your site) . See the Confidential Acts section. Confidential Reasons Maintains confidential reasons ("confidential" may be customised at your site) . See the Confidential Reasons section. Councils Maintains councils. If configured to use councils. See the Councils section. Deadline Types Maintains deadline types which are applied to meetings to assist completing meeting preparation on time. See the Deadline Types section. Divisions Maintains divisions. See the Divisions section. Meeting Cycles Maintains meeting dates. See the Meeting Cycles section. Meeting Dates Maintains meeting dates. See the Meeting Dates section. Master Event Types Maintains meeting events. See the Meeting Event Types section. Meeting Types Maintains meeting types. See the Meeting Types section. Meetings Maintains meetings. See the Meetings section. Members: Maintains meeting members/councillors. See the Members section. Procedural Motion Types Maintains procedural motion types. See the Procedural Motion Types section. Publish Destinations Maintains publish destinations. See the Publish Destinations section. Ministry Portfolios Maintains ministry portfolios. If configured to use ministry portfolios. See the Ministry Portfolios section. Reference Types: Maintains reference types. See the Reference Types section. Roles Maintains roles. If configured to use roles. See the Roles section. Security Functions Maintains security functions which are parts of Infocouncil to which security can be controlled for. See the Security Functions section. Security Groups Maintains security groups to which security functions can be given access to and authors may be a part of. See the Security Groups section. Subscriber Types Maintains subscriber types. See the Subscriber Types section. Subscribers Maintains subscribers. See the Subscribers section. Text Defnitions Maintains text definitions. See the Text Definitions section. EDRMS Parameters Manages the EDRMS parameters. If configured to interface to an EDRMS. See the EDRMS Parameters section. System Parameters Manages system parameters. See the System Parameters section. Custom Help Links Maintains client-authored help links. See the Client Help Links section.

title: Make Resolution Row
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Make Resolution Row When a minutes document is open, clicking the Make Resolution Row button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-7 ) opens the Resolution Row form. The Make Resolution Row tool allows the minute taker to either make the row THE resolution row, or make the row and ADDITIONAL row. Infocouncil then adds or moves bookmarks accordingly. Make the current row the resolution row The resolution bookmark moves. Click A to select this option. Make the current row an additional row An additional resolution bookmark is added so the resolution can be resolved in parts. Click B to select this option. OK Click to initiate the change requested. Cancel Click to exit without making any changes.

title: Meetings
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meetings Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting opens the Meeting window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit meetings. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the meetings name. List all meetings Select to view a list of all meetings. Show Inactive Check to include inactive meetings in the search results. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of meetings matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new meeting to be entered. Delete Select a meeting in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the meeting. Up/Down The up and down buttons will only appear if ALL meetings are being displayed. In this situation the meetings are sorted in the order that they will appear in the drop downs throughout the system. Clicking the up and down buttons will shift the selected meeting in that order. Details The Details Section has 6 tabs containing all the details for a individual meeting. General tab The General tab is illustrated above. Meeting Name of Meeting. Meeting Type Type of Meeting. The default is Committee. The meeting type controls whether deadlines are generated for meeting dates for this committee. Changing the meeting type will therefore affect any pre-existing deadlines. Where the new meeting type defines deadline rules and any of the rules have a deadline time as being mandatory, then the Regenerate Deadlines screen will display on save. Abbreviation A unique abbreviation for the meeting name (2 or 3 letters). One of the places where users may notice this is in the File Name of their documents. For example, a report file name for a report to Council meeting on 3 Sept 2016 could begin with CO20160903.... But in the Agenda the report will be titled Apples and Oranges , or whatever the author specified as the Subject in the Report Data Entry window. Minute Number Prefix The letters that are to prefix the minute number for the meeting. For example, FIN/2016/122. Default Action Days The default number of days given for a person to complete an action. An administrator can edit the default number on any action sheet as it is being created. Quorum Some organisations choose to display the number of members who form a quorum for the meeting on the cover of their agendas. Maximum Number of Reports for a Meeting The default maximum number of reports for a meeting. The default may be changed for an individual meeting, on the Meeting Dates screen. A warning will be displayed to report writers when this number is exceeded. Default Approver The person who will be the default approver of business papers. Paper Signer The person who will be inserted in agenda documents as the signer of the paper. Meeting Bookmark Name Set by Infocouncil

title: Meeting Cycles
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Cycles Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Cycles opens the Meeting Cycles window. This form provides the mechanism to add, edit or delete Meeting Cycles. Cycles may be used by a Council to define the rollover period for item and/or minutes numbers. There may be more than one cycle in a year, or a cycle could define a longer period such as a Council term. It could also define a financial year. Search by Cycle Searches for cycles matching the year or number you enter. Cycle year Enter the year. This may be any text up to 4 characters long. Examples are 2018, 18, FY16, CT18. Cycle number Enter the cycle number. This may be any text up to 4 characters long. Examples are 1, 2, TERM, YEAR. Search by date range Searches for cycles within the date range you specify. Start Date Select a start date. End Date Select an end date. List all Meeting Cycles Lists all meeting cycles without filtering. Search Click to initiate the search for the criteria you chose. List Lists all the meeting cycles matching the criteria you chose. New Click to enter a new meeting cycle. Edit Click to edit the selected meeting cycle. The details will be displayed on the right. Delete Click to delete the selected meeting cycle. Details Details of the meeting cycle selected on the left. Year Enter the year as a 4 digit number (e.g. 2011). Cycle number Enter the cycle number. Start Date Use the date picker to enter the start date. End Date Use the date picker to enter the end date. Save Click to save the changes you made. Undo Click to undo changes you have made. Cancel Click to discard any changes you made to the details of the selected meeting cycle. Close Click to close the form.

title: Meeting Dates
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Dates Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Dates opens the Meeting Dates window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add, edit or delete individual meetings. Search Criteria Search by meeting type Select a meeting type from the drop down list to view a list of individual meetings of the selected type. List all meetings Select to view a list of individual meetings of every type. Show meetings older than 9 Months By default meeting dates will be displayed that are 9 months old or greater. Clicking the checkbox will show all meeting dates. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of meetings matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new meeting to be entered. Delete Select a meeting in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the meeting. Details The Details Section has 4 tabs containing all the details for a individual meeting. General tab The General tab contains basic information about one meeting. Committee Name of the Committee/Meeting Type. Meeting Date Date of the meeting. Note: If you are editing a meeting, you will not be able to change the date if there are reports for the meeting or recommended to the meeting. If there are such reports, you will need to open each one and update the Meeting Date in the Report Data form. Meeting Locations Location of the meeting. This should be detailed so members of the public can find the room and attend the meeting. In most organisations it appears on the front of business papers. Note that it will default to the location on the meeting form if one has been entered. Actl/Approx Time Actual or approximate time of the meeting. Entered time value will be used for the sorting purpose when papers are published. Meeting Time Time of the meeting. It may be specified as a specific time or informally, e.g., "Following council meeting". It will be displayed in business papers in exactly the format typed. Meeting Number Number of the meeting. This option is informational and not required. Maximum Reports Defaults to the maximum number of reports for the meeting but may be varied for this meeting. Report writers will be warned when they try to save a report to meeting if they exceed this number. A zero (0) will disable this function. This option is only applicable for some organisations. Extraordinary/Special Checked if meeting is Extraordinary or Special. Meeting Cycle If the council uses Meeting Cycles the Cycle will display. Additional Meeting Checked if the meeting is in addition to the normal cycle of meetings. This option is only applicable for some organisations. Information Only Meeting Checked if the meeting is for information only. This option is only applicable for some organisations. Dates and Times

title: Meeting Event
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Meeting Events > Meeting Event Clicking the New , Edit or Details button on the Meeting Events form opens the Meeting Event form. This form provides the means to view, add new or modify an existing event's details. Note that until the minutes paper for the meeting has been uploaded (had its actions generated), events cannot be associated with a minutes paper or its items or resolutions. The Associate with Minutes combo will indicate that Minutes have not been uploaded for the meeting. Events that were created automatically by the system may show an Associated Bookmark which on upload will then automatically be updated to show the association to the relevant resolution. item and minutes paper. Meeting The name of the meeting that the event relates to. Mandatory. Meeting Date The date of the meeting that the event relates to. Mandatory. Meeting Time The time of the meeting that the event relates to. Mandatory. Event Category The meeting event type category of the event. Selecting the category will populate Event Type combo with the possible types. Mandatory. Event Type The meeting event type of the event. Mandatory. Date Time The date and time the event occurred. The time is in 24 hour time and to one thousandths of a second. Mandatory. Description A summary description on what the event was. Mandatory. Associated with Minutes Optionally associate the event with any of the minutes papers produced for the selected meeting date. Selecting the minutes will populate the Minute Item combo with the possible minute items. Associated with Minute Item Optionally associate the event with any of the minute items for the selected minutes. Selecting the minute item will populate the Resolution combo with the possible resolutions for the minute item. It will also automatically populate the Associated Bookmark with the minute item's bookmark. Associated with Resolution Optionally associate the event with any of the resolutions for the selected minute item. Selecting the minute item will automatically populate the Associated Bookmark with the resolution's bookmark. Associated with Member Optionally associate the event with a member. Save Click to accept any change and exit the screens. Undo Click to undo any changes. Close Click to cancel any changes and exit the screen. See Also Meeting Events - Screen Topic Meeting Events - Concept Topic

title: Meeting Events
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Meeting Events Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and selecting Meeting Events opens the Meeting Events form. This form lists meeting events according to your search criteria so you can monitor, report on and update meeting events. The form is divided into three parts. The search criteria, the list and the buttons. Search Criteria The following are the fields that can be used to filter what is displayed in the list. Single Meeting Check to display a Meeting and Meeting Date control for filtering events by a single meeting. Multiple Meetings Check to display a Meeting multi combo and a From and To Date controls for filtering events by none, one or any number of meeting, for all time or for a specific date range. Meeting Show events for the selected meeting. Meeting Date Show events for the selected meeting date. Meetings The drop-down lists all the Meetings (committees) that have been set up for your organisation. Use the drop down to choose one or more meeting types to list actions arising from the selected meetings. To remove a meeting from the list, select it then click on the X button. Click the Include inactive button to add all inactive meetings to the list. Meeting From and To Dates Enter a From Date to show events that occurred on or after that date. Enter a To Date to show events that occurred on or before that date. Enter no dates for all time, or both dates to restrict to a specific time range. Event Types Displays all event type categories.
