Expanding each category displays all event types contained by the category. Select or deselect any category or type to filter matching events. Members The drop-down lists all the Members who have been set up for your organisation. Use the drop down to choose one or more members to list meeting events associated with them. To remove a member from the list, select it then click on the X button. Click the Include inactive button to add all inactive members to the list. Search Click so the list of meeting events can be reloaded according to your selected search criteria. Sort Click to display the Define Grid Sort screen to allow control over how the actions list is ordered. More complex sorting can be achieved via this screen or simple single column sorting can be achieved by clicking a column heading. Either method cancels any sort defined by the other. Where a multi column sort is defined, only the header of the first column defined in the sort will show an up or down arrow in it. The sort is preserved when performing a modified search, or when exiting and returning to this screen. Meeting Events List Displays details about the matching meeting events. Double click on a meeting event to open its details in the Meeting Event screen. Hovering the mouse over a column header or document detail will display the full text in a tooltip where the text is truncated. The list can be customised and changes

title: Meeting Events Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Meeting Events > Meeting Events Report Clicking the Print button on the Actions window opens the Action Report window. This window allows you to select a layout then an Actions Report in Word will be created. Report Layout Choose the layout of the report you wish to produce. Additional layouts may be configured. Standard layout supplied at implementation are: · Conflict of Interests OK Click to produce the report. Cancel Click to exit without creating a report. See Also Meeting Events - Screen Topic Meeting Events - Concept Topic Modifying Meeting Event Templates - How To Topic

title: Meeting Event Type Catgories
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Event Types > Meeting Event Type Catgories Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Event Types opens the Meeting Event Types window. Clicking on the Manage Categories button opens the Meeting Event Type Categories window. This form provides the means for an administrator to define the types of meeting type categories they wish to group the meeting event types by. List Lists all the meeting event type categories that have been created for the organisation. Details Contains details of the selected Meeting Event Type Category. Name Name of selected Meeting Event Type Category. Meeting Event Types Displays the meeting event types in this category. Cannot be changed in this screen. A meeting event type category can be changed in the Meeting Event Type screen. Save Click to save the details you have been adding or editing. Undo Click to undo any changes you made to the details you have been adding or editing. Cancel Click to exit the details without saving any changes. Close Closes the window. See Also Meeting Event Types - Screen Help Meeting Events - Concept Topic

title: Meeting Event Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Event Types Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Event Types opens the Meeting Event Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to change a meeting type's category or active state. Meeting Types can only be added by Infocouncil or for Text Definitions . List Lists all the meeting event types that have been created for the organisation. Details Contains details of the selected Meeting Event Type. Name Name of selected Meeting Event Type. Category The category the Meeting Event Type belongs to. Active Active meeting events will be allowed to be created by the system. Make an event type inactive if you do not need events of this type being created. Save Click to save the details you have been adding or editing. Undo Click to undo any changes you made to the details you have been adding or editing. Cancel Click to exit the details without saving any changes. Manage Categories Click to open the Meeting Event Type Categories form where you can add, edit or delete categories. Close Closes the window. See Also Meeting Event Type Categories - Screen Help Text Definitions - Screen Help Meeting Events - Screen Help Meeting Events - Concept Topic

title: Meeting System Parameters
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meetings > Meeting System Parameters Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu, then choosing Meeting Maintenance , then selecting a meeting and clicking System Parameters opens the System Parameters window. The system parameters defined here apply to the selected meeting from the Meeting Maintenance form. Infocouncil uses system parameters to customise the application so it can be adapted to each organisation without the need to change the system's code. For example, the EDRMS Document Naming Conventions or location to the document templates. Below is a table of all system parameters that apply to meetings, their name and their description. Expand a parameter group to see all the parameters in that group. Select a parameter in the list to see its setting. The grey pane at the bottom of the window provides an explanation on the selected parameter. The pane can be increased or decreased in size by dragging the bar above it. OK Click to close the form and save any changes you made. Cancel Click to close the form and discard any changes. Actions Name Description Actions visible on upload Actions will be visible on upload. They will immediately become available to the default responsible officer on the Actions screen. Authorisations - Default actions authorisers from report If true, and a minute item is associated with a report, actions authorisers will be defaulted from the report. If False no authorisers will be defaulted - they will be added manually if required. Authorisations - Default original officer to first authoriser on reallocation If true, the original officer will be defaulted to the first authoriser on reallocation. Closed items in open minutes create open actions If true, actions created from confidential reports in open minutes documents will be open. Default prevent change of target date When an action is created, the default for prevent change of target date will be set using this flag. Use EDRMS for Actions For EDRMS organisations, if false the Generate Actions screen will prevent action sheets being able to be committed to the EDRMS. Use Email for Actions If true then action sheets may be emailed to the action officers. Attachments Name Description PDF Attachments (2) - Include PDF attachment images in the Word version of papers [This option is applicable only if the option to insert original PDF attachments in the PDF version of papers, is set to True] If true, images of PDF pages will be inserted in the Word version of papers. If false, a placeholder page will be inserted. In both cases the original (real) PDF page will appear in the PDF version of the paper. This parameter may be overwritten at the meeting (committee) level. PDF Attachments (3) - Ask to include attachment images or placeholders [This option is applicable only if the option to insert original PDF attachments in the PDF version of papers, is set to True] Prompt

title: Meeting Speakers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Dates > Meeting Speakers Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Dates opens the Meeting Dates window. With a meeting date selected, click the Speakers... button from the bottom left of the window to open the Meeting Speakers window. This form provides the means for an administrator to enter details of people who wish to address a meeting. Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, the details could appear in the business papers when they are created. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the speakers name. List all speakers Select to view a list of all speakers. Please note that sorting buttons are provided to re-order speakers within a meeting when all speakers are shown. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of speakers matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the details ready for a new speaker to be entered. Delete Select a speaker in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the speaker. Details The Details Section contains details for an individual closed speaker. Name Name of person wishing to address the meeting. Company Their company. Address Their address. Representing The name of the company/group/organisation they are representing. Home Phone Their home phone number. Work Phone Their work phone number. FAX Their fax number. Date requested Use the date-picker to record the date they made their request to speak. Report Addressed Choose the name of the report they wish to speak about. The list will contain all reports for that meeting. For/Against Radio Buttons Click the radio button to indicate whether they wish to speak for or against the report. Recommendation/Development Radio buttons Click the radio button to indicate whether they wish to speak about the recommendation or a development. Buttons Save Click to save changes you have made. Undo Click to undo any changes you have made. Close Click to close the window.

title: Meeting Timetable
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Meeting Timetable Clicking on Reporting ribbon menu and selecting Meeting Timetable opens the Meeting Timetable form. This report lists all the meetings for a date range, with one meeting per row. The report can include deadline information from the Dates and Times (second) tab on the Meeting Dates window. Time Frame From Date Select a date to filter for meetings from a certain date. To Date Select a date to filter for meetings to a certain date. Select meeting type Leave set to the default of <All meeting types> or choose an individual meeting type. Sort Order Meeting Date Choose to sort by Meeting Date. Committee Sort Order by Meeting Type. Choose to sort by Committee name. Report Layout By default there may only be one choice - "Meeting details". But if your organisation uses Deadline Dates then you may also be able to choose "With deadlines". OK Click to produce Meeting Timetable report. It appears as a "throw-away" document in Word format. Cancel Click to exit the window without creating a report. Example Meeting Timetable Report for the all meetings scheduled between 1 January and 31 December 2016. See Also Modifying and Creating Report Templates

title: Meeting Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Types Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Types opens the Meeting Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to define meeting types. A meeting can have a meeting type associated with it via the Meeting Maintenance screen. A meeting type is a way to categorise meetings so that deadline types can be defined for the set of meetings in a category. For instance, the Council meeting type may have a set of deadline types that ensure the agenda can be compiled and distributed x number of days before the meeting because the agendas are large and members need time to read them. But meetings of the Committee meeting type may have a slightly shorter set of deadlines because their agendas are smaller. List Lists all the meeting types that have been created for the organisation. New Click to create a new meeting type. Edit Click to edit the selected meeting type. Delete Click to delete the selected meeting type. Details Contains details of the selected Meeting Type. Description Name of selected Meeting Type. Deadlines The list displays the following columns. Deadline Type Name of deadline type. Required Whether required. True means a date must be specified when creating a new meeting. False means the default date can be cleared (set to nothing). Days Out Number of business days before the meeting date. Time Whether required. True means a time must be specified when creating a new meeting. False means the time can be left blank. Time Required Time reports are due. The deadline time is a text field only. It does not enforce correct time format. The reason for this is to allow other time expressions such as Afternoon, Before end of day, etc. Significance Identifies the significance of the deadline to the system. As the system does not understand the significance of the deadline type, because it can be any text that a organisation desires, this column tells the system how it is significant. Possible values are 1st Authoriser, 2nd Authoriser, 3rd Authoriser, 4th Authoriser or Last Authoriser. New Click to add details of a new deadline type for the selected meeting. The following pop up displays whose fields match the list column descriptions above. Edit Select a deadline from the list above then click to edit the details of that deadline type. The same pop up displays as when the New button is clicked. Delete Select a deadline from the list above then click to delete that deadline type. Save Click to save the details you have been adding or editing. Where deadline rules have been changed that require deadlines to be regenerated, they will be. Where one or more of the deadline rules stipulate time is mandatory, the Regenerate Deadlines screen will be displayed to allow default times to be provided. Undo Click to undo any changes you made to the details you have been adding or editing. Cancel Click to exit the details without savi

title: Member Names
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Member Names When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Member Names opens the Members Names - select names to insert form. The Members Names tool allows the minute taker to select one or more member names to be inserted into the document at the cursor point. The system will insert the selected name(s) in the organisation's format. Check the box beside their name to select. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. OK Inserts the selected name(s), at the cursor point and using your organisation's format. For example: Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the form and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Members
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Members Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Members opens the Members window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add and edit members. Search Criteria Search by name Choose Search by name then enter the text in the adjacent box. Search by meeting type Choose Search by meeting type then choose the required Meeting Type from the drop-down list. List all Members Select "List all Members" to view a list of all members. Show Inactive Check to include inactive members. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of members matching your selection. List Sorting the List Depending on how the system has been configured for your organisation, the list of members: · may always appear sorted by name · or when searching by Meeting Type they may appear in seating order. This option will be available only if the system flag to always sort members by name is not on. The up and down arrows beside the list allow adjustment to the seating order. This option may be of assistance when recording divisions during a meeting. New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new member to be entered. Delete Select a member in the list above so their Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the member. You should ONLY delete a member if you accidentally enter them twice or make some similar mistake. If an member has ever attended a meeting then instead of deleting them you should mark them as Inactive. Details The Details Section has 6 tabs containing all the details for a individual member. Details tab The Details tab contains basic information about one member. First Name Member's first name. Last Name Member's last name. Initials Member's initials. Name Prefix Any text that is to appear before the member's name. For example, Councillor, Cr, Clr, Member, Councillor Sir. Name Suffix Any text that is to appear after the member's name. For Example, OAM, ONZ, QSO, JP Email Address Member's email address. Phone Member's phone number. Mobile Member's mobile phone number. Other Phone Member's other phone number (if applicable) Fax Member's fax number (if applicable) Active Check if member is active/current. Remove the check when the member becomes inactive. Default Membership Position The default position for the selected member when they are added to any new Meeting Types. For example, Mayor, Deputy Mayor, Chair, Member, Councillor. Membership tab The Membership tab lists all the meetings of which the member is currently a member. Meeting Name of meeting. Position Position in this meeting. For example, Chair, Member, Cr. Ex-Officio Check to indicate that the member is an ex-officio member of the Meeting Type (i.e. not an elected member). Ex-Officio members are not recorded in the apologies if they do not attend a meeting but have voting rights wh

title: Ministry Portfolios
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Ministry Portfolios This form is specific to Local Government South Australia.

title: Minute Item
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > Minute Item Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking the Details button on the Actions form opens the Minute Item form with the Action Sheets tab displayed. Clicking the Details button on the Generate Actions form opens the Minute Item form with the Item Details tab displayed. Clicking the Action Sheets button on the Generate Actions form opens the Minute Item form with the Action Sheets tab displayed. This form provides the means to view details about a minute item, any of its resolutions and any of its action sheets. For administrators navigating from the Generate Actions screen certain details are editable including the full ability to add, edit and delete action sheets. Item Details Tab This tab displays the item detail and the resolution detail. Title/Subject The title or subject of the minute item in the minutes. This is editable for administrators navigating from the Generate Actions screen. Minute Item Id The internal identifier of the minute item which may be needed if a support issue arises. Meeting The meeting from which this minute item originated. Meeting Date The date of the meeting from which this minute item originated. Item Type The type of minute item, either: Report, Set Item or New Item. Item No Number of item in the minutes document. File No For new items, any file number entered. Member For new items, the member if one was selected from the New Item form. Confidential Yes if the item is confidential. Complete If any action sheets exist for the minute item that are actionable, this will show whether they are outstanding or the date that the last action sheet was completed. Uploaded The date that the minute item was uploaded. Resolution Tabs There will be as many tabs as there are resolutions for the minute item and they will be titled "Resolution 1", "Resolution 2", etc. If there are no resolutions there will be one tab with empty details. Resolution Fields Minute No Any minute number inserted for the resolution. This is editable for administrators navigating from the Generate Actions screen. Mover Any mover recorded for the resolution. This is editable for administrators navigating from the Generate Actions screen. Seconder Any seconder recorded inserted for the resolution. This is editable for administrators navigating from the Generate Actions screen. Resolution This contains the text of the resolution as found in the minutes document and minus any Word formatting. Action Sheets Tab This tab shows action sheet details and any notes associated with each action sheet. Action Sheet List Actions associated with a minute item or any of its resolutions are listed. Note that any changes to this screen will not be saved until the Save button at the bottom of the screen is clicked. Action Sheet List Columns Associated With This shows what the action sheet is associated with. It can be either the minute item itself or one of its resolutions. Action Type The type of action,

title: Minute Number
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Minute Number When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Minute Number inserts a new minute number at the cursor point.

title: Minutes Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group Whenever you open a Minutes document Infocouncil displays the Minutes group on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Minutes Group provides Minute takers with tools that can be used when completing a Minutes document. They are very quick and simple to use and each has an easy-to-remember keyboard shortcut that is worth learning. They are designed to assist rapid minute taking. Tool Description (Ctrl-Shift-O) Displays the Outline form. The user may view an outline of the document, move and copy items, and navigate directly to an item from this form. See the Outline section. (Ctrl-Shift-E) Enters the attendance details for a meeting. The user can specify that a member or members are not present. Absent members are made inactive in member lists displayed for other Minutes tools e.g. the Division tool. See the Attendance section. (Ctrl-1) Displays the Resolution form. The user may insert resolution text (including movers and seconders) from this form. See the Resolutions section. (Ctrl-2) Displays the Procedural Motions form. The user may insert procedural motion text (including movers and seconders) from this form. See the Motion section. (Ctrl-3) Displays a form prompting for For/Against and Absent/Abstaining members when a division has been called. The division text will to be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the Division section. (Ctrl-4) Displays the New Item form. An example of a new item is a Question Without Notice. The New Item text will to be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the New Item section. (Ctrl-UpArrow) Inserts a new row above the current row. The new row may be used for entering Procedural Motions, Divisions or any other text related to the item. See the Row Above section. (Ctrl-DownArrow) Inserts a new row below the current row. The new row may be used for entering Procedural Motions, Divisions or any other text related to the item. See the Row Below section. (Ctrl-5) Displays a form prompting for a Member who is leaving the meeting. Text will to be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the Left the Meeting section. (Ctrl-6) Displays a form prompting for a Member who is returning to the meeting. Text will to be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the Return to Meeting section. (Ctrl-Shift-R) or (Ctrl-7) Chooses between making the current row the resolution row or an additional resolution row. There is always at least one row that is designated as the row containing the resolution to the item. See the Make Resolution Row section. Notify Authorisers : Displays the list of authorisers for a report. Authorisers may be notified using this function. See the Notify Paper Authorisers section. Authorise : Allows an Authoriser to approve a report. See the Authorise or Return a Paper section. Return : Allows an Authoriser to return a report. See the Authorise or Return a Pa

title: Minutes Index Report
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Minutes Index Report Clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and choosing Minutes Index opens the Minutes Index Report window . This report lists minuted items, between two dates. It is designed as an index for a hard-copy book of signed minutes. Time Frame From Date Use the date picker to choose a start date. To Date Use the date picker to choose an end date. Selection Criteria Meeting The drop-down lists all the meetings that have been set up for your organisation. Type Choose between Reports, New Items or Set Items. Sort Order Meeting Date Click to sort by Meeting Date Committee Sort Order by Meeting Date Click to sort by Committee name then by Date order. All/Open/Closed All List all minutes items Open Only List open minutes items only Closed Only List confidential minutes items only OK Click to create the report. It will be displayed as a "throwaway" Word document that you can edit if you wish. Cancel Click to exit without creating a report. Example Minutes Index Report for Council meetings between 1 January and 31 December 2018. See Also Modifying and Creating Report Templates

title: Motion
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Motion When a minutes document is open, clicking the Motion button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-2 ) opens the Insert a procedural motion Form. The Motion tool allows the minute taker to insert a minute number, choose a mover and seconder and a procedural motion. Insert Minute Number / Resolve item fully The label is customised for your organisation in the "Text for check box on resolution screen" system parameters under Papers. Most commonly it is used to cause a minute number to be inserted with the resolution. It could be used for other purposes such as causing the item to be resolved fully. Check the box or alternatively, hit the space-bar. Choose a mover... Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Choose a seconder... Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Choose a procedural motion... Click the radio button beside a motion. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside the motion. OK Closes the window and inserts the motion into the Minutes document. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Procedural Motion Types
text: Navigation: »No topics above this level« Procedural Motion Types Show/Hide Hidden Text Click on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choose Procedural Maintenance Types. This opens the Procedural Motion Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit procedural motions. ID The Id of the motion type. To add a new motion, click the new row selector (bottom left of window) marked with an asterisk (*). Name Name of the Procedural Motion, as it will be seen by the minute taker when adding a procedural motion into the minutes. Text The text to appear when the motion type is inserted into a Minutes document. Tags that can be added to the text to substitute the matching text <LF> - New Line <TAB> - Tab <NAME OF MEETING> - the name of the meeting <MOVERTITLE> - the title at the meeting of the mover <SECONDERTITLE> - the title at the meeting of the seconder <MOVERLASTNAME> - the last name of the mover <MOVERLASTNAME,U> - the last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERLASTNAME> - the last name of the seconder <SECONDERLASTNAME,U> - the last name of the seconder in upper case <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME> - the initials and last name of the mover <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME,U> - the initials and last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME> - the initials and last name of the seconder <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME,U> - the initials and last name of the seconder in upper case <MOVERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME> - the first name and last name of the mover <MOVERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME,U> - the first name and last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME> - the first name and last name of the seconder <SECONDERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME,U> - the first name and last name of the seconder in upper case For example the text: Moved by <MOVERTITLE> <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME><LF>Seconded by <SECONDERTITLE> <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME> Would be interpreted as: Moved by Cr F Jones Seconded by Cr B Smith Default Flag Set by Infocouncil staff.

title: Move Item
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Move Item When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Move Item opens the Move Item form. The Move Item tool allows the minute taker to move (or copy) an item to another part of the document. Select item to move to cursor position Select the item you wish to move or copy. Select item to move above Make no selection to move or copy the item to the current cursor position. Alternately, select another item to move or copy the item above it. Add note to original location if moving If you wish a note to be left in the original location check the box. The note will look something like "Item - 5.4 Tender... - has been moved to another part of the document.". The note may also include an "enter number" field that can be filled when finalising the minutes. Copy the Item The item will be copied to the new location. The item will now be present in both the original location and the new one. Move the item The item will be moved to the new location. The item will now be present in only the new location. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the form and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Mover/Seconder
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Mover/Seconder When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Mover/Seconder opens the Mover/Seconder form. The Mover/Seconder tool allows the minute taker to insert the mover/seconder at the cursor point. Mover Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Seconder Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. OK Inserts the selected mover and seconder names into the minutes document using your organisation's format. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the form and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: New Item
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > New Item When a minutes document is open, clicking the New Item button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-4 ) opens the New Item Form. The New Item tool allows the minute taker to insert new items (e.g. a Question or Motion Without Notice), which are actionable. The new item text will be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point and additional information may then be entered. Any new item can be resolved using the Resolution tool. Item Type Select from the drop down list. The types and their format are organisation-specific. Item Number Enter the item number for this item. Depending on the Item Type the next item number for the selected type may be defaulted but may be changed if desired. Member Select the Member requesting the New Item. Title Type a short title/subject for the item. This becomes the item heading in the Minutes document. File Number Type a file number, if required. Action Officer Select an Officer if required and known. During the minutes upload an action sheet will be automatically created for the new item if an action officer has been entered otherwise action sheets may be manually created as desired. OK Closes the form and inserts the new item in the Minutes document. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Next Item Number Allocation
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Next Item Number Allocation Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Next Item Number Allocation opens the Next Item Number Allocation window. This form provides the means for an administrator in an organisation who have year, month or cycle-based item numbering to view or change item numbers for reports. Find Year Select the year to filter the list by the cycle. Clear the year to show next item numbers for all years. Month Select the Month to filter the list by the month. Clear the month to show next item numbers for all month. Cycle This is only visible if you organisation uses cycles. Select the Cycle to filter the list by the cycle. Clear the cycle to show next item numbers for all cycles. Next Item Numbers Click the column heading to sort by that column. Click again to sort in the opposite direction. Any resizing of the columns will be preserved next time you return to the screen on the same computer. Reports to Select the Year, Month or Cycle to list the numbers for a period. Next Number Shows the next item number for the report type. Reset All Numbers to 1 Resets all numbers to 1. Buttons Reset Click to reset all displayed item numbers to one. Then click save when ready. Save Click to save changes you have made. Undo Click to undo any changes you have made. Close Click to close the window.

title: Next Item To Edit
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Clauses Group > Next Item To Edit Clicking on the Next Item To Edit button on the Infocouncil ribbon moves the cursor to the next form field. The Next Item To Edit button is useful when you have inserted multiple clauses and you then need to find and edit all the fields in each clause.

title: Notify Action Authorisers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > Notify Action Authorisers To notify an authoriser on the action screen click the button at the beginning of the row to complete the action The Action Authorisers window displays a list of the authorisers and buttons to facilitate the authorisation process. Authoriser Names of authorisers for the action. Status There are four options: · To Be Notified · Notified · Authorised · Returned Date Date the status was last updated. History Lists the date and changes to status that a selected user has made. Notify Clicking on the Notify button opens the Create Mail form - a pre-formatted email to the selected authoriser. You can add an optional note before sending the email. Close The Close button closes the Report Authorisers form and returns you to the report.

title: Create Mail
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > Notify Action Authorisers > Create Mail Infocouncil uses a Create Mail form in several places throughout the system. It will open whenever you use the system to send a message to other users. A Create Mail form opens when you wish to notify an authoriser that your report or paper is ready for them to authorise it. The same form opens if an administrator wishes to send a reminder email. When the form opens it is pre-populated with all the required information: · Recipient · Sender · Subject · Details that will appear in the body of the email. An Optional Note field allows you to add a comment or an instruction. Subject This comprises two parts: · The action you are requesting. · The title of the document. Details/Body of Email The pre-populated body of the email contains details of the meeting that the request relates to. It also contains a link that will open the document for the recipient. Note (None, Optional or Mandatory) If the You may wish to use the optional note to request that the authoriser pay particular attention to a specific part of the document to be authorised. For instance, if it has been difficult to write or you know it is likely to be contentious or politically sensitive. Refresh (F5) button If you write an Optional Note then clicking on the Refresh button, will cause your note to appear in the body of the email so you can see what it will look like in the email. But if you write an Optional Note and don't click on the Refresh button then the system will still include your note in the email as it sends it. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Modifying the Email Templates - How To

title: Notify Paper Authorisers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Agenda Group > Authorise > Notify Paper Authorisers When you have a Paper open, and it has been saved at least once, clicking on the Authorisers button on the Infocouncil ribbon and selecting Notify Authorisers , opens the Paper Authorisers window. The Paper Authorisers window displays a list of the paper authorisers and buttons to facilitate the authorisation process. Authoriser Names of authorisers for this particular document are listed. Status There are four options: · To Be Notified · Notified · Authorised · Returned Date Date the status was last updated. History... Lists the date and changes to status that a selected user has made. Notify... Clicking on the Notify button opens the Create Mail form - a pre-formatted email to the selected authoriser. You can add an optional note before sending the email. Override Authorisation If a user has access rights to this button they can override the selected authoriser to authorise the report. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Reverse Authorisation If a user has access rights to this button they can reverse the status and return it to To Be Notified. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. New Authoriser Click to add a new authoriser. Delete Authoriser Select an authoriser then click to remove them. Override All If a user has access rights to this button they can override all authorisers to get the report to a fully authorised state. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Reverse All If a user has access rights to this button they can undo all authorisations and return the report to a completely unauthorised state. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Close The Close button closes the Report Authorisers form and returns you to the report.

title: Notify Report Authorisers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Authorise > Notify Report Authorisers When you have a Report open, and it has been saved at least once, clicking on the Authorisers button on the Infocouncil ribbon and selecting Notify Authorisers , opens the Report Authorisers window. The Report Authorisers window displays a list of the report authorisers and buttons to facilitate the authorisation process. Authoriser Names of authorisers for this particular report are listed. To edit the list close this form and go to the Report Data Entry form. Status There are four options: · To Be Notified · Notified · Authorised · Returned Date Date the status was last updated. History... Lists the date and changes to status that a selected user has made. Notify... Clicking on the Notify button opens the Create Mail form - a pre-formatted email to the selected authoriser. You can add a note before sending the email. Override Authorisation Overrides the selected authoriser and authorises the report. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Reverse Authorisation Reverses the status and returns it to To Be Notified. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Email Author... Clicking on the Email Author button opens the Create Mail form - a pre-formatted email to the author. Override All Overrides all authorisers to get the report to a fully authorised state. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Reverse All Reverses all authorisations and returns the report to a completely unauthorised state. Only visible if you have the rights to use it. Close The Close button closes the Report Authorisers form and returns you to the report.

title: Other
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other When you have a report open, click the Other ribbon menu to list other options. Defer/Refer A report may be deferred or referred to another meeting. See the Defer/Refer a Report section. Include In Meeting Once a report is fully authorised, this marks the report for inclusion in a meeting. (Same as marking it for inclusion on the Create Paper window.) This option is available to administrators only. See the Include in Meeting section. Set Item No Enters a different item number for the report than the one automatically allocated by the system. This is only possible if the report type allows it (the Update Item Number flag must be checked in the Agenda Item Maintenance screen). See the Set Item No section. Unprotect Unprotects the report. A password is required. See the Unprotect section.

title: Other Tool
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other Tool When you have a Minutes document open, click the Other ribbon menu to see more tools that can be used when taking minutes. The options on this menu provide minute takers with tools that can be used when completing a Minutes document. They are designed to assist rapid minute taking. Move item Displays the Move Item form to facilitate moving an item to another part of the document. Do not copy and paste items use this tool instead. See the Moving Item section. Block Resolve Displays the Block Resolve form to allow multiple items to be resolved at once. Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl-Shift-B. See the Block Resolve section. Attachments Opens Attachments form from a Minutes document so items tabled at a meeting can be added to the Minutes. Note: to take advantage of this feature a new script will need to written to produce the Minutes Attachment document please ask us. See the Attachments section. Member Names Displays a form prompting for member names to be inserted into the document at the cursor point. Names will be inserted in the the organisation's format. See the Member Names section. Mover/Seconder Displays a form prompting for a Mover and a Seconder. The mover/seconder text will to be inserted (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the Mover/Seconder section. Minute Number Inserts a new Minute Number (in the organisation's format) at the cursor point. See the Minute Number section. Reset Minute Numbers Displays a form prompting for a new starting minute (or resolution) number. If an organisation includes minute numbers in its Minute documents, this option will start the numbers from the entered value. See the Reset the Minute Numbers section. Prevent Actions Generation Displays a message giving a choice to prevent the minute item at the cursor from having actions automatically generated for it or not. Available only if the system parameter Papers - Show Prevent Actions Generation Minutes Tool is set to True. See the Prevent Actions Generation section.

title: Outline
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Agenda Group > Outline When any business paper is open, clicking the Outline button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Outline form. The Outline tool helps navigate through the document as it is being built, and assists with moving and copying items if they have been moved out of order during the meeting. List Type The type of item: Report - a report item that has been put to the meeting and included during the generation of the Minutes. New Item - added at the meeting using the New Item tool. Set Item - added at the meeting using the Motion tool. Resolved Whether resolved or not. Item The item's title. Go to item Click to go to the beginning of the selected item. Alternatively, double-click on an item or select an item and press Alt+G. Go to start of recommendation Click to go to the start of the recommendation in the selected item. Alternatively, press Alt+O. Go to end of recommendation Click to go to the end of the recommendation in the selected item. Alternatively, press Alt+T. Include headings Check to include headings in the list. Alternatively, press Alt+U. Include attachments (will show for documents that have attachments) Check to include attachments in the list. Alternatively, press Alt+L. Close Click to the window. Alternatively, press Alt+C.

title: Page Number Reset
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Agenda Group > Page Number Reset When an administrator has any business paper open, clicking the Page Number Reset button on the Infocouncil ribbon initiates the page numbering reset function. Re-paginates the document based on the number of pages entered for each attachment. Used by councils who hand insert their attachments between reports in the Agenda. OK Re-paginates the document. Cancel Closes the window and returns you to the paper.

title: Prevent Actions Generation
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Prevent Actions Generation When an administrator has a minutes document open, and has their cursor at a minute item, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Prevent Actions Generation opens a message dialog. The option will only appear if the system parameter Papers - Show Prevent Actions Generation Minutes Tool is set to True. The dialog allows the minute taker to flag the minute item at the cursor, to prevent actions from being automatically generated during the minutes upload process. If the minute item is already flagged to prevent actions from being automatically generated, then the dialog will allow the user to stop the prevention. OK Click to prevent or stop prevention. Cancel Click to exit the form without affecting the minute item. See Also Generate Actions - Concept

title: Preview Attachments
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Preview Attachments When you have a report open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Preview Attachments opens the Cover Sheet Report window. This report allows you to quality check your report and its attachments to see how they will look when compiled into the agenda. It also give you an opportunity to check that you attached the attachments that you intended, and that you formatted them correctly. Include Cover Sheet If ticked then a page of statistics will precede your report. It contains such details as: · Meeting Details · Report Type · Agenda Section · Subject · Keywords · Creation and Last Saved dates and names · File Name and Directory · Word, Character and Attachment counts Append Attachments If ticked then all the attachments will appear after the report. If un-ticked then only the report will appear. OK Click to produce the report. It appears as a "throw-away" document in Word format. If you notice something you wish to change, remember to close the "throw-away" document then you edit your actual report. Cancel Closes the Cover Sheet Report window without creating a report.

title: Procedural Motion Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Procedural Motion Types Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Procedural Motion Types opens the Procedural Motion Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add and edit procedural motion types. Search Criteria Search by name Type part of the name of a procedural motion then click on the Search button. List all Motion Types Select to view a list of all procedural motion types. Show Inactive Check to include inactive procedural motion types. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of procedural motion types matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new procedural motion type to be entered. Delete Select a procedural motion type in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the procedural motion. Details Name Name of the Procedural Motion, as it will be seen by the minute taker when adding a procedural motion into the minutes. Text The text to appear when the motion type is inserted into a Minutes document. Tags that can be added to the text to substitute the matching text <LF> - New Line <TAB> - Tab <NAME OF MEETING> - the name of the meeting <MOVERTITLE> - the title at the meeting of the mover <SECONDERTITLE> - the title at the meeting of the seconder <MOVERLASTNAME> - the last name of the mover <MOVERLASTNAME,U> - the last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERLASTNAME> - the last name of the seconder <SECONDERLASTNAME,U> - the last name of the seconder in upper case <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME> - the initials and last name of the mover <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME,U> - the initials and last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME> - the initials and last name of the seconder <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME,U> - the initials and last name of the seconder in upper case <MOVERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME> - the first name and last name of the mover <MOVERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME,U> - the first name and last name of the mover in upper case <SECONDERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME> - the first name and last name of the seconder <SECONDERFIRSTNAMELASTNAME,U> - the first name and last name of the seconder in upper case For example the text: Moved by <MOVERTITLE> <MOVERINITIALSLASTNAME><LF>Seconded by <SECONDERTITLE> <SECONDERINITIALSLASTNAME> Would be interpreted as: Moved by Cr F Jones Seconded by Cr B Smith Active Checked when Procedural Motion Type is active. Default Actions When a minutes is uploaded, any items for a procedural motion type will have actions automatically created for it if there are default actions defined here. Any default action will have the action officer automatically be the user uploading the minutes. Should it need changing that user can change it from the Generate Actions form by highlighting the minute item, clicking the Action Sheets button and making the

title: Process Destination Folders
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Process Destination Folders Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Process Destination Folders opens the Process Destination Folders window. Only used by EDRMS integrated organisations. This form provides the means for an administrator to run a process that will move all documents and their EDRMS records associated with the meeting from the Draft Location to the Official Location. This process is run after the meeting has been finalised (including the generation of Action Sheets). If the system in your organisation has been set up with Draft and Official Locations , then this provides you with the ability to have Initial and Final Containers for Reports, Agendas, Minutes and Attachment documents. Both Locations will have default folders set up. When saving an Infocouncil document a user will need to nominate two save locations Container/Folder (Draft Location) and Destination (Official Location). Normally the user will just accept the defaults. On Save of the document a record will be created in the Draft Location. Once the meeting has been finalised (including the generation of Action Sheets), the Administrator will run the process that moves all documents and their EDRMS records associated with the meeting from the Draft Location to the Official Location.

title: Publish
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish If you have sufficient user rights, you will see the Publish ribbon menu on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Publish ribbon menu provides administrators with tools to create and publish both HTML and PDF papers to various locations including the web and LG Hub. Publish Opens the Publish Business Papers window. See the Publish Business Papers section. Create HTML Creates an HTML version of a Business Paper that you have open on your desktop. See the Create HTML Document section. Create PDF Creates an HTML version of a Business Paper that you have open on your desktop. See the Create PDF Document section. Un-Publish Opens the Un-Publish Business Papers window. See the Un-Publish section.

title: Publish Business Papers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish > Publish Business Papers Clicking on the Publish ribbon menu and choosing Publish opens the Publish Business Papers window. This form provides an administrator with the functionality to upload an HTML or PDF version of a business paper to the web, your network, SharePoint or to LG Hub. LG Hub is only available to those organisations using the LG Hub product supplied by the third party Task Exchange. Automatic notification to subscribers is also possible from this form. The grid displays a row for each PDF and/or HTML version of a paper available. The first three columns describe the paper and the last column provides a button to delete the paper version. Other columns appear based on what publish destinations have been configured in the Publish Destinations window. This columns have a column heading that matches the Name of the publish destination as defined in the same window. Within these columns, some cells will be disabled if the way the destination has been configured prohibits a paper from publishing to it. It might do this based on the version's format being HTML or PDF, whether the paper is open or confidential (closed), or draft, or depending which meeting type the paper is for. In addition to using the Publish Destinations window to configure the destinations, the Meeting Publish Destinations allows some configuration to be overridden for individual meetings. Destination Information Click to display some information about each of the publish destinations configured in the system. List Paper Name of the paper. Meeting Date Date and time of the meeting. Type Type of document - whether HTML or PDF. Other Columns - Publish Destinations Check the box for each paper you wish to publish to the column's publish destination, or check the box in the column header to select or deselect all papers in a column. Disabled cells will never be checked. If the destination type is to LG Hub, then its column is always followed by a Channel column, allowing control over which channel each paper is published to. This deletes the converted HTML or PDF version of the paper. It does not delete the original paper. Once you have finished publishing a paper you can click the trash can button at the end of the row to delete the converted version of it. Alternatively, you can check 'Delete converted files after publish' to automatically delete the converted version of the paper as it is published. Progress Log During publishing, the progress is displayed in the progress log. The progress log can be expanded or contracted by hovering the mouse over the grey bar below the Cancel button, clicking and dragging up or down (the bar is invisible until the drag operation begins). Cancel This button appears during publishing and allows you to cancel the publishing. The process will stop when it is suitable to do so. Cancelling does not remove any already published papers. Look at the log to determ

title: Publish Destinations
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Publish Destinations Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Publish Destinations opens the Publish Destinations window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit publish destinations. Any number of destinations may be defined for any available type in the Type field drop down. Search Criteria Search by name Search by any part of the publish destination name. List all Publish Destinations Select to view a list of all publish destinations. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of publish destinations matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new publish destination to be entered. Delete Select a publish destination in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the publish destination. Details The Details Section contains two tabs showing all the details for a publish destination . Details tab The Details tab is illustrated above. Name A name to identify the publish destination. It must be unique for all publish destinations defined in the system. Description A description to provide further detail about the publish destination. Type The type must be entered and is restricted to "WebAPI", "FTP", "File Server", "SharePoint" or "LG Hub". This identifies the type of destination being defined. Infocouncil supports these types only. When the type is first selected the Configuration tab will change from a blank tab to a set of fields that supports Infocouncil's implementation of the type. If the type is subsequently changed, the user will be warned (as below) that any configuration fields values will be cleared and a different set of fields will appear for the new type. Valid Meetings Lists all meetings in the system. Those checked are permitted to publish to this destination. By default all meetings are valid for a new destination. Those unchecked will not be able to publish to this destination. Going into the Meeting maintenance form, selecting a meeting and clicking the Publish Destinations button will open the Meeting Publish Destinations maintenance form. It will automatically list all destinations that have the meeting checked as valid. From that screen a publish destination can be made invalid for the meeting by "deleting" the destination. It does not actually delete the publish destination. That is only possible from within this form. Adding a publish destination in that form, allows the user to choose an already defined publish destination for which the meeting is currently invalid. Once added the meeting will be valid and will appear checked in this screen. So a meeting can be made valid or invalid for a publish destination in both places. Allows PDF If checked then PDF papers may be published to the destination. Allows HTML If checked then HTML

title: Reference Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Reference Types Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Reference Types opens the Reference Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add, edit or delete reference types. Search Criteria Search by name Enter all or part of the name and click Search. List all Reference Types Select to view a list of all reference types. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of meetings matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new reference type to be entered. Delete Select a reference type in the list so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the reference type. Details The Details Section has 2 tabs containing all the details for an individual reference type . General tab The General tab contains basic information about the reference type. Label This is the unique name of the reference type. Version Label On the Report Data form, when this reference type is selected, the label for its versions will be changed to this. Item Label On the Report Data form, when this reference type is selected the label for its list of items will be changed to this. Versions The list of versions currently defined for the reference type. Buttons Up Arrow Click to move the selected version after the next one. This controls the order the versions appear in the Report Data screen. After reordering the Save button must be clicked to persist the change. Down Arrow Click to move the selected version before the prior one. This controls the order the versions appear in the Report Data screen. After reordering the Save button must be clicked to persist the change. Delete (Cross) Click to delete the selected version. After deleting the Save button must be clicked to persist the change. Import New Click to import a new version for the reference type. Importing is not possible if the reference type has unsaved changes. New Click to add a new version for the reference type. Adding a new version is not possible if the reference type has unsaved changes. Edit Click to edit the selected version. Editing a version is not possible if the reference type has unsaved changes. Report Types The Report Types tabs contains detail of which report types may reference this reference type and in what way. Select report types to use this reference type Any report types selected here will see this reference type be available on the Report Data screen to associate a report of the type with. Edit the reference type settings for the selected report type Select a report type to edit the reference type settings for the report type. Reference type order All reference types that can be reference by the selected report type will be listed here. The order of the reference types, as they appear on the Report Data screen when the report

title: Reference Type Versions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Reference Types > Reference Type Versions Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Reference Types opens the Reference Types window. Search for, or list all reference types and select one to import a reference type version for. Either c licking on the New or Edit buttons opens the Reference Type Version window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view or edit a reference type version. Versions can also be imported. Label Enter a label for the version unique to the reference type. This might be the year the version relates to for a master plan or it might be anything that identifies this version from other versions for the reference type. Active Active versions will be available to choose from in the report data screen. Unchecked (inactive) versions will no longer be visible. Make a version inactive if you no longer wish report authors to identify reports with this version. Start Date Leave blank or provide a meeting date from which this version is valid for report authors to identify reports with this version. Invalid versions will not be visible on the Report Data screen. End Date Leave blank or provide a meeting date after which this version is no longer valid for report authors to identify reports with this version. Invalid versions will not be visible on the Report Data screen. Items The list of items in the version. The list may be a flat or hierarchical list. A hierarchical list may be any depth and any item in the list may contain any number of items. Select an item in the list to edit its details. Arrow Buttons (or Alt - Arrow key) Reposition the selected item by using the arrows. Up moves it above its prior sibling. Down moves it below its next sibling. Left moves it as the next sibling of its parent. Right moves it as child of its prior sibling. Be aware that rules for the reference type may exist specifying which levels in a hierarchical list are able to be selected from in the Report Data screen. Moving items to different levels may result in them becoming selectable or unselectable. Add Adds a new item to the list as a child to the currently selected item. Delete Deletes the selected item and all its descendants from the list. Delete is prevented if any reports exist that are associated with the item or its descendant items. Edit Selected Item Click an item in the list to be able to edit its details. List Number An optional list number for the item in any format desired. E.g. 1 or A or 1.a.i. a. Any list number would typically match the numbering used in the organisations source document (if any used at all). Maximum 30 characters. Be aware, if you already have a version with the Summary being prefixed with list numbers, separating the list numbers out into this field will have no benefit, unless the scripting to produce the report is also changed to utilise the List Number field. Summary This is the text that appears in t

title: Regenerate Deadlines
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Meeting Types > Regenerate Deadlines Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meeting Types opens the Meeting Types window. Modifying and saving a meeting type's deadlines, where there are meetings for the meeting type and there are deadline rules for this meeting type with mandatory time, opens the Regenerate Deadlines window. Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Meetings opens the Meetings window. Modifying the meeting's meeting type and saving, where there are deadline rules for the new meeting type with mandatory time, opens the Regenerate Deadlines window. This form provides the means to notify an administrator that deadlines will be regenerated for all meeting dates for one of more meetings, and provides the administrator with a means to specify a time value for each deadline rule with a mandatory time, for which each deadline generated for the rule will have that time. Meeting List Lists all the meetings that have the meeting type. Deadline Rule List Lists all the deadline rules that have a mandatory time for the meeting type. Deadline Rule Description The description of the deadline rule. Not editable. Default Time The time that will be applied to all deadlines generated for this rule. It is mandatory to provide a time for each rule in the list, otherwise Infocouncil cannot generate deadlines without a time for these rules. OK Click to close the form and continue with regenerating deadlines. Cancel Click to close the form and cancel the operation. See Also Deadline Types - Screen Help Meeting Types - Screen Help Meetings - Screen Help

title: Remote Minutes
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Remote Minutes Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Remote Minutes opens the Remote Minutes window. This form provides the means for an administrator to take the minutes offline ( away from the network and database), update remotely and then re-integrate back into the system. Multiple minutes documents can be taken offline. It is possible to override offline documents in the case where a laptop is lost, damaged or an unforeseen circumstance occurs that requires the documents to be no longer offline. Going Offline Search Criteria Search by Meeting type or choose to list all meetings. Top List Minutes available to be taken offline. Check-box Check to select meeting. Meeting Name of Meeting. Meeting Date Date of Meeting. Description Description of the Document. Special Whether special/extraordinary or ordinary. Closed Whether confidential or open. Record Id Record ID in the organisation's EDRMS. Taken Offline By Name of user who took the document offline. Bottom List Meeting Name of Meeting. Meeting Date Date of Meeting. Description Description of the Document. Special Whether special/extraordinary or ordinary. Closed Whether confidential or open. Record Id Record ID in the organisation's EDRMS. Buttons Remote Click to take the selected document(s) offline. Cancel Click to close window without taking any documents offline. Going Online List Minutes currently in an offline state. Meeting Name of Meeting. Meeting Date Date of Meeting. Description Description of the Document. Special Whether special/extraordinary or ordinary. Closed Whether confidential or open. Record Id Record ID in the organisation's EDRMS. Buttons Online Click to take all document(s) online. Cancel Click to close window without taking documents online. See Also Remote Minutes - Concept

title: Report Data
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Report Data Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Create Report button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Report Data Entry form. Also, clicking on the Report Data button while in a report opens the same Report Data Entry form. This form allows you to enter or edit the meta data for your report. NOTE: The example shown above is indicative only. The Report Data Entry form is customised for the specific requirements of each organisation. Some controls are mandatory and will always appear (such as Meeting, Meeting Date and Authors). Others will be dependent on the organisation's requirements. The data that can be captured here is quite extensive with provision for up to four tabs. The labels for the fields could be quite different at your organisation and only the major term for the field and common aliases are listed. The data captured, the report's meta data, can be used to automatically customise the resulting report through the scripts. That is, the scripts can take advantage of the meta data in anyway the organisation desires to affect the content of their reports. Common fields There are a few fields that your organisation will most certainly have. Meeting Select the name of the meeting (or committee) that this report is for. Meetings are defined in Meetings Maintenance . Meeting Date Select the date of the meeting to which you wish your report to be put. Dates are loaded for the selected meeting for the last nine months and onwards. The date defaults to the next meeting date for the nominated meeting. Meeting Dates are defined in Meeting Dates . Authors Select the name of the person responsible for creating the content of the report. This person is normally the "author" or "report writer" but it can also be the name of a "manager" if a "typist" or "personal assistant" is entering the report on behalf of their "manager". There must be at least one, but an unlimited number may be selected. Clicking on the up and down arrows re-orders the authors. Highlighting one and clicking on the X button deletes that author. Authors are defined in Authors . Authorisers (common aliases: Approvers) Zero to unlimited authorisers may be selected, the report type may force at least one to be entered. The names of the default authorisers appear as soon as the name of an author is chosen. However, if the authorisers are incorrect, or one of them is known to be away or on leave, then the default authorisers can be deleted and others added. Clicking on the up and down arrows re-orders the authorisers. Highlighting one and clicking on the X button deletes that author. See the Report Authorisations concept topic for more details. Report Type (common aliases: Agenda Item) Report types are created for each organisation according to their requirements. The report types available for a meeting will be dependent on what meeting has been nominated. Report templates are created for each report type to accurately re

title: Report Detail
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Document Menu > Report Detail When you open the Document Menu form and select a report then click on the Detail button then the View Report Detail window opens in a view only mode. When you open the Create Paper form and select a report then click the Details button then the Edit Report Detail window opens in an edit mode. The Report Detail windows allow you to view (or edit) details about an individual report without the need to open the actual report. View Report Detail form - a view only window. Edit Report Detail form - fields in the "You may edit these fields" section may be edited by administrators via the Create Paper Menu. Report ID This is the internal, unique identifier for the report. Generally this is of no value to users but it can be useful when dealing with a support issue to track down problems with a certain report. Report No The order in which reports were created for the meeting. Item No The item number of the report. In most cases this will be blank until the agenda is created. The first field is the raw number and may be edited in edit mode. The second field is the number formatted to the organisation's requirement. It may not be edited. Date Created Date the report was created. Referred/Deferred From... Resolved Checked if the item was resolved in the previous meeting. Meeting Name of meeting from which the report has come. Meeting Date Date of meeting from which the report has come. Meeting The name of meeting to which the report has been put. Meeting Date The date of meeting to which the report has been put. Report Type The type of report. Section The section in the agenda that the report is currently assigned to. Title/Subject The title of the report, as typed in the Report Data Entry window, and as it will appear in the meeting documents. File Path If a document management system is not being used, the path the report resides in. Author The name of the first author of the report, as listed in the Report Data Entry window. Typist The network login of the person who entered the report. Normally this will be the author, but it can, for instance, be a personal assistant who entered the report on behalf of their manager. File No If a document management system is used, its record number. Order No The order allocated to the report for it to appear in the agenda. Save Allows an administrator to save changes. Only appears on the Editable window . Undo Allows an administrator to reverse any changes they have made. Only appears on the Editable window . Close Click this button to close the Report Detail window and return to where called from.

title: Report Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group Whenever you open a Report Infocouncil displays the Report group on the Infocouncil Ribbon. The Report Group provides users with tools to be used when creating or editing a report document. For instance, they enable the user to modify a report's details, make it confidential, add attachments and move the report through the authorisation process. Tool Description Changes the details of the report. See the Report Data section. Makes the report confidential and choose the relevant clauses of the Act. In your organisation the system may also be configured for you to enter a reason and/or a review date. See the Confidential Reasons section. Add, edit or delete attachments from the attachments list in the report. See the Attachments section. Notify Authorisers : Displays the list of authorisers for a report. Authorisers may be notified using this function. See the Notify Authorisers section. Authorise : Allows an Authoriser to approve a report. See the Authorise or Return a Report section. Return : Allows an Authoriser to return a report. See the Authorise or Return a Report section. Email Report Link: Email an officer with details of the active report. There may be a link in the email that opens the report in Word. See the Email Report Link section. Clone : Allows any user to clone a report for this or another meeting. See the Clone a Report section. Save : Saves the report and commit details of the report to the database. See the Save section. Modify and Save : Allows the EDRMS to be updated with a different container, a memo/note or a new revision. For EDRMS based councils. See the Save section. Save As : Saves the report as another document (this will no longer be an Infocouncil document) See the Save section. Preview Attachments : Creates a copy of the report which may contain cover sheet page of details and statistics about the report and/or a copy of all the attachments for a report. See the Preview Attachments section. Go To: Go to the start or end of document, or go to the recommendation. See the Go To Recommendations section. Report Notes: When you have a Report open, clicking Report Notes from the Other options opens the Report Notes window. The Report Notes option allows any number of notes to added to a report. See the Report Notes section. Defer/Refer : Allows an administrator to defer or refer the report to another meeting. See the Defer/Refer a Report section. Include/Exclude Report : Once a report is fully authorised, marks the report for inclusion in a meeting. (Same as marking it for inclusion on the Create Paper window.) This option is available to administrators only. See the Include in Meeting section. Set Item No : Enters a different item number for the report than the one automatically allocated by the system. See the Set Item No section. Unprotect : Unprotects the report. A password is required. See the Unprotect section.

title: Reporting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting Click on the Reporting ribbon menu to choose reports useful for administrators. For these reports: · Templates must be present in the templates folder. If missing, a message will be displayed. Please contact Infocouncil to organise templates. · Each report may have multiple layouts. · The layout of each may be added to and configured by your organisation. Infocouncil supplies two default reports: · List Reports for a Meeting · Minutes Index Report · List Minute Items for a Meeting · Meeting Timetable · Yearly Meeting Schedule · Meeting Events · Authors Report

title: Report Notes
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other > Report Notes When you have a Report open, clicking Report Notes from the Other options opens the Report Notes window. The Report Notes option allows any number of notes to added to a report: Copy Copy the list of notes into the clipboard. Add Add a new note. The note screen will be displayed (type the note and click OK to save it): Edit Edit the highlighted note. The is available if the user has either the 'Rights to Edit or Delete All Report Notes' OR the 'Rights to Edit or Delete Own Report Notes' security option. Delete Delete the highlighted note. The is available if the user has either the 'Rights to Edit or Delete All Report Notes' OR the 'Rights to Edit or Delete Own Report Notes' security option. Close Close the window. Reporting on Notes Report Notes can be included in the List Reports report. Please see: List Reports for a Meeting Modifying and Creating Report Templates List Reports from a Meeting Tags

title: Reset Minute Numbers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Other > Reset Minute Numbers When an administrator has a minutes document open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Reset Minute Numbers opens the Reset minute number form. The Reset Minute Numbers tool allows the minute taker to reset the numbers from the beginning of the document or from the cursor point onwards. New Starting Number Number the minute taker wishes to begin with. Start numbering... From the beginning of the document Resets all the minute numbers in the whole document. From the cursor point onwards Resets only those minute numbers from the cursor point forwards. OK Click to reset the minute numbers. Cancel Click to exit the form without resetting the minute numbers.

title: Resolution
