text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Resolution When a minutes document is open, clicking the Resolution button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-1 ) opens the Insert Resolution for [Item number and Title] form. The Resolution tool allows the minute taker to choose a mover and seconder and insert a minute/resolution number. Insert Minute Number / Resolve item fully The label is customised for your organisation in the "Text for check box on resolution screen" system parameters under Papers. Most commonly it is used to cause a minute number to be inserted with the resolution. It could be used for other purposes such as causing the item to be resolved fully. Check the box or alternatively, hit the space-bar. Choose a mover... Click the radio button beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. Choose a seconder... Click the radio button beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. OK Closes the window and edits the recommendation in the Minutes document to change it into a resolution. Cancel Discards any changes you made then closes the window and returns you to your Minutes document.

title: Return To Last Item
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Clauses Group > Return To Last Item Clicking on the Return to Last Item button on the Infocouncil ribbon moves the cursor back to the last condition that you edited. The Return to Last button is useful when you have scrolled to another part of the document before editing was completed and wish to return to the previous position. When you return to the last item it is all selected. You will need to place your cursor in the field you wish to edit before beginning to type.

title: Return To Meeting
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Return To Meeting When a minutes document is open, clicking the Return To Meeting button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-6 ) opens the Who returned to the meeting form. The Return To Meeting tool allows the minute taker to quickly enter a statement noting the time and name of the member who returned. Infocouncil then marks them as active for the purposes of moving, seconding or voting. Select Click the check box beside their name. Alternatively, type the letter that is beside their name. OK Click to complete the process. Text similar to the illustration below (but in the organisation's wording and format) will be appear wherever the cursor was positioned: Cancel Click to exit without making any changes. See Also Left the Meeting

title: Roles
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Roles Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Roles opens the Roles window. If an organisation is using a file-based (non EDRMS) environment or HPRM/HPCM environment (no other EDRMS) and they have chosen to store confidential documents based on Roles, then this form provides the means for an administrator to set up Roles. For HPRM/HPCM, the system parameter EDRMS - Apply Record Level Security, must be set to true in order for roles to have any effect. Search Criteria Search by name Searches by any part of the role's name. List all Roles Click to search for a list of all roles. Search Searches for roles matching the selected criteria. List New Click on the New button to clear the details ready for a new role to be entered. Edit Select a role in the list above so its details appear on the right where you can edit them. Delete Select a role in the list above so its details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the role. You should ONLY delete a role if you accidentally enter one twice or make some similar mistake. If a role has ever been used then instead of deleting them you should mark them as Inactive. Details Name Name of the role. Folder Click the folder button beside the Folder field to locate the folder to which documents are to be saved. Active Check to indicate the role is active. Uncheck for inactive. Save Click to save changes you have made to an individual role. Undo If you have not already saved your changes, you can click the Undo button to undo any changes you have made for an individual role. Cancel Click to cancel out of a role without saving any changes you made. Close Closes the window. See Also How To - Use Roles With HPCM

title: Row Above
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Row Above When a minutes document is open and the cursor positioned inside a Recommendation row (or any row in the items table), clicking the Row Above button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-UpArrow ) inserts a row above the row where the cursor is currently positioned. Infocouncil demands that the whole of a Resolution must be contained within a single cell. However, a single Recommendation may be split up into multiple rows and resolved in parts. Or while an item is being discussed you may wish to enter some narrative, a division, an amendment or a procedural motion. To do any of these you should use the Row Above or Row Below tools.

title: Row Below
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Minutes Group > Row Below When a minutes document is open and the cursor positioned inside a Recommendation row (or any row in the item's table), clicking the Row Below button on the Infocouncil ribbon (or pressing Ctrl-DownArrow ) inserts a row below the row where the cursor is currently positioned. Infocouncil demands that the whole of a Resolution must be contained within a single cell. However, a single Recommendation may be split up into multiple rows and resolved in parts. Or while an item is being discussed you may wish to enter some narrative, a division, an amendment or a procedural motion. To do any of these you should use the Row Above or Row Below tools.

title: Save
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save The Infocouncil save button and save button menu looks like this. The menu will hide options that the system has not been configured for your. Word's standard save methods are also available. Save When you have an Infocouncil document open it can be saved by the following means · Click the Save button in the Infocouncil Ribbon · Click the Save button in the Quick Access Toolbar · Select File-Save · Press Ctrl-S, Word's default shortcut key combination · Click the Save ribbon menu in the Infocouncil Ribbon and then click the Save button under there Modify and Save This option is only available to those integrated to an EDRMS (either directly or for export purposes only). The initial save process for an Infocouncil document to an EDRMS involves determining the location within the EDRMS to store the document. Thereafter, saving will no longer provide the opportunity to alter the location. But, Modify and Save will provide the opportunity. Invoke this by · Click the Save ribbon menu in the Infocouncil Ribbon and then click the Modify And Save button under there Save As Depending on how the system parameter called 'Prevent "Save As" for report' is set, this option may be prevented in your organisation. Saving a copy of an Infocouncil document makes an exact copy of the document which Infocouncil will not recognise as an Infocouncil document. This can be done by the following means · Select File-Save As · Press F12, Word's default shortcut key combination · Click the Save ribbon menu in the Infocouncil Ribbon and then click the Save As button under there The Save Process For saves other than the first save, the user will receive no feedback. The document will simply be saved just as a regular Word document would be. For the initial save (or for a Modify and Save) the save process varies depending on how Infocouncil is configured. If your organisation is configured to save to an EDRMS A screen is presented that shows the default location that the document will be saved to within the EDRMS. It allows the user to modify the default location including being able to search for a location within the EDRMS. See the relevant link below for further detail and more options. Once the location is okay, the save process continues. On completion a message will be displayed showing the new EDRMS Saving To TRIM Saving To HPRM Saving To CM Saving To InfoXpert Saving To Objective If your organisation is configured to save to a File-based System and also to be able to export to an EDRMS You will be presented with a search screen specific to the EDRMS to allow you to select how or where the document will be located in the EDRMS when a copy of it is exported. See the relevant link below for further detail. Typically organisations configured this way prepare their reports, generate their business papers, conduct their meeting and once the minutes is finalised, all reports and business papers for the meeting

title: Saving To CM
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To CM Where Infocouncil is integrated to versions of CM (Content Manager) that are above or equal 10.0, saving a document for the first time will display the Save to Content Manager screen. This screen will also be displayed if Modify And Save is clicked from the Save ribbon menu that appears whenever an Infocouncil document is open. This form allows you to change the default container (or folder) that the document will be saved to within CM. It also allows some other meta data to be provided. On clicking OK the save process will continue. Meeting Type Name of Meeting. Read only. Meeting Date Date of Meeting. Read only. Special Meeting Checked if meeting is special/extraordinary. Read only. Closed Meeting Checked if meeting is closed/confidential. Read only. Author Name of first author. Read only. Title Title/Subject of document. Read only. Record Number Record number. Read only. This will be blank on the first save. Container/Folder This will be defaulted as defined on the EDRMS Parameters form available from the Meeting Maintenance screen . Enter a Container/Folder number or use the default. To search CM for a Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Container/Folder field. Alternates This is only displayed if the system parameter Show Alternate Locations is True. CM allows records to be not only contained by one folder but also to be alternately contained in other folders. Enter a Container/Folder number or to search CM for a Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Alternate Container/Folder field. Then click the Add Alternate button (blue arrow) to add it to the list. To delete an alternate folder, select it from the list, then click the Delete Alternate button. Alternate folders are not mandatory unless the system parameter Alternate Locations Mandatory is set to True. This will not default except in the situation where a development planning application document, from CM, has been entered on the Report Data screen. In this case, that document's container will be listed here. That particular container may be removed here, however it will be re-added on the next save. Destination This is only displayed if the system parameter Show Destination Folders is True. When true an organisation utilises a draft folder (as identified in the Container/Folder field) and a final folder (as identified here). Documents will start their life in the draft folder and be moved to the destination folder via the Finalise screen typically when the meeting is finished. This will be defaulted as defined on the EDRMS Parameters form available from the Meeting Maintenance screen . Enter a destination Container/Folder number or use the default. To search CM for a Destination Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Container/Folder field. Comment Enter a comment (note) if desired, for the CM record. Create new revisio

Enter a Container/Folder number or use the default. To search HPRM for a Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Container/Folder field. Alternates This is only displayed if the system parameter Show Alternate Locations is True. HPRM allows records to be not only contained by one folder but also to be alternately contained in other folders. Enter a Container/Folder number or to search HPRM for a Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Alternate Container/Folder field. Then click the Add Alternate button (blue arrow) to add it to the list. To delete an alternate folder, select it from the list, then click the Delete Alternate button. Alternate folders are not mandatory unless the system parameter Alternate Locations Mandatory is set to True. This will not default except in the situation where a development planning application document, from HPRM, has been entered on the Report Data screen. In this case, that document's container will be listed here. That particular container may be removed here, however it will be re-added on the next save. Destination This is only displayed if the system parameter Show Destination Folders is True. When true an organisation utilises a draft folder (as identified in the Container/Folder field) and a final folder (as identified here). Documents will start their life in the draft folder and be moved to the destination folder via the Finalise screen typically when the meeting is finished. This will be defaulted as defined on the EDRMS Parameters form available from the Meeting Maintenance screen . Enter a destination Container/Folder number or use the default. To search HPRM for a Destination Container/Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Container/Folder field. Comment Enter a comment (note) if

title: Saving To InfoXpert
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To InfoXpert Where Infocouncil is integrated to InfoXpert, saving a document for the first time will display the Save to InfoXpert screen. This screen will also be displayed if Modify And Save is clicked from the Save ribbon menu that appears whenever an Infocouncil document is open. This form allows you to change the default folder that the document will be saved to within InfoXpert. It also allows some other meta data to be provided. On clicking OK the save process will continue. Meeting Type Name of Meeting. Read only. Meeting Date Date of Meeting. Read only. Special Meeting Checked if meeting is special/extraordinary. Read only. Closed Meeting Checked if meeting is closed/confidential. Read only. Author Name of first author. Read only. Title Title/Subject of document. Read only. Record Number Record number. Read only. This will be blank on the first save. Folder This will be defaulted as defined on the EDRMS Parameters form available from the Meeting Maintenance screen . Enter a Folder number or use the default. To search InfoXpert for a Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Folder field. Destination This is only displayed if the system parameter Show Destination Folders is True. When true an organisation utilises a draft folder (as identified in the Folder field) and a final folder (as identified here). Documents will start their life in the draft folder and be moved to the destination folder via the Finalise screen typically when the meeting is finished. This will be defaulted as defined on the EDRMS Parameters form available from the Meeting Maintenance screen . Enter a destination Folder number or use the default. To search InfoXpert for a Destination Folder click the Search Folders button at the end of the Folder field. Comment Enter a comment (note) if desired, for the InfoXpert record. Create new revision Check if you wish to create a new revision. View Click to view the InfoXpert meta data details via a native InfoXpert screen. OK Click to accept the folders specified and to continue to save your document. Cancel Click to close the window without saving your document. See Also Save Search InfoXpert Retaining Revisions and Restoring - How To

title: Search CM
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To CM > Search CM Note the below screen may differ depending on your version of CM. Where Infocouncil is integrated to CM (Content Manager), the CM Save screen allows containers to be searched for instead of directly entering them. This screen can also be invoked when a save is performed without first displaying a save screen, if Infocouncil is configured for CM but is saving to the file system, and the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. See Exporting to CM below. This form allows you to search a folder with CM. For details on this screen please see the CM documentation. Exporting to CM When a document is exported from Infocouncil to CM, CM needs to know what folder the document is to be contained in. Infocouncil provides the means to specify default folders. Sometimes this will not be enough so Infocouncil provides the means to allow an author to alter the default folder for each document. This feature is enabled when the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. The above form will be displayed, the folder can be decided on, which is then stored in the Infocouncil database until such time that the document is exported, when it will attach a copy of the document into the container within CM. See Also Save Saving To CM

title: Search DataWorks Connect
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Search DataWorks Connect When first saving an Infocouncil document or clicking the Modify and Save tool from the Save ribbon menu, the following form will appear. It will only appear if Infocouncil is configured for Dataworks (aka ECM) and the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. When a document is exported from Infocouncil to Dataworks, Dataworks needs to know what indexes to register the document against. Infocouncil provides the means to specify default indexes. Often this will not be enough so Infocouncil provides the means to allow an author to alter or add to the default index for each document. This feature is enabled when the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. The below form will be displayed, indexes can be decided on, they are then stored in the Infocouncil database until such time that the document is exported, when it will register a copy of the document against those indexes within Dataworks. Accept the defaulted selected index entries, or · Search via a document number for a document you know is registered against the indexes you want, enter the EMS number and click EMS No Search, or ... · Search with an index search by specifying an ECM index and entering some filter criteria and click Index Search. · Search results will be listed in the Matching Index Entries. Select the index entry(s) desired and then click Add. · Any selected index entries can be selected and deleted by clicking the delete button. Click okay to continue with the save process and store the selected indexes for when the document is exported. See Also Save

title: Search HPRM
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To HPRM > Search HPRM Note the below screen may differ depending on your version of HPRM. Where Infocouncil is integrated to HPRM (aka HP Records Manager or HP Content Manager or TRIM), the HPRM Save screen or TRIM Save screen allows containers to be searched for instead of directly entering them. This screen can also be invoked when a save is performed without first displaying a save screen, if Infocouncil is configured for HPRM but is saving to the file system, and the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. See Exporting to HPRM below. This form allows you to search a folder with HPRM. For details on this screen please see the HPRM documentation. Exporting to HPRM When a document is exported from Infocouncil to HPRM, HPRM needs to know what folder the document is to be contained in. Infocouncil provides the means to specify default folders. Sometimes this will not be enough so Infocouncil provides the means to allow an author to alter the default folder for each document. This feature is enabled when the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. The above form will be displayed, the folder can be decided on, which is then stored in the Infocouncil database until such time that the document is exported, when it will attach a copy of the document into the container within HPRM. See Also Save Saving To HPRM Saving To TRIM

title: Search InfoXpert
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To InfoXpert > Search InfoXpert Where Infocouncil is integrated to Objective, the InfoXpert Save screen allows folders to be searched for instead of directly entering them. This screen can also be invoked when a save is performed without first displaying a save screen, if Infocouncil is configured for InfoXpert but is saving to the file system, and the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. See Exporting to InfoXpert below. This form allows you to search a folder with InfoXpert by browsing through the folder hierarchy, selecting the desired folder then clicking OK. Exporting to InfoXpert When a document is exported from Infocouncil to InfoXpert, InfoXpert needs to know what folder the document is to be contained in. Infocouncil provides the means to specify default folders. Sometimes this will not be enough so Infocouncil provides the means to allow an author to alter the default folder for each document. This feature is enabled when the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. The above form will be displayed, the folder can be decided on, which is then stored in the Infocouncil database until such time that the document is exported, when it will attach a copy of the document into the folder within InfoXpert. See Also Save Saving To InfoXpert

title: Search Objective
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Save > Saving To Objective > Search Objective Where Infocouncil is integrated to Objective, the Objective Save screen allows folders to be searched for instead of directly entering them. This screen can also be invoked when a save is performed without first displaying a save screen, if Infocouncil is configured for Objective but is saving to the file system, and the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. See Exporting to Objective below. This form allows you to search a folder with Objective by browsing through the folder hierarchy, selecting the desired folder then clicking OK. Exporting to Objective When a document is exported from Infocouncil to Objective, Objective needs to know what folder the document is to be contained in. Infocouncil provides the means to specify default folders. Sometimes this will not be enough so Infocouncil provides the means to allow an author to alter the default folder for each document. This feature is enabled when the system parameter Allowed to Specify EDRMS Archive Location Per Document is set to True. The above form will be displayed, the folder can be decided on, which is then stored in the Infocouncil database until such time that the document is exported, when it will attach a copy of the document into the folder within Objective. title: Search by name
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Security Functions Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Security Functions opens the Security Function Maintenance window. The Security Function Maintenance window is used during the process of setting up Security. It allows the modification of which Security Functions belong to which Security Group and whether the function is allowed or blocked. Security Functions represent the various areas of functionality within Infocouncil, and allow for a very high degree of control over which user can access which function. Note: To modify the permissions for a group it is easier to do so on the Security Groups Screen . Search Criteria Search by name Choose Search by name then enter the text in the adjacent box. List all Security Functions Select "List all Security Functions" to view a list of all security functions. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of security functions matching your selection. List List of all the security functions Name Description Actions - Add New Action Sheet Users & Groups with this permission assigned are allowed to add new action sheets from the Generate Actions screen. Actions - Allow actions to be un-completed Users & Groups with this permission assigned are allowed to un-complete actions that are completed from the Minute Item screen when accessed from the Generate Actions screen (not from the Actions screen). Actions - Allow search of confidential actions Actions - permission to search all confidential actions Actions - Allow search of open actions Actions - permission to search all open actions Actions - Always display own actions on load of actions screen Actions - always display my own actions on load of the actions screen, else display all actions I am responsible for Actions - Can Update Locked Target Date Users & Groups with this permission assigned are allowed to update a locked target date. Actions - Change Estimated Completion Date Users & Groups with this permission assigned are allowed to change the estimated completion date. Actions - Change Revised Date For Future Reports Permission to change the Revised Date for actions that identify a future report Actions - Create Actions For Other Users Users & Groups with this permission assigned are allowed to add new action sheets for other users as opposed to themselves, from the Actions screen. Actions - Display advanced search options when form is opened Users & Groups with this permission assigned will see the advanced search options when the Actions form is opened. Actions - Display email an officer column Users & Groups with this permission assigned may email an officer directly (the Email button column is displayed). Actions - Edit or Delete Action Notes Actions - permission to edit or delete action notes (a person may enter a note but they will not be able to then edit or delete it without this

title: Security Groups
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Security Groups Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Security Groups opens the Security Group Maintenance window. The Security Group Maintenance window is used during the process of setting up Security. It allows the modification of which Security Functions belong to which Security Groups and whether the function is allowed or blocked for a group. It also allows the modification of which groups inherit functions from which other groups, and which groups they pass their functions on to. Security Groups define the broad types of users of the system. By default the system is delivered with groups for Administrators, Division Users and Authors. Search Criteria Search by name Choose Search by name then enter the text in the adjacent box. List all Security Groups Select "List all Security Groups" to view a list of all security groups. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of security groups matching your selection. List Sorting the List The up and down arrows beside the list allow adjustment to the order. New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new security group to be entered. Delete Select a group in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the security group. Details The Details Section has 2 tabs containing all the details for a individual security function. Details tab The Details tab contains the description of the security group and lists the security functions that the group has been explicitly granted or denied. Note: it only takes one disallow within a specific users hierarchy of security to block access to that function for the user. Description Name of Security Group currently selected. Security Functions Drop down list of all security functions from which you can select. Plus a list of security functions already allocated to the group and whether the functions are allowed (tick) or disallowed (no tick). Click the button to remove the selected security function. Membership tab The Membership tab provides maintenance of the inheritance relationships between the groups. I.e. which groups the current group inherits security from, and which groups it passes security information on to. Member Groups - inherit security from this group The group selected from the list on the left has some of the rights of the group listed in this section of the Membership tab. The specific rights to which they have access are listed on the Details tab. For example, in the first illustration, they can authorise or return both open and confidential reports for their division. Click the to remove the selected group. Member of Groups - security inherited from these groups The group selected from the list on the left has all the same rights as the group listed in this section of the Membership tab. For example, in the il

title: Select Cc Recipients
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Authorise > Notify Report Authorisers > Create Mail > Select Cc Recipients Clicking on the Cc button, on the Send Email form, will open the Select Recipients window. This form provides the means for a user to select Authors who have an email address recorded in the system. Available List of all Authors who have an email address recorded in the system. Select recipients and move between Available and Selected lists using the arrow buttons. Selected List of recipients / selected Authors. OK Click to accept the selections. Close Click to close the window.

title: Select Minute Items
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Generate Actions > Select Minute Items Clicking the Select button on the Generate Actions form opens the Select Minute Items form. This form provides the means to select all minute items in the Generate Actions form that match the criteria entered. Select By Section Use the drop-down to select as many sections as you like. Minute items that are for one of the sections entered will be selected on the Generate Actions form. If no sections are selected then minute items for any section will be selected. Select By Item Type Use the drop-down to select as by any or all Item Types: New Item, Set Item, Report. Minute items that are for one of the item types entered will be selected on the Generate Actions form. If no item types are selected then minute items for any item type will be selected. Select By Officer Use the drop-down to select as many officers as you like. Minute items that have actions for an officer for one of the sections entered will be selected on the Generate Actions form. If no officers are selected then minute items for any officer will be selected. Ok Click the Ok button to have the Generate Actions form select matching minute items, that is check the checkbox for the row. Cancel Click the Cancel button to return to the Generate Actions form without changing the selection.

title: Send Email
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Send Email Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Send Email link will open the Send Email window. This form provides the means for an administrator to send an email, with an attachment if required, to any Councillor/Member, Author or Subscriber who has an email address recorded in the system. Send To Councillors/Members Click to list all Councillors/Members (with email addresses recorded in the system) in the Available list.When members is selected the Nominated Meeting drop down becomes available. Selecting a meeting from this list will filter the available members to those associated the the meeting. To again show the full member list choose the blank entry at the top of the list in the drop down. Authors Click to list all Authors (with email addresses recorded in the system) in the Available list. Subscribers Click to list all Subscribers (with email addresses recorded in the system) in the Available list. When subscribers is selected the Nominated Meeting drop down becomes available to choose the required committee from which to select subscribers. Available List of all recipients who meet the criteria you selected in the Send To section. Select recipients and move between Available and Selected lists using the arrow buttons. Selected List of recipients Email Subject Subject of Email Email Attachment Type file path to attachment, if you wish to send an attachment to your selected recipients, or.... Search EDRMS Click to search for an attachment in your EDRMS, or.... Browse Click to search for an attachment in your file system. Body Type the text of the body of your email. Copy Send Result to Clipboard This will copy the results of the send operation to the clipboard. This is useful where an email may have failed to a number of recipients and you need to take some corrective action and resend to just those recipients. Send Email Button Click to send an email to each of the selected recipients. The names of other recipients do not appear in the To or Cc fields of the recipient's email. Close Click to close the window.

title: Send To Support - Additional Information
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Send To Support - Additional Information When exceptions (errors) occur, an error screen will appear to detail the exception. Clicking the Send to support button, brings the Additional Information form. This form provides an opportunity to send exception data to Infocouncil Support via an e-mail. Name Current user's name - read only. Title Current user's title - read only. Email Current user's email address - read only. Phone Best phone number for support staff to reach you on - optional. Area code must be entered, if the phone number is provided. Additional information An open text field where any additional information can be entered, that might be helpful and/or relevant to the exception report being sent - optional. How would you like to proceed? Select a course of action regarding what you would like to happen next. You may choose not to be contacted, or initiate a contact yourself, or be contacted by us. OK Click to send an e-mail. The e-mail will include the additional information you have chosen to provide, along with a technical data, including error logs and a screen shot. Cancel Closes the Additional Information window without sending an e-mail.

title: Set Item No
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other > Set Item No When an administrator has a report open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Set Item No opens the Set Item Number form. It provides an opportunity to enter a different item number for the report than the one automatically allocated by the system. Enter Report/Item No. Defaults to number already set. Can be edited. OK Accepts any change made on the screen and returns to report. The report must be saved to complete the item number update. Cancel Closes the Set Item Number form without making any changes and returns you to the report.

title: SharePoint Browse Dialog
text: Navigation: Screen Help > SharePoint Browse Dialog At various locations within the Infocouncil application there is an option to 'Browse...' for a particular resource. Where it is sensible to do so we have introduced the ability to browse your SharePoint tenancy for an appropriate resource. The SharePoint browse dialog operates in two modes: 1. Browse for and select a file. In this mode this dialog will appear as below: 2. Browse for, and select a folder. In this mode the dialog will appear as per below: The tree control in each dialog will display a list of SharePoint 'sites' accessible to the user within the given tenancy. Expanding these site nodes will display all accessible 'Document Libraries' and 'Sub-Sites' if available to the user within that site. Expanding the document library will show all folders contained within. If you are browsing in folder select mode then no contained files will display. When a folder is highlighted the user is able to select OK and the selected folder information will be returned to the caller. When browsing in file select mode, when a folder is selected the dialog will also display all contained files in list on the right. This list is able to be sorted in ascending or descending by any column. The OK button will not be enabled until a file is selected. When OK is selected the file information is returned to the caller. If at any time the user decides to browse for a file using the in-built Windows browse functionality to select a locally attached file, then the 'Browse local file system' button is provided that, when clicked, will close the SharePoint dialog and show the Windows browse dialog. Cancel will close the dialog and no information will be passed back to the caller.

title: Subscriber Notification Email
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish > Publish Business Papers > Subscriber Notification Email A notification email to subscribers is generated when a paper is published for a meeting. All subscribers will be blind carbon copy (Bcc) recipients. Should two papers for the same meeting be published at the same time, they will both appear in the email. Should a paper be published in both PDF and HTML format, they both will appear in the email. Should a paper be published to both the primary and the secondary FTP locations, and they are both set up for subscriber notifications, then two links will appear for the paper, one for each location. To This will default to blank but other recipients (email addresses) can be added here. Remember everyone receiving this email will see any recipients added here. Cc This will default to blank but other recipients (email addresses) can be added here. Remember everyone receiving this email will see any recipients added here. Bcc This will be populated with all subscribers who subscribe to the meeting that the papers being published are for. Recipients may be added, changed or deleted. Bcc recipients will not be visible to any other recipient. Subject This will default to something sensible which may be customised for your organisation (see Setting Up Subscriber Notifications ). The subject may also be modified in the email screen. Details/Body of Email This will default to something sensible which may be customised for your organisation (see Setting Up Subscriber Notifications ). It will · Likely inform you of details of the meeting to which the paper is for. · Likely link to the paper which can be clicked by the recipient to directly open the paper. · Possibly include an optional note from the sender. Note (None, Optional or Mandatory) Depending on the template that the email is based on, a compulsory note, an optional note or no note, may be entered. The template will dictate where a note will be positioned. Type in the note and click F5 to refresh the mail body to show the note in the email. Refresh (F5) button If you write a Note then clicking on the Refresh button, will cause your note to appear in the body of the email so you can see what it will look like in the email. But if you write a Note and don't click on the Refresh button then the system will still include your note in the email as it sends it. The email sent is based on a template which may be customised for your organisation. See Modifying the Email Templates . See Also Subscribers - Screen Help Subscriber Types Maintenance - Screen Help Setting Up Subscriber Notifications - How To Modifying the Email Templates - How To

title: Subscribers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Subscribers Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Open Database opens the Infocouncil database. On the Main Menu , click on the System Maintenance link to open a Systems Maintenance sub-menu, then click on the Subscribers link to open the Subscribers Maintenance window. This form provides the means for an administrator to view, add or edit lists of subscribers. Subscribers can be automatically notified by email when papers are published. See Setting Up Subscriber Notifications . Subscribers can form a mail list. See Send Mail . Subscribers can have individual cover sheets generated (if your organisations is configured to produce them). See Setup Cover Sheets . Search Criteria Search by name Searches by any part of the subscriber's name. List By Meeting Type Searches for all subscribers who are subscribing to the selected meeting type . List All Subscribers Lists all subscribers. Search Searches for subscribers matching the selected criteria . List Lists all subscribers matching the selected criteria. New Click on the New button to clear the details ready for a new subscriber to be entered. Delete Select a subscriber in the list above so their details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the subscriber. Details Tab Details of the subscriber selected in the list. Name Name of subscriber. This could be a person, or an organisation. Contact Name If the subscriber Name if an organisation then fill in the name of a contact person. Email Address Email address of subscriber. Subscriber Type Select a subscriber type. Meeting Types List of committees/meetings to which the subscriber subscribes. Address Tab Address of the subscriber selected in the list. Address Line 1 First line of subscriber's address. Address Line 2 Second line of subscriber's address. Suburb Subscriber's suburb. State Subscriber's state. Postcode Subscriber's postcode. Phone Phone number of subscriber. Fax Fax number of subscriber. Cover Sheet Name Name to appear on a business paper coversheet. Buttons Save Click to save changes you have made. Undo Click to undo any changes you have made. Close Click to close the window. See Also Subscriber Types Setting Up Subscriber Notifications - How To Send Mail Setup Cover Sheets

title: Subscriber Types
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Subscriber Types Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Subscriber Types opens the Subscriber Types window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add edit or delete subscriber types. The subscriber type is just a label which is not used by the system in any way, other than to enforce that a subscriber must be given a subscriber type. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new subscriber type to be entered. Edit Select a subscriber type in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Edit button to change the subscriber type. Delete Select a subscriber type in the list above so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the subscriber type. Details Name Name of the subscriber type. Buttons Save Saves changes to the Details of the selected subscriber type. Undo Undoes changes since you last saved to the selected subscriber type. Cancel Cancels the new or edit operation. Close Closes the window. See Also Subscribers Maintenance

title: System Parameters
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > System Parameters Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing System Parameters opens the System Parameters window. Infocouncil uses system parameters to customise the application so it can be adapted to each organisation without the need to change the system's code. For example, the EDRMS Document Naming Conventions or location to the document templates. Below is a table of all system parameters, their name and their description. Expand a parameter group to see all the parameters in that group. Select a parameter in the list to see its setting. The grey pane at the bottom of the window provides an explanation on the selected parameter. The pane can be increased or decreased in size by dragging the bar above it. Some parameters describe formats of titles, email subjects and other things. The Specifying a Parameter Format topic provides much detail on how to set these parameters. OK Click to close the form and save any changes you made. Cancel Click to close the form and discard any changes. System Settings Click to open the System Settings form. Absolute care must be taken when changing anything in this form. Actions Name Description Acknowledge Information Only In EDRMS On Commit If true, when a For Information action is committed to EDRMS, it will be finalised in EDRMS. If false then it will not be. Acknowledge Information Only In Infocouncil On Commit Or Email If true, when a For Information action is committed to EDRMS or emailed to the action officer, the action will have its acknowledgement date set to the current date. If false then it will remain un-acknowledged in Infocouncil. Acknowledge Unacknowledged Information Only On Delete If true then when deleting an Information Only action that is committed to EDRMS but not acknowledged, it will be attempted to be acknowledged in the EDRMS. Acknowledgement Note Required This indicates that a note is required as part of the Information Only action acknowledgement process. Actions visible on upload Actions will be visible on upload. They will immediately become available to the default responsible officer on the Actions screen. Allow actions attachments If true attachments may be added to actions. A button to allow this will appear on the Actions screen. Allow User Choice To Email Relevant Officers On Action Reallocation If true, when an action reallocation occurs the user will be given a choice whether to email the relevant officers. If false, then officers will be emailed with no option for the user. Allow User Defined Actions Allows users to create their own actions via the Actions form. Such actions do not relate to any minute item or resolution. Ask before reassigning actions when reallocating Ask before reassigning actions when reallocating. Note that when reallocating on the Actions screen the action may be reassigned to the logged in user in the EDRMS system and/or in Infocou

title: System Settings
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > System Parameters > System Settings Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing System Parameters opens the System Parameters window. Clicking on the System Settings button opens up the System Settings window. System settings are very high level settings that define the implementation of Infocouncil to your site. PLEASE do not change any settings in here unless guided by Infocouncil Support. Infocouncil will preconfigure these prior to implementing Infocouncil at your site and for most sites they will never change. System Key A key uniquely identifying your site from all other sites to Infocouncil. This is provided by Infocouncil. Site Name The name of your site. This is provided by Infocouncil. System Flag For use by Infocouncil staff only. Uses EDRMS Check to indicate the an EDRMS is used with Infocouncil. Unchecked means no EDRMS is used and a network file system is used instead. Select EDRMS Type Select the type of EDRMS being used with Infocouncil Buttons OK Click to save changes you have made. Undo Click to undo any changes you have made. Close Click to close the window.

title: TeamViewer
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > About Infocouncil > TeamViewer Clicking the Remote Support button on the About Infocouncil window opens a TeamViewer window. The TeamViewer window allows Infocouncil support to initiate a secure connection with the user so they can see the user's screen and provide support. Infocouncil will need to know Your ID and Password , as displayed on the left of the TeamViewer window. The Password is unique to the current TeamViewer session. None of the other options on the window are used during a direct support session. When Infocouncil has finished supporting you and they log off then the secure connection is broken and you can close the TeamViewer window.

title: Text Definitions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Maintenance > Text Definitions Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking on the Maintenance ribbon menu and choosing Text Definitions opens the Text Definitions window. This form provides the means for an administrator to add edit or delete individual blocks of text that can be inserted into Word document, for example Minutes. This is done using the Insert Text tool which is available from the Minutes ribbon and the general toolbar.It is used to insert any text based on choices made by either the Minute Taker or anyone else who wishes to insert blocks of predefined text (for instance in reports). An example of where this might be used is inserting multiple choice conflict of interest text into a Minutes document. Search Criteria Search by name Enter all or part of the name and click Search. List all text definitions Select to view a list of all text definitions. Search Having specified search criteria click on the Search button to display a list of meetings matching your selection. List New Click on the New button to clear the Details ready for a new text definition to be entered. Delete Select a text definition in the list so its Details appear on the right of the window. Then click the Delete button to delete the text definition. Details The Details Section has 2 tabs containing all the details for an individual text definition. General tab The General tab contains basic information about one text definition. Name The name of the text definition. Active If a text definition is no longer to be used, make it inactive by unchecking the box. Inactive text definitions remain in the system but no longer appear in Insert Text tools. Description A general description of the text to be inserted and where it might be used. Document The path and file name of a Word (.docx, .doc) or Text Document (.txt) containing the text to be inserted. Alternatively, the text can be entered directly on the second tab. If there is a path it will take precedence over any text. Both Document and Text can be left blank if the name of a script is entered in the Script field. See below for a list of tags that may be used in the text. Script Choose a script to run, either to create the text, or to act on the text after it has been inserted. Any scripts will need to be created by Infocouncil. Include in Minutes List If checked the text definition will be available in Minutes Tools. Select Member Only enabled if Include in Minutes List is checked. If checked a member will be prompted for when inserting the text in a Minutes document ( only available from the Minutes Tools ). Meeting Event Type Only enabled if Include in Minutes List is checked. Flags to the system that this text definition is also a meeting event type. In this case when the minutes Insert Text tool is used to insert this text definition, a meeting event will automatically be generated by the system. Once meeting events are generated, this checkbox cannot

title: Tools Group
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Tools Group The Tools Group is always visible on the Infocouncil ribbon tab. The Tools Group provides all users additional functions that may be used during the process of creating a report or business paper. Tool Description Add : Allows the user to add a comment for the selected text. Delete : Allows the user to delete all comments for the current paragraph. Track Changes : Turns on Track Changes for a report with protected sections (this is also available from the Word toolbar) Accept All Changes : Allows the user to Accept All Changes for a report with protected sections (this is also available from the Word toolbar) Bullets And Numbering : Presents the Bullets and Numbering dialog box for a report with protected sections (this is also available from the Word toolbar) Borders And Shading : Presents the Borders and Shading dialog box for a report with protected sections (this is also available from the Word toolbar) Copy: Performs a clean copy of selected data for pasting into a report. Paste : Pastes the copied text in its original format. Paste Plain Text : Pastes the copied text as plain text. Insert Text: Displays the Insert Text form listing predefined texts to insert into the active document. See also Text Definitions Maintenance to maintain predefined texts. Show/Hide Hidden Text: Toggles hidden text on and off (Note: a system parameter controls the visibility of this button) Update Fields: Updates all the fields in the document including those in headers and footers Spelling and Grammar: Performs a comprehensive spell check of the report, including all protected sections and form fields. Convert Numbers To Text: Converts any list numbering in a selection to text. Next Field: Moves cursor to the next macro, form or comment field in the report (same as F11). Delete Unused Fields: Deletes any unused macro-button, comment or form fields in the document. Delete Hidden Text : Deletes hidden text from the document. Insert Landscape Section: Will insert a landscape section into a report. This is available in version 7.1.2 and above and for those councils who have been converted to a non-section based report template.

title: Transfer Reports
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Functions > Transfer Reports Clicking on the Functions ribbon menu and choosing Transfer Reports opens the Transfer Reports window. This form provides the means for an administrator to move reports from one meeting to another. Note: The agenda sections of the destination meeting must match the source meeting. This means that transferring of reports from one meeting type to another (e.g. from a council meeting to a committee meeting) may not be possible. If the two meetings have different agenda sections then you will need to open each report and edit the Report Data to move the reports. Source Meeting Choose Name and Date of source meeting. Destination Meeting Choose Name and Date of destination meeting. List Item No Item number of report to be transferred. Subject Name/Title of Report to be transferred. Include All Click to check and thus include all reports for the meeting. Exclude All Click to un-check and thus exclude all reports for the meeting. OK Click to initiate the process of transferring reports from the source meeting to the destination meeting. Progress of the transfer is indicated in the Status Bar at the bottom of window. Infocouncil opens a new session of Word to run the transfer function. You may see reports being opened and closed during the transfer. The speed of the transfer is dependent on a number of factors, including the EDRMS your Council uses and the performance of your servers and network. A message is displayed when the transfer has completed. Close Click to close the window. See Also Shifting Reports Between Meetings - Concept

title: Unprotect
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Report Group > Other > Unprotect When an administrator has a report open, clicking on the Other ribbon menu and choosing Unprotect opens this form. If Infocouncil support directs you to unprotect a report, they will provide you with a password in order to do so. The most common scenario for needing to do this is if you have previously deferred or referred a report but you do not want the statement saying so to appear in your report. Warning: You need to be extremely careful when editing a protected section of a report. Before beginning you should always turn on your paragraph marker and show bookmarks (File > Options > Advanced > Show bookmarks). Showing these helps you avoid accidentally deleting something you should keep. OK If correct password is entered, unprotects the the protected sections of the report. Cancel Closes the window and returns you to the report. The report will automatically be re-protected on the next save.

title: Un-Publish
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Publish > Un-Publish Clicking on the Publish ribbon menu, and choosing Un-Publish, opens the Un-Publish Business Papers window. This form provides an administrator with the functionality to un-publish business paper from the web. These are the papers that were published from a "Web API" type publish destination. Note that you cannot un-publish from other publish destination types. The grid displays a row for each PDF and/or HTML version of a published paper. The first three columns describe the paper and the last column allows you to select paper for un-publishing. Publish Destinations Click to select a publish destination. Selecting a publish destination, will fetch and populate all the publication years from the selected publish destination (website). If there is only one configured publish destination, it will automatically be selected. Search Click on the Search button to search for published business papers based on your selections. The search button becomes visible after you select a value for each of the following fields: · Publish Destinations · Years · Months List Paper Name of the paper. Meeting Date Date and time of the meeting. Type Type of document - whether HTML or PDF. Select Check the box for each paper you wish to un-publish, or check the box in the column header to select or deselect all papers in a column. Progress Log During un-publishing, the progress is displayed in the progress log. The progress log can be expanded or contracted by hovering the mouse over the grey bar below the Hide button, clicking and dragging up or down (the bar is invisible until the drag operation begins). Cancel This button appears during un-publishing and allows you to cancel the un-publishing. The process will stop when it is suitable to do so. Cancelling does not remove any already published papers. Look at the log to determine what work has already been done. When un-publishing is completed or cancelled, Hide, Save As and Copy options appear. When un-publishing is in progress, Hide, Save As and Copy options appear. Hide Click to hide the log. The Hide button will change to "Show" which when clicked will reshow the log. Save As Click to save the un-publishing log to your computer. This could be useful if problems arise and you need help with issue resolution. Copy Click to copy the un-publishing log to the clipboard then paste it into a Word document, email or similar. You can then examine each step of the un-publishing process. Un-Publish Un-Publishes the checked papers from the selected publish destination (website). Close Closes the window. See Also Create HTML Document - Screen Help Create PDF Document - Screen Help Publish Destinations - Screen Help Meeting Publish Destinations - Screen Help Publishing Papers - How To UnPublishing from the Web - How To Publishing to the Web - How to Publish Destinations - Concept

title: Update Action Authorisers
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Generate Actions > Minute Item > Update Action Authorisers To update authorisers on the minute item screen click the Authorisers button for the highlighted action row. The Action Authorisers window displays a list of the authorisers and buttons to facilitate the authorisation process. Authoriser Names of authorisers for the action. Status There are four options: · To Be Notified · Notified · Authorised · Returned Date Date the status was last updated. History Lists the date and changes to status that a selected user has made. Add Authoriser Add an authoriser. Delete Authoriser Delete an authoriser. Close The Close button closes the Report Authorisers form and returns you to the report.

title: Generate Actions
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Generate Actions Show/Hide Hidden Text Clicking the Generate Actions button on the Infocouncil ribbon opens the Generate Actions Form. This form provides the means for an administrator to produce Action Sheets for a meeting, in either printed or electronic format by email or into an EDRMS. The creation of Action Sheets should occur after the Minutes document for the meeting has been completed. Meeting Select a meeting (committee) from the list. Meeting Date Select a meeting date from the last 6 months onward. Older dates can be viewed by selecting "<More dates...>" from the list. Selecting "<Less Dates...>" will hide the older dates again. Minutes Type Select a minutes document. All of the minutes documents created for the meeting are available including both the open and confidential versions. It may be necessary to upload both the open and confidential minutes if a summary of the confidential items are not included in the open minutes: If no minutes document has been created yet, the drop-down will be disabled and display: Unreleased Actions (button) If there are unreleased actions, this label and button will display otherwise it will be hidden. Actions must be released before the action officer can see them in the Actions screen. Depending on your system configuration you may never see this. See Releasing Actions concept topic for more detail. Clicking the button just displays a message "There are actions yet to be released, to appear in the action officers' lists. Review the Released column to identify which minute items they belong to.". Upload Click to upload minute items from the selected minutes document. The minute items list will populate with all items found. When a paper has no uploaded minute items, just the plain upload button will appear. Otherwise the button changes to a menu button with the options as listed below Upload All Items The entire minutes is re-uploaded. All prior minute items are cleared down from the database and regenerated. Upload All Changed Items Only changed minute items are re-uploaded. The minutes document is checked, any changed minute items are cleared down from the database and regenerated. Any new minute items are uploaded, any deleted items are cleared down and not regenerated. All minute items have their order number rechecked and updated as required. Upload Any New Items Only new minute items are uploaded. The minutes document is checked, any new minute items found are uploaded. All minute items have their order number rechecked and updated as required. Upload Selected Items Only the selected minute items are re-uploaded. All selected minute items will be cleared down from the database and regenerated. If one of the items no longer exists in the minutes document it will not be regenerated. All minute items have their order number rechecked and updated as required. Upload Selected Changed Items Only the selected minute items that have

title: User Action
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Main Group > Actions > User Action Show/Hide Hidden Text If Allow User Defined Actions System Parameter within the Actions grouping, is set to true then User Action functionality is available. Clicking the New button on the Actions form opens the User Action form. This form provides the means to enter a new user action. Title The title for the user action. Type The type of action, whether "For Action" and actionable or for "Information Only". Officer The officer to whom the action sheet is assigned. This will default to the current user and may only be changed via the Reallocate to Another Officer function in the Actions form. Initial Target The original target date for the action sheet to be completed by. Does not mean much for an "Information Only" action sheet. Revised Target This is not displayed when first creating the user action. This will be the same as the Initial Target date unless the action has had its target date revised via the Actions form. Does not mean much for an "Information Only" action sheet. Completed This is not displayed when first creating the user action. If the action sheet was completed via the Actions form, this will show the date it was completed. Action Notes List The date and time that the note was first created. Not editable. Action Note List Columns Date Time The date and time that the note was first created. Type The type of the note. Where a user creates the notes the type will be "User". Notes are also generated by the system when an action has its target date changed, is reallocated to another officer or is completed. These system generated notes have their own type. Officer The officer who created the note. Note The note text. Add Opens the Action Sheet Note form to add a new note. Edit Opens the Action Sheet Note form to edit the currently highlighted action note. Delete Deletes the currently highlighted action sheet note following a confirmation message. Save Click to accept any change and exit the screens. Undo Click to undo any changes. Close Click to cancel any changes and exit the screen.

title: Yearly Meeting Schedule
text: Navigation: Screen Help > Infocouncil Ribbon > Administration Group > Reporting > Yearly Meeting Schedule Clicking on the Reporting ribbon menu and selecting Yearly Meeting Schedule opens the Yearly Meeting Schedule form. The report produced lists all meetings for date range, by meeting type: From Date Select a date to filter for meetings from a certain date. To Date Select a date to filter for meetings before a certain date. Layout By default there may only be one choice, but your organisation may have created additional layouts. OK Click to produce Yearly Meeting Schedule report. It appears as a "throw-away" document in Word format. Cancel Click to exit the window without creating a report. See Also Modifying and Creating Report Templates

title: Why Is The Infocouncil Ribbon Not Visible
text: Navigation: Troubleshooting > Why Is The Infocouncil Ribbon Not Visible Infocouncil not installed on the machine First and foremost Infocouncil must be installed on the workstation. Verifying this can easily be done by going into Word's options (click File, then Options) and selecting Add-Ins. At the bottom of this screen select "COM Addins" in the Manage combo box and click Go. In the list that is presented check that "Infocouncil .ExpressAddin" is present. If it is then Infocouncil is installed. Infocouncil Add-In is disabled The most common cause for the ribbon not being visible is that the Infocouncil Add-In has been disabled. This can occur if an unexpected error occurs in the Add-In. When such an event occurs, next time Word starts up it will ask the user whether they wish to disable the Add-In. It is very easy to press yes to this, resulting in the Infocouncil ribbon no longer appearing. If this has occurred it can easily be checked by going into the same screen to manage the COM Add-Ins, as described above. If the "Infocouncil .ExpressAddin" is unchecked, then check it to enable the Add-In once more. The Infocouncil ribbon should now be visible.
